traits.description: The traits that describe characteristics, features of an entity. See specific usage.

transaction_uid.caption: Transaction UID
transaction_uid.description: The unique identifier of the transaction.

transformation_info_list.caption: Transformation Info
transformation_info_list.description: An array of transformation info that describes the mappings or transforms applied to the data.

transmit_time.caption: Transmission Time
transmit_time.description: The event transmission time from one device to another.  See specific usage.

tree_uid.caption: Tree UID
tree_uid.description: The tree id is a unique SMB identifier which represents an open connection to a share.

ttl.caption: TTL
ttl.description: The time interval that the resource record may be cached. Zero value means that the resource record can only be used for the transaction in progress, and should not be cached.

tunnel_interface.caption: Interface
tunnel_interface.description: The information about the tunnel interface. See specific usage.

tunnel_type.caption: Type
tunnel_type.description: The tunnel type. See specific usage.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of an object or value, normalized to the caption of the type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source. See specific usage.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized type identifier of an object. See specific usage.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

type_name.caption: Type Name
type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.caption: Type ID
type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

types.caption: Types
types.description: The type/s of an entity. See specific usage.

udid.caption: Unique Device Identifier
udid.description: The Apple assigned Unique Device Identifier (UDID). For iOS, iPadOS, tvOS, watchOS and visionOS devices, this is the UDID. For macOS devices, it is the Provisioning UDID. For example: 00008020-008D4548007B4F26

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier. See specific usage.

uid_alt.caption: Alternate ID
uid_alt.description: The alternate unique identifier. See specific usage.

unique_malware_count.caption: Unique Malware Count
unique_malware_count.description: The number of unique malware detected during a scan. See specific usage.

unmanned_aerial_system.caption: Unmanned Aerial System
unmanned_aerial_system.description: The Unmanned Aerial System object describes the characteristics, Position Location Information (PLI), and other metadata of Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) and other unmanned and drone systems used in Remote ID. Remote ID is defined in the Standard Specification for Remote ID and Tracking (ASTM Designation: F3411-22a) ASTM F3411-22a.

unmanned_system_operating_area.caption: UAS Operating Area
unmanned_system_operating_area.description: The UAS Operating Area object describes details about a precise area of operations for a UAS flight or mission.

unmanned_system_operator.caption: Unmanned Systems Operator
unmanned_system_operator.description: The human or machine operator of an Unmanned System.

unmapped.caption: Unmapped Data
unmapped.description: The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of those attributes are specific to the event source.

untruncated_size.caption: Untruncated Size
untruncated_size.description: The size in bytes of an attribute before truncation. See specific usage.

unused_privileges_count.caption: Unused Privileges Count
unused_privileges_count.description: The number of unused Privileges. See specific usage.

unused_services_count.caption: Unused Services Count
unused_services_count.description: The number of unused services. See specific usage.

uploaded_time.caption: Uploaded Time
uploaded_time.description: The timestamp at which an entity was uploaded. See specific usage.

uri.caption: URI
uri.description: A Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) is a string of characters that identifies a resource on the Internet. URIs can identify physical resources, like webpages and documents. See specific usage.

url.caption: URL
url.description: The URL object that pertains to the event or object. See specific usage.

url_string.caption: URL String
url_string.description: The URL string. See RFC 1738. For example: http://www.example.com/download/trouble.exe.

urls.caption: URLs
urls.description: The URLs that pertain to the event or object.

user.caption: User
user.description: The user that pertains to the event or object.

user_agent.caption: HTTP User-Agent
user_agent.description: The request header that identifies the operating system and web browser.

user_result.caption: User Result
user_result.description: The result of the user account change. It should contain the new values of the changed attributes.

users.caption: Users
users.description: The users that pertain to the event or object.

uuid.caption: UUID
uuid.description: The universally unique identifier. See specific usage.

value.caption: Value
value.description: The value associated to an attribute. See specific usage.

values.caption: Values
values.description: An array of values associated to an attribute. See specific usage.

vector_string.caption: Vector String
vector_string.description: The CVSS vector string is a text representation of a set of CVSS metrics. It is commonly used to record or transfer CVSS metric information in a concise form. For example: 3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:H.

vendor_attributes.caption: Vendor Attributes
vendor_attributes.description:
The Vendor Attributes object can be used to represent values of attributes populated by the Vendor/Finding Provider. It can help distinguish between the vendor-provided values and consumer-updated values, of key attributes like severity_id.
The original finding producer should not populate this object. It should be populated by consuming systems that support data mutability.

vendor_id_list.caption: Vendor ID List
vendor_id_list.description: The list of vendor IDs. See specific usage.

vendor_name.caption: Vendor Name
vendor_name.description: The name of the vendor. See specific usage.

verdict.caption: Verdict
verdict.description: The verdict assigned to an Incident finding.

verdict_id.caption: Verdict ID
verdict_id.description: The normalized verdict of an Incident.

1.caption: False Positive
1.description: The incident is a false positive.

2.caption: True Positive
2.description: The incident is a true positive.

3.caption: Disregard
3.description: The incident can be disregarded as it is unimportant, an error or accident.

4.caption: Suspicious
4.description: The incident is suspicious.

5.caption: Benign
5.description: The incident is benign.

6.caption: Test
6.description: The incident is a test.

7.caption: Insufficient Data
7.description: The incident has insufficient data to make a verdict.

8.caption: Security Risk
8.description: The incident is a security risk.

9.caption: Managed Externally
9.description: The incident remediation or required actions are managed externally.

10.caption: Duplicate
10.description: The incident is a duplicate.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version that pertains to the event or object. See specific usage.

vertical_speed.caption: Vertical Speed
vertical_speed.description: Vertical speed upward relative to the WGS-84 datum, measured in meters per second. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: 63 m/s.

volume.caption: Volume
volume.description: The volume on the storage device where the file is located. See specific usage.

vpc_uid.caption: VPC UID
vpc_uid.description: The unique identifier of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

vram_mode.caption: VRAM Mode
vram_mode.description: The video memory attachment mode, indicating how the VRAM hardware is integrated with the system (e.g., shared or dedicated), normalized to the caption of the vram_mode_id value. For 'Other', the exact attachment mode is defined by the event source.

vram_mode_id.caption: VRAM Mode ID
vram_mode_id.description: The normalized identifier of the video memory attachment mode.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The VRAM mode is unknown or not reported.

1.caption: Shared
1.description: Video memory is allocated from system memory.

2.caption: Dedicated
2.description: Video memory is physically separate from system memory.

99.caption: Other
99.description: A VRAM mode not covered by the defined values; the exact value is reported by the event source.

vram_size.caption: VRAM Size
vram_size.description: The total amount of installed video RAM.

vulnerabilities.caption: Vulnerabilities
vulnerabilities.description: This object describes vulnerabilities reported in a security finding.

vulnerability.caption: Vulnerability
vulnerability.description: The vulnerability object describes details related to the observed vulnerability

web_resources.caption: Web Resources
web_resources.description: Describes details about web resources that were affected by an activity/event.

web_resources_result.caption: Web Resources Result
web_resources_result.description: The results of the activity on web resources. It should contain the new values of the changed attributes of the web resources.

whois.caption: WHOIS
whois.description: The resources of a WHOIS record for a given domain. This can include domain names, IP address blocks, autonomous system information, and/or contact and registration information for a domain.

working_directory.caption: Working Directory
working_directory.description: The working directory of a process.

write_count.caption: Write Count
write_count.description: The write count. See specific usage.

x_forwarded_for.caption: X-Forwarded-For
x_forwarded_for.description: The X-Forwarded-For header identifying the originating IP address(es) of a client connecting to a web server through an HTTP proxy or a load balancer.

x_originating_ip.caption: X-Originating-IP
x_originating_ip.description: The X-Originating-IP header identifying the emails originating IP address(es).

xattributes.caption: Extended Attributes
xattributes.description:
An unordered collection of zero or more name/value pairs where each pair represents a file or folder extended attribute.

For example: Windows alternate data stream attributes (ADS stream name, ADS size, etc.), user-defined or application-defined attributes, ACL, owner, primary group, etc. Examples from DCS:

  • ads_name
  • ads_size
  • dacl
  • owner
  • primary_group
  • link_name - name of the link associated to the file.
  • hard_link_count - the number of links that are associated to the file.

zone.caption: Network Zone
zone.description: The network zone or LAN segment. See specific usage.

win/hosted_services.caption: Hosted Services
win/hosted_services.description: The Windows services that this process is hosting.

win/hosting_process.caption: Hosting Process
win/hosting_process.description: The process that is hosting this service.

win/load_order_group.caption: Load Order Group
win/load_order_group.description: The name of the load ordering group of which this service is a member.

win/prev_reg_key.caption: Previous Registry Key
win/prev_reg_key.description: The registry key before the mutation

win/prev_reg_value.caption: Previous Registry Value
win/prev_reg_value.description: The registry value before the mutation

win/reg_binary_data.caption: Registry Binary Data
win/reg_binary_data.description: The data of the registry value when type_id is REG_BINARY or REG_NONE.

win/reg_integer_data.caption: Registry Integer Data
win/reg_integer_data.description: The data of the registry value when type_id is REG_DWORD, REG_DWORD_BIG_ENDIAN, or REG_QWORD.

win/reg_string_data.caption: Registry String Data
win/reg_string_data.description: The data of the registry value when type_id is REG_SZ, REG_EXPAND_SZ, or REG_LINK.

win/reg_string_list_data.caption: Registry String List Data
win/reg_string_list_data.description: The data of the registry value when type_id is REG_MULTI_SZ.

win/service_category.caption: Service Category
win/service_category.description: The service category, normalized to the caption of the service_category_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

win/service_category_id.caption: Service Category ID
win/service_category_id.description: The normalized identifier of the service category.

1.caption: Kernel Mode
1.description: A kernel mode driver.

2.caption: User Mode
2.description: A user mode service.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The service category is not mapped. See the service_category attribute, which contains an event source specific value.

win/service_dependencies.caption: Service Dependencies
win/service_dependencies.description: The names of other services upon which this service has a dependency.

win/service_dll_file.caption: Service DLL
win/service_dll_file.description: For a shared user mode service (service_type_id is 4) this is the DLL that gets loaded by the generic service host process (e.g. svchost.exe) to implement the service.

win/service_error_control.caption: Service Error Control
win/service_error_control.description: The service error control, normalized to the caption of the service_error_control_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

win/service_error_control_id.caption: Service Error Control ID
win/service_error_control_id.description: The normalized identifier of the service error control.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The service error control is unknown.

1.caption: Ignore
1.description: The startup program ignores the error and continues the startup operation.

2.caption: Normal
2.description: The startup program logs the error in the event log but continues the startup operation.

3.caption: Severe
3.description: The startup program logs the error in the event log. If the last-known-good configuration is being started, the startup operation continues. Otherwise, the system is restarted with the last-known-good configuration.

4.caption: Critical
4.description: The startup program logs the error in the event log, if possible. If the last-known-good configuration is being started, the startup operation fails. Otherwise, the system is restarted with the last-known good configuration.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The service error control is not mapped. See the service_error_control attribute, which contains an event source specific value.

win/service_file.caption: Service File
win/service_file.description:
For a user mode service (service_type_id 3 or 4) this is the executable program that the SCM launches as the service process.
For a kernel mode driver (service_type_id 1 or 2) this is the driver file loaded into the kernel at the request of the SCM.

win/service_start_name.caption: Service Start Name
win/service_start_name.description: For a user mode service, this attribute represents the name of the account under which the service is run. For a kernel mode driver, this attribute represents the object name used to load the driver.

win/service_start_type.caption: Service Start Type
win/service_start_type.description: The service start type, normalized to the caption of the service_start_type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

win/service_start_type_id.caption: Service Start Type ID
win/service_start_type_id.description: The normalized identifier of the service start type.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The service start type is unknown.

1.caption: Boot
1.description: A kernel mode driver loaded at boot.

2.caption: System
2.description: A kernel mode driver loaded during system startup.

3.caption: Auto
3.description: A user mode service started automatically during system startup.

4.caption: Demand
4.description: A user mode service started on demand when a process calls StartService.

5.caption: Disabled
5.description: A driver or service that cannot be started.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The service start type is not mapped. See the service_start_type attribute, which contains an event source specific value.

win/service_type.caption: Service Type
win/service_type.description: The service type, normalized to the caption of the service_type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

win/service_type_id.caption: Service Type ID
win/service_type_id.description: The normalized identifier of the service type.

1.caption: Kernel Driver
1.description: A kernel mode driver.

2.caption: File System Driver
2.description: A kernel mode file system minifilter.

3.caption: Own Process
3.description: A user mode service that runs in its own process.

4.caption: Share Process
4.description: A user mode service that shares a process with other services.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The service type is not mapped. See the service_type attribute, which contains an event source specific value.

win/win_resource.caption: Windows Resource
win/win_resource.description: The Windows resource object that was accessed, such as a mutant or timer.

accessed_time_dt.caption: Accessed Time
accessed_time_dt.description: The time when the file was last accessed.

boot_time_dt.caption: Boot Time
boot_time_dt.description: The time when the system was booted.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time when the object was created. See specific usage.

deleted_time_dt.caption: Deleted Time
deleted_time_dt.description: The timestamp when the user was deleted. In Active Directory (AD), when a user is deleted they are moved to a temporary container and then removed after 30 days. So, this field can be populated even after a user is deleted for the next 30 days.

end_time_dt.caption: End Time
end_time_dt.description: The end time of a time period. See specific usage.

expiration_time_dt.caption: Expiration Time
expiration_time_dt.description: The expiration time. See specific usage.

exploit_last_seen_time_dt.caption: Exploit Last Seen Time
exploit_last_seen_time_dt.description: The time when the exploit was most recently observed.

first_seen_time_dt.caption: First Seen
first_seen_time_dt.description: The initial detection time of the activity or object. See specific usage

hire_time_dt.caption: Hire Time
hire_time_dt.description: The timestamp when the user was or will be hired by the organization.

last_authentication_time_dt.caption: Last Authentication Time
last_authentication_time_dt.description: The timestamp when this identity last successfully authenticated to any system or service. See specific usage.

last_login_time_dt.caption: Last Login
last_login_time_dt.description: The last time when the user logged in.

last_run_time_dt.caption: Last Run
last_run_time_dt.description: The last run time of application or service. See specific usage.

last_seen_time_dt.caption: Last Seen
last_seen_time_dt.description: The most recent detection time of the activity or object. See specific usage.

last_used_time_dt.caption: Last Used Time
last_used_time_dt.description: The most recent usage time of an entity. See specific usage.

leave_time_dt.caption: Leave Time
leave_time_dt.description: The timestamp when the user left or will be leaving the organization.

logged_time_dt.caption: Logged Time
logged_time_dt.description:
The time when the logging system collected and logged the event.

This attribute is distinct from the event time in that event time typically contain the time extracted from the original event. Most of the time, these two times will be different.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The time when the object was last modified. See specific usage.

next_run_time_dt.caption: Next Run
next_run_time_dt.description: The next run time. See specific usage.

password_last_used_time_dt.caption: Password Last Used Time
password_last_used_time_dt.description: The time when a user's password was last used. See specific usage.

processed_time_dt.caption: Processed Time
processed_time_dt.description: The event processed time, such as an ETL operation.

query_time_dt.caption: Query Time
query_time_dt.description: The Domain Name System (DNS) query time.

response_time_dt.caption: Response Time
response_time_dt.description: The Domain Name System (DNS) response time.

start_time_dt.caption: Start Time
start_time_dt.description: The start time of a time period. See specific usage.

terminated_time_dt.caption: Terminated Time
terminated_time_dt.description: The time when the entity was terminated. See specific usage.

time_dt.caption: Event Time
time_dt.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

transmit_time_dt.caption: Transmission Time
transmit_time_dt.description: The event transmission time from one device to another.  See specific usage.

uploaded_time_dt.caption: Uploaded Time
uploaded_time_dt.description: The timestamp at which an entity was uploaded. See specific usage.

types.caption: Data Types
types.description: The data types available in OCSF. Each data type specifies constraints in the form regular expressions, max lengths or value limits. Implementers of OCSF should ensure they abide to these constraints.

bytestring_t.caption: Byte String
bytestring_t.description: Base 64 encoded immutable byte sequence. Traditional Base 64 is preferred but publishers may use URL-safe Base 64 when known to be acceptable to consumers. These encodings are described in RFC 4648.

datetime_t.caption: Datetime
datetime_t.description:
The Internet Date/Time format as defined in RFC-3339. For example:
2024-09-10T23:20:50.520Z,
2024-09-10 23:20:50.520789Z.

file_hash_t.caption: Hash
file_hash_t.description:
Fingerprint. A value, in any format, that maps an arbitrarily large data item to a much shorter string that uniquely identifies the original data. Examples include cryptographic hashing of a file, code signing, and Network Protocol Fingerprinting (NPF).

Note about name. The type name file_hast_t and the caption "Hash" are used for legacy reasons. This type has been generalized from a file hash to a general fingerprint. The existing type name and caption were retained for backwards compatibility.

file_path_t.caption: File Path
file_path_t.description:
The full path to the file. For example: For example:
c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe.

hostname_t.caption: Hostname
hostname_t.description:
Unique name assigned to a device connected to a computer network. It may be a fully qualified domain name (FQDN). For example:
r2-d2.example.com.,
mx.example.com

ip_t.caption: IP Address
ip_t.description:
Internet Protocol address (IP address), in either IPv4 or IPv6 format. For example:
192.168.200.24,
2001:0db8:85a3:0000:0000:8a2e:0370:7334.

json_t.caption: JSON
json_t.description: Embedded JSON value. A value can be a string, or a number, or true or false or null, or an object or an array. These structures can be nested. See www.json.org.

resource_uid_t.caption: Resource UID
resource_uid_t.description: Resource unique identifier. For example, S3 Bucket name or EC2 Instance ID.

subnet_t.caption: Subnet
subnet_t.description:
The subnet represented in a CIDR notation, using the format network_address/prefix_length. The network_address can be in either IPv4 or IPv6 format. The prefix length indicates the number of bits used for the network portion, and the remaining bits are available for host addresses within that subnet. For example:
192.168.1.0/24,
2001:0db8:85a3:0000::/64

timestamp_t.caption: Timestamp
timestamp_t.description:
The timestamp format is the number of milliseconds since the Epoch 01/01/1970 00:00:00 UTC. For example:
1618524549901.

ssh_activity.caption: SSH Activity
ssh_activity.description: SSH Activity events report remote client connections to a server using the Secure Shell (SSH) Protocol.
ssh_activity.name: ssh_activity

action_id.caption: Action ID
action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The action was unknown. The disposition_id attribute may still be set to a non-unknown value, for example 'Custom Action', 'Challenge'.

1.caption: Allowed
1.description: The activity was allowed. The disposition_id attribute should be set to a value that conforms to this action, for example 'Allowed', 'Approved', 'Delayed', 'No Action', 'Count' etc.

2.caption: Denied
2.description: The attempted activity was denied. The disposition_id attribute should be set to a value that conforms to this action, for example 'Blocked', 'Rejected', 'Quarantined', 'Isolated', 'Dropped', 'Access Revoked, etc.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The action is not mapped. See the action attribute which contains a data source specific value.

3.caption: Observed
3.description: The activity was observed, but neither explicitly allowed nor denied. This is common with IDS and EDR controls that report additional information on observed behavior such as TTPs. The disposition_id attribute should be set to a value that conforms to this action, for example 'Logged', 'Alert', 'Detected', 'Count', etc.

4.caption: Modified
4.description: The activity was modified, adjusted, or corrected. The disposition_id attribute should be set appropriately, for example 'Restored', 'Corrected', 'Delayed', 'Captcha', 'Tagged'.

attacks.caption: MITRE ATT&CK® and ATLAS™ Details
attacks.description: An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques & sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics, techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.caption: Authorization Information
authorizations.description: Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any associated policies related to the activity/event.

confidence.caption: Confidence
confidence.description: The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.caption: Confidence ID
confidence_id.description: The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The confidence is not mapped to the defined enum values. See the confidence attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

confidence_score.caption: Confidence Score
confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

disposition.caption: Disposition
disposition.description: The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.caption: Disposition ID
disposition_id.description: Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

1.caption: Allowed
1.description: Granted access or allowed the action to the protected resource.

2.caption: Blocked
2.description: Denied access or blocked the action to the protected resource.

3.caption: Quarantined
3.description: A suspicious file or other content was moved to a benign location.

4.caption: Isolated
4.description: A session was isolated on the network or within a browser.

5.caption: Deleted
5.description: A file or other content was deleted.

6.caption: Dropped
6.description: The request was detected as a threat and resulted in the connection being dropped.

7.caption: Custom Action
7.description: A custom action was executed such as running of a command script. Use the message attribute of the base class for details.

8.caption: Approved
8.description: A request or submission was approved. For example, when a form was properly filled out and submitted. This is distinct from 1 'Allowed'.

9.caption: Restored
9.description: A quarantined file or other content was restored to its original location.

10.caption: Exonerated
10.description: A suspicious or risky entity was deemed to no longer be suspicious (re-scored).

11.caption: Corrected
11.description: A corrupt file or configuration was corrected.

12.caption: Partially Corrected
12.description: A corrupt file or configuration was partially corrected.

13.caption: Uncorrected
13.description: A corrupt file or configuration was not corrected.

14.caption: Delayed
14.description: An operation was delayed, for example if a restart was required to finish the operation.

15.caption: Detected
15.description: Suspicious activity or a policy violation was detected without further action.

16.caption: No Action
16.description: The outcome of an operation had no action taken.

17.caption: Logged
17.description: The operation or action was logged without further action.

18.caption: Tagged
18.description: A file or other entity was marked with extended attributes.

19.caption: Alert
19.description: The request or activity was detected as a threat and resulted in a notification but request was not blocked.

20.caption: Count
20.description: Counted the request or activity but did not determine whether to allow it or block it.

21.caption: Reset
21.description: The request was detected as a threat and resulted in the connection being reset.

22.caption: Captcha
22.description: Required the end user to solve a CAPTCHA puzzle to prove that a human being is sending the request.

23.caption: Challenge
23.description: Ran a silent challenge that required the client session to verify that it's a browser, and not a bot.

24.caption: Access Revoked
24.description: The requestor's access has been revoked due to security policy enforcements. Note: use the Host profile if the User or Actor requestor is not present in the event class.

25.caption: Rejected
25.description: A request or submission was rejected. For example, when a form was improperly filled out and submitted. This is distinct from 2 'Blocked'.

26.caption: Unauthorized
26.description: An attempt to access a resource was denied due to an authorization check that failed. This is a more specific disposition than 2 'Blocked' and can be complemented with the authorizations attribute for more detail.

27.caption: Error
27.description: An error occurred during the processing of the activity or request. Use the message attribute of the base class for details.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The disposition is not mapped. See the disposition attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

firewall_rule.caption: Firewall Rule
firewall_rule.description: The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if applicable.

is_alert.caption: Alert
is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.caption: Malware
malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.caption: Malware Scan Info
malware_scan_info.description: Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target system.

policy.caption: Policy
policy.description: The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control policy.

risk_details.caption: Risk Details
risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.caption: Risk Level
risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The risk level is not mapped. See the risk_level attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

risk_score.caption: Risk Score
risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

osint.caption: OSINT
osint.description: The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation, registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

actor.caption: Actor
actor.description: The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign but may be part of a campaign.

device.caption: Device
device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

api.caption: API Details
api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

cloud.caption: Cloud
cloud.description: Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was created.

activity_id.caption: Activity ID
activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The event activity is not mapped. See the activity_name attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Open
1.description: A new network connection was opened.

2.caption: Close
2.description: The network connection was closed.

3.caption: Reset
3.description: The network connection was abnormally terminated or closed by a middle device like firewalls.

4.caption: Fail
4.description: The network connection failed. For example a connection timeout or no route to host.

5.caption: Refuse
5.description: The network connection was refused. For example an attempt to connect to a server port which is not open.

6.caption: Traffic
6.description: Network traffic report.

7.caption: Listen
7.description: A network endpoint began listening for new network connections.

activity_name.caption: Activity
activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

category_name.caption: Category
category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value: Network Activity.

category_uid.caption: Category ID
category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

4.caption: Network Activity
4.description: Network Activity events.

class_name.caption: Class
class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value: SSH Activity.

class_uid.caption: Class ID
class_uid.description: The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in an event.

4007.caption: SSH Activity
4007.description: SSH Activity events report remote client connections to a server using the Secure Shell (SSH) Protocol.

count.caption: Count
count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

duration.caption: Duration Milliseconds
duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.caption: End Time
end_time.description: The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in the aggregate event.

enrichments.caption: Enrichments
enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

message.caption: Message
message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.caption: Metadata
metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.caption: Observables
observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

raw_data.caption: Raw Data
raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.caption: Raw Data Hash
raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.caption: Raw Data Size
raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

severity.caption: Severity
severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.caption: Severity ID
severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

1.caption: Informational
1.description: Informational message. No action required.

2.caption: Low
2.description: The user decides if action is needed.

3.caption: Medium
3.description: Action is required but the situation is not serious at this time.

4.caption: High
4.description: Action is required immediately.

5.caption: Critical
5.description: Action is required immediately and the scope is broad.

6.caption: Fatal
6.description: An error occurred but it is too late to take remedial action.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The event/finding severity is not mapped. See the severity attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

start_time.caption: Start Time
start_time.description: The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included in the aggregate event.

status.caption: Status
status.description: The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.caption: Status Code
status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.caption: Status Detail
status_detail.description: The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding outcome.

status_id.caption: Status ID
status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The status is not mapped. See the status attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

time.caption: Event Time
time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.caption: Timezone Offset
timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.caption: Type Name
type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.caption: Type ID
type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

400701.caption: SSH Activity: Open
400701.description: A new network connection was opened.

400703.caption: SSH Activity: Reset
400703.description: The network connection was abnormally terminated or closed by a middle device like firewalls.

400704.caption: SSH Activity: Fail
400704.description: The network connection failed. For example a connection timeout or no route to host.

400705.caption: SSH Activity: Refuse
400705.description: The network connection was refused. For example an attempt to connect to a server port which is not open.

400707.caption: SSH Activity: Listen
400707.description: A network endpoint began listening for new network connections.

unmapped.caption: Unmapped Data
unmapped.description: The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of those attributes are specific to the event source.

load_balancer.caption: Load Balancer
load_balancer.description: The Load Balancer object contains information related to the device that is distributing incoming traffic to specified destinations.

proxy_connection_info.caption: Proxy Connection Info
proxy_connection_info.description: The connection information from the proxy server to the remote server.

proxy_endpoint.caption: Proxy Endpoint
proxy_endpoint.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

proxy_http_request.caption: Proxy HTTP Request
proxy_http_request.description: The HTTP Request from the proxy server to the remote server.

proxy_http_response.caption: Proxy HTTP Response
proxy_http_response.description: The HTTP Response from the remote server to the proxy server.

proxy_tls.caption: Proxy TLS
proxy_tls.description: The TLS protocol negotiated between the proxy server and the remote server.

proxy_traffic.caption: Proxy Traffic
proxy_traffic.description: The network traffic refers to the amount of data moving across a network, from proxy to remote server at a given point of time.

app_name.caption: Application Name
app_name.description: The network application name identified by tools such as NBAR or App ID (e.g., youtube, facebook, webex). This represents a specific network application that uses standard protocols (such as https or quic) to deliver its service.

app_protocol_name.caption: Application Protocol Name
app_protocol_name.description:
The application-layer (Layer 7) protocol name identified by deep packet inspection or packet parsing (e.g., https, quic, ssh, dns), expressed as an IANA-registered service name from the IANA Service Name and Transport Protocol Port Number Registry.

Note: Port numbers alone are not always a reliable indicator of the actual application protocol in use.

references.description: IANA Service Name and Transport Protocol Port Number Registry

cumulative_traffic.caption: Cumulative Traffic
cumulative_traffic.description: The cumulative (running total) network traffic aggregated from the start of a flow or session. Use when reporting: (1) total accumulated bytes/packets since flow initiation, (2) combined aggregation models where both incremental deltas and running totals are reported together (populate both traffic for the delta and this attribute for the cumulative total), or (3) final summary metrics when a long-lived connection closes. This represents the sum of all activity from flow start to the current observation, not a delta or point-in-time value.

dst_endpoint.caption: Destination Endpoint
dst_endpoint.description: The responder (server) in a network connection.

ja4_fingerprint_list.caption: JA4+ Fingerprints
ja4_fingerprint_list.description: A list of the JA4+ network fingerprints.

network_observation_point.caption: Network Observation Point
network_observation_point.description: The network endpoint that observes or inspects network traffic as a third-party system, used when the observer is neither the source nor the destination of the communication (when observation_point_id = 3). Examples include network taps, span ports, inline security devices, or packet capture systems that monitor traffic between other endpoints.

observation_point.caption: Observation Point
observation_point.description: Indicates whether the source network endpoint, destination network endpoint, or neither served as the observation point for the activity. The value is normalized to the caption of the observation_point_id.

observation_point_id.caption: Observation Point ID
observation_point_id.description: The normalized identifier of the observation point. The observation point identifier indicates whether the source network endpoint, destination network endpoint, or neither served as the observation point for the activity.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The observation point is not mapped. See the observation_point attribute for a data source specific value.

1.caption: Source
1.description: The source network endpoint is the observation point.

2.caption: Destination
2.description: The destination network endpoint is the observation point.

3.caption: Neither
3.description: Neither the source nor destination network endpoint is the observation point. Refer to the network_observation_point attribute for details.

4.caption: Both
4.description: Both the source and destination network endpoint are the observation point. This typically occurs in localhost or internal communications where the source and destination are the same endpoint, often resulting in a connection_info.direction of Local.

packet_list.caption: Packets
packet_list.description: The list of packet objects describing captured network packets.

proxy.caption: Proxy
proxy.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.

src_endpoint.caption: Source Endpoint
src_endpoint.description: The initiator (client) of the network connection.

traffic.caption: Traffic
traffic.description: The network traffic for this observation period. Use when reporting: (1) delta values (bytes/packets transferred since the last observation), (2) instantaneous measurements at a specific point in time, or (3) standalone single-event metrics. This attribute represents a point-in-time measurement or incremental change, not a running total. For accumulated totals across multiple observations or the lifetime of a flow, use cumulative_traffic instead.

auth_type.caption: Authentication Type
auth_type.description: The SSH authentication type, normalized to the caption of 'auth_type_id'. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

auth_type_id.caption: Authentication Type ID
auth_type_id.description: The normalized identifier of the SSH authentication type.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The authentication type is not mapped. See the auth_type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Certificate Based
1.description: Authentication using digital certificates.

2.caption: GSSAPI
2.description: GSSAPI for centralized authentication.

3.caption: Host Based
3.description: Authentication based on the client host's identity.

4.caption: Keyboard Interactive
4.description: Multi-step, interactive authentication.

5.caption: Password
5.description: Password Authentication.

6.caption: Public Key
6.description: Paired public key authentication.

file.caption: File
file.description: The file that is the target of the SSH activity.

end_time_dt.caption: End Time
end_time_dt.description: The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in the aggregate event.

start_time_dt.caption: Start Time
start_time_dt.description: The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included in the aggregate event.

time_dt.caption: Event Time
time_dt.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

email_file_activity.caption: Email File Activity
email_file_activity.description: Email File Activity events report files within emails.
email_file_activity.name: email_file_activity

action_id.caption: Action ID
action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The action was unknown. The disposition_id attribute may still be set to a non-unknown value, for example 'Custom Action', 'Challenge'.

1.caption: Allowed
1.description: The activity was allowed. The disposition_id attribute should be set to a value that conforms to this action, for example 'Allowed', 'Approved', 'Delayed', 'No Action', 'Count' etc.

2.caption: Denied
2.description: The attempted activity was denied. The disposition_id attribute should be set to a value that conforms to this action, for example 'Blocked', 'Rejected', 'Quarantined', 'Isolated', 'Dropped', 'Access Revoked, etc.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The action is not mapped. See the action attribute which contains a data source specific value.

3.caption: Observed
3.description: The activity was observed, but neither explicitly allowed nor denied. This is common with IDS and EDR controls that report additional information on observed behavior such as TTPs. The disposition_id attribute should be set to a value that conforms to this action, for example 'Logged', 'Alert', 'Detected', 'Count', etc.

4.caption: Modified
4.description: The activity was modified, adjusted, or corrected. The disposition_id attribute should be set appropriately, for example 'Restored', 'Corrected', 'Delayed', 'Captcha', 'Tagged'.

attacks.caption: MITRE ATT&CK® and ATLAS™ Details
attacks.description: An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques & sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics, techniques & sub-techniques.

authorizations.caption: Authorization Information
authorizations.description: Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any associated policies related to the activity/event.

confidence.caption: Confidence
confidence.description: The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidence_id.caption: Confidence ID
confidence_id.description: The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The confidence is not mapped to the defined enum values. See the confidence attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

confidence_score.caption: Confidence Score
confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.

disposition.caption: Disposition
disposition.description: The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

disposition_id.caption: Disposition ID
disposition_id.description: Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.

1.caption: Allowed
1.description: Granted access or allowed the action to the protected resource.

2.caption: Blocked
2.description: Denied access or blocked the action to the protected resource.

3.caption: Quarantined
3.description: A suspicious file or other content was moved to a benign location.

4.caption: Isolated
4.description: A session was isolated on the network or within a browser.

5.caption: Deleted
5.description: A file or other content was deleted.

6.caption: Dropped
6.description: The request was detected as a threat and resulted in the connection being dropped.

7.caption: Custom Action
7.description: A custom action was executed such as running of a command script. Use the message attribute of the base class for details.

8.caption: Approved
8.description: A request or submission was approved. For example, when a form was properly filled out and submitted. This is distinct from 1 'Allowed'.

9.caption: Restored
9.description: A quarantined file or other content was restored to its original location.

10.caption: Exonerated
10.description: A suspicious or risky entity was deemed to no longer be suspicious (re-scored).

11.caption: Corrected
11.description: A corrupt file or configuration was corrected.

12.caption: Partially Corrected
12.description: A corrupt file or configuration was partially corrected.

13.caption: Uncorrected
13.description: A corrupt file or configuration was not corrected.

14.caption: Delayed
14.description: An operation was delayed, for example if a restart was required to finish the operation.

15.caption: Detected
15.description: Suspicious activity or a policy violation was detected without further action.

16.caption: No Action
16.description: The outcome of an operation had no action taken.

17.caption: Logged
17.description: The operation or action was logged without further action.

18.caption: Tagged
18.description: A file or other entity was marked with extended attributes.

19.caption: Alert
19.description: The request or activity was detected as a threat and resulted in a notification but request was not blocked.

20.caption: Count
20.description: Counted the request or activity but did not determine whether to allow it or block it.

21.caption: Reset
21.description: The request was detected as a threat and resulted in the connection being reset.

22.caption: Captcha
22.description: Required the end user to solve a CAPTCHA puzzle to prove that a human being is sending the request.

23.caption: Challenge
23.description: Ran a silent challenge that required the client session to verify that it's a browser, and not a bot.

24.caption: Access Revoked
24.description: The requestor's access has been revoked due to security policy enforcements. Note: use the Host profile if the User or Actor requestor is not present in the event class.

25.caption: Rejected
25.description: A request or submission was rejected. For example, when a form was improperly filled out and submitted. This is distinct from 2 'Blocked'.

26.caption: Unauthorized
26.description: An attempt to access a resource was denied due to an authorization check that failed. This is a more specific disposition than 2 'Blocked' and can be complemented with the authorizations attribute for more detail.

27.caption: Error
27.description: An error occurred during the processing of the activity or request. Use the message attribute of the base class for details.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The disposition is not mapped. See the disposition attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

firewall_rule.caption: Firewall Rule
firewall_rule.description: The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if applicable.

is_alert.caption: Alert
is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.

malware.caption: Malware
malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.

malware_scan_info.caption: Malware Scan Info
malware_scan_info.description: Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target system.

policy.caption: Policy
policy.description: The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control policy.

risk_details.caption: Risk Details
risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.

risk_level.caption: Risk Level
risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The risk level is not mapped. See the risk_level attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

risk_score.caption: Risk Score
risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

osint.caption: OSINT
osint.description: The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation, registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.

actor.caption: Actor
actor.description: The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign but may be part of a campaign.

device.caption: Device
device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

api.caption: API Details
api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.

cloud.caption: Cloud
cloud.description: Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was created.

activity_id.caption: Activity ID
activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The event activity is not mapped. See the activity_name attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

3.caption: Scan
3.description: Email file being scanned (example: security scanning).

activity_name.caption: Activity
activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.

category_name.caption: Category
category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value: Network Activity.

category_uid.caption: Category ID
category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.

4.caption: Network Activity
4.description: Network Activity events.

class_name.caption: Class
class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value: Email File Activity.

class_uid.caption: Class ID
class_uid.description: The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in an event.

4011.caption: Email File Activity
4011.description: Email File Activity events report files within emails.

count.caption: Count
count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.

duration.caption: Duration Milliseconds
duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.caption: End Time
end_time.description: The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in the aggregate event.

enrichments.caption: Enrichments
enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]

message.caption: Message
message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

metadata.caption: Metadata
metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.

observables.caption: Observables
observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

raw_data.caption: Raw Data
raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.

raw_data_hash.caption: Raw Data Hash
raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.

raw_data_size.caption: Raw Data Size
raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.

severity.caption: Severity
severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.caption: Severity ID
severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

1.caption: Informational
1.description: Informational message. No action required.

2.caption: Low
2.description: The user decides if action is needed.

3.caption: Medium
3.description: Action is required but the situation is not serious at this time.

4.caption: High
4.description: Action is required immediately.

5.caption: Critical
5.description: Action is required immediately and the scope is broad.

6.caption: Fatal
6.description: An error occurred but it is too late to take remedial action.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The event/finding severity is not mapped. See the severity attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

start_time.caption: Start Time
start_time.description: The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included in the aggregate event.

status.caption: Status
status.description: The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.caption: Status Code
status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.

status_detail.caption: Status Detail
status_detail.description: The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding outcome.

status_id.caption: Status ID
status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The status is not mapped. See the status attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

time.caption: Event Time
time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

timezone_offset.caption: Timezone Offset
timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.

type_name.caption: Type Name
type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.

type_uid.caption: Type ID
type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.

401103.caption: Email File Activity: Scan
401103.description: Email file being scanned (example: security scanning).

unmapped.caption: Unmapped Data
unmapped.description: The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of those attributes are specific to the event source.

email_uid.caption: Email UID
email_uid.description: The unique identifier of the email, used to correlate related email alert and activity events.

file.caption: File
file.description: The email file attachment.

end_time_dt.caption: End Time
end_time_dt.description: The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in the aggregate event.

start_time_dt.caption: Start Time
start_time_dt.description: The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included in the aggregate event.

time_dt.caption: Event Time
time_dt.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.

@deprecated.message: Use the Email Activity class with the email.files[] array instead.

email_activity.caption: Email Activity
email_activity.description: Email Activity events report SMTP protocol and email activities including those with embedded URLs and files. See the Email object for details.
