feature.description: The Feature object provides information about the software product feature that generated a specific event. It encompasses details related to the capabilities, components, user interface (UI) design, and performance upgrades associated with the feature.
feature.name: feature

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the feature.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the feature.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the feature.

function_invocation.caption: Function Invocation
function_invocation.description: The Function Invocation object provides details regarding the invocation of a function.
function_invocation.name: function_invocation

error.caption: Error Code
error.description: The error indication returned from the function. This may differ from the return value (e.g. when errno is used).

parameters.caption: Parameters
parameters.description: The parameters passed into a function invocation.

return_value.caption: Return Value
return_value.description: The value returned from a function.

policy.caption: Policy
policy.description:
The Policy object describes the policies that are applicable.

Policy attributes provide traceability to the operational state of the security product at the time that the event was captured, facilitating forensics, troubleshooting, and policy tuning/adjustments.
policy.name: policy

name.caption: Name
name.description: The policy name. For example: AdministratorAccess Policy.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: A unique identifier of the policy instance.

data.caption: Data
data.description: Additional data about the policy such as the underlying JSON policy itself or other details.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The description of the policy.

is_applied.caption: Applied
is_applied.description: A determination if the content of a policy was applied to a target or request, or not.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The policy type. For example: Identity Policy, Resource Policy, Service Control Policy, etc..

version.caption: Version
version.description: The policy version number.

ai_model.caption: AI Model
ai_model.description: The AI Model object describes the characteristics of an AI/ML model. Examples include language models like GPT-4, embedding models like text-embedding-ada-002, and computer vision models like CLIP.
ai_model.name: ai_model

name.caption: Name
name.description: Human-readable model name. For example: gpt-4o, claude-3-sonnet, or text-embedding-ada-002.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the AI model.

ai_provider.caption: AI Provider
ai_provider.description: AI service provider or organization name. For example: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or Internal.

version.caption: Version
version.description: Model version identifier. For example: 2024-05-13, v2.1.0, or beta.

references.description: AI Model Registry and Documentation Standards

trace.caption: Trace
trace.description: The trace object contains information about a distributed trace,  which is crucial for observability. Traces are made up of one or more spans, which are individual units of work in application activity. Traces track the journey of a request as it moves through various services in a system, capturing key details like timing, status, and dependencies at each step. Traces provide insights into system performance, helping to identify latency, bottlenecks, and issues in complex, distributed environments.
trace.name: trace

duration.caption: Duration Milliseconds
duration.description: The total time, in milliseconds, that the trace covers, calculated as the difference between start_time and end_time. This duration helps assess the overall performance of a request as it travels across various services, and is essential for identifying latency and potential bottlenecks within the distributed system. The trace duration may differ from individual span durations due to the propagation and processing times of the trace as it spans multiple components.

end_time.caption: End Time
end_time.description: The end timestamp of the trace, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. Like the start time, this timestamp is normalized across the trace system to ensure consistency, even when events are recorded across distributed services with unsynchronized clocks. Normalized time allows for accurate trace duration calculations and helps observability tools track overall performance across services, regardless of the individual system time settings.

flags.caption: Flags
flags.description: The flags associated with the trace, used to indicate specific properties or behaviors, such as whether the trace is sampled or if it has special handling. Flags help control how traces are processed, logged, and analyzed, providing valuable context for tracing and observability tools in identifying trace characteristics or specific tracking requirements.

service.caption: Service
service.description: Identifies the service or component generating the trace, helping to track and correlate the flow of requests through various parts of a distributed system. This information is essential for understanding the role and performance of specific services within the broader context of system operations and for diagnosing issues across different components.

span.caption: Span
span.description: Represents a single unit of work or operation within a distributed trace. A span typically tracks the execution of a request across a service, capturing important details such as the operation, timestamps, and status. Spans help break down the overall trace into smaller, manageable parts, enabling detailed analysis of the performance and behavior of specific operations within the system. They are crucial for understanding latency, dependencies, and bottlenecks in complex distributed systems.

start_time.caption: Start Time
start_time.description: The start timestamp of the trace, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. Like the end time, this timestamp is normalized across the trace system to ensure consistency, even when events are recorded across distributed services with unsynchronized clocks. Normalized time enables accurate trace duration calculations and helps observability tools track performance across services, regardless of the individual system time settings.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the trace used in distributed systems and microservices architecture to track and correlate requests across various components of an application.

end_time_dt.caption: End Time
end_time_dt.description: The end timestamp of the trace, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. Like the start time, this timestamp is normalized across the trace system to ensure consistency, even when events are recorded across distributed services with unsynchronized clocks. Normalized time allows for accurate trace duration calculations and helps observability tools track overall performance across services, regardless of the individual system time settings.

start_time_dt.caption: Start Time
start_time_dt.description: The start timestamp of the trace, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. Like the end time, this timestamp is normalized across the trace system to ensure consistency, even when events are recorded across distributed services with unsynchronized clocks. Normalized time enables accurate trace duration calculations and helps observability tools track performance across services, regardless of the individual system time settings.

data_security.caption: Data Security
data_security.description: The Data Security object describes the characteristics, techniques and content of a Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Data Loss Detection (DLD), Data Classification, or similar tools' finding, alert, or detection mechanism(s).
data_security.name: data_security

category.caption: Category
category.description: The name of the data classification category that data matched into, e.g. Financial, Personal, Governmental, etc.

category_id.caption: Category ID
category_id.description: The normalized identifier of the data classification category.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The type is not mapped. See the data_type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Personal
1.description: Any Personally Identifiable Information (PII), Electronic Personal Health Information (ePHI), or similarly personal information. E.g., full name, home address, date of birth, etc.

2.caption: Governmental
2.description: Any sensitive government identification number related to a person or other classified material. E.g., Passport numbers, driver license numbers, business identification, taxation identifiers, etc.

3.caption: Financial
3.description: Any financially-related sensitive information or Cardholder Data (CHD). E.g., banking account numbers, credit card numbers, International Banking Account Numbers (IBAN), SWIFT codes, etc.

4.caption: Business
4.description: Any business-specific sensitive data such as intellectual property, trademarks, copyrights, human resource data, Board of Directors meeting minutes, and similar.

5.caption: Military and Law Enforcement
5.description: Any mission-specific sensitive data for military, law enforcement, or other government agencies such as specifically classified data, weapon systems information, or other planning data.

6.caption: Security
6.description: Any sensitive security-related data such as passwords, passkeys, IP addresses, API keys, credentials and similar secrets. E.g., AWS Access Secret Key, SaaS API Keys, user passwords, database credentials, etc.

99.caption: Other
99.description: Any other type of data classification or a multi-variate classification made up of several other classification categories.

classifier_details.caption: Classifier Details
classifier_details.description: Describes details about the classifier used for data classification.

confidentiality.caption: Confidentiality
confidentiality.description: The file content confidentiality, normalized to the confidentiality_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidentiality_id.caption: Confidentiality ID
confidentiality_id.description: The normalized identifier of the file content confidentiality indicator.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The confidentiality is not mapped. See the confidentiality attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

discovery_details.caption: Discovery Details
discovery_details.description: Details about the data discovered by classification job.

policy.caption: Policy
policy.description: Details about the policy that triggered the finding.

src_url.caption: Source URL
src_url.description: The source URL pointing towards the full classification job details.

status.caption: Status
status.description: The resultant status of the classification job normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_details.caption: Status Details
status_details.description: The contextual description of the status, status_id value.

status_id.caption: Status ID
status_id.description: The normalized status identifier of the classification job.

1.caption: Complete
1.description: The classification job completed for the evaluated resource.

2.caption: Partial
2.description: The classification job partially completed for the evaluated resource.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The classification job type id is not mapped.

3.caption: Fail
3.description: The classification job failed for the evaluated resource.

total.caption: Total
total.description: The total count of discovered entities, by the classification job.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the classification job.

data_lifecycle_state.caption: Data Lifecycle State
data_lifecycle_state.description: The name of the stage or state that the data was in. E.g., Data-at-Rest, Data-in-Transit, etc.

data_lifecycle_state_id.caption: Data Lifecycle State ID
data_lifecycle_state_id.description: The stage or state that the data was in when it was assessed or scanned by a data security tool.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The data lifecycle state is unknown.

1.caption: Data at-Rest
1.description: The data was stored on physical or logical media and was not actively moving through the network nor was being processed. E.g., data stored in a database, PDF files in a file share, or EHR records in object storage.

2.caption: Data in-Transit
2.description: The data was actively moving through the network or from one physical or logical location to another. E.g., emails being send, data replication or Change Data Capture (CDC) streams, or sensitive data processed on an API.

3.caption: Data in-Use
3.description: The data was being processed, accessed, or read by a system, making it active in memory or CPU. E.g., sensitive data in a Business Intelligence tool, ePHI being processed in an EHR application or a user viewing data stored in a spreadsheet or PDF.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The data lifecycle state is not mapped. See the data_lifecycle_state attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

detection_pattern.caption: Detection Pattern
detection_pattern.description: Specific pattern, algorithm, fingerprint, or model used for detection.

detection_system.caption: Detection System
detection_system.description: The name of the type of data security tool or system that the finding, detection, or alert originated from. E.g., Endpoint, Secure Email Gateway, etc.

detection_system_id.caption: Detection System ID
detection_system_id.description: The type of data security tool or system that the finding, detection, or alert originated from.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The type is not mapped. See the detection_system attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Endpoint
1.description: A dedicated agent or sensor installed on a device, either a dedicated data security tool or an Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR) tool that can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies. E.g., Forcepoint DLP, Symantec DLP, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (MDE).

2.caption: DLP Gateway
2.description: A Data Loss Prevention (DLP) gateway that is positioned in-line of an information store such as a network share, a database, or otherwise that can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies.

3.caption: Mobile Device Management
3.description: A Mobile Device Management (MDM) or Enterprise Mobility Management (EMM) tool that can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies on mobile devices (e.g., cellphones, tablets, End User Devices [EUDs]).

4.caption: Data Discovery & Classification
4.description: A tool that actively identifies and classifies sensitive data in digital media and information stores in accordance with a policy or automated functionality. E.g, Amazon Macie, Microsoft Purview.

5.caption: Secure Web Gateway
5.description: A Secure Web Gateway (SWG) is any tool that can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies at a network-edge such as within a proxy or firewall service.

6.caption: Secure Email Gateway
6.description: A Secure Email Gateway (SEG) is any tool that can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies within email systems. E.g., Microsoft Defender for Office, Google Workspaces.

7.caption: Digital Rights Management
7.description: A Digital Rights Management (DRM) or a dedicated Information Rights Management (IRM) are tools which can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies on digital media via policy or user access rights.

8.caption: Cloud Access Security Broker
8.description: A Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) that can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies in-line to cloud systems such as the public cloud or Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) tool. E.g., Forcepoint CASB, SkyHigh Security.

9.caption: Database Activity Monitoring
9.description: A Database Activity Monitoring (DAM) tool that can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies as part of a dedicated database or warehouse monitoring solution.

10.caption: Application-Level DLP
10.description: A built in Data Loss Prevention (DLP) or other data security capability within a tool or platform such as an Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) or Customer Relations Management (CRM) tool that can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies.

11.caption: Developer Security
11.description: Any Developer Security tool such as an Infrastructure-as-Code (IAC) security scanner, Secrets Detection, or Secure Software Development Lifecycle (SSDLC) tool that can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies. E.g., TruffleHog, GitGuardian, Git-Secrets.

12.caption: Data Security Posture Management
12.description: A Data Security Posture Management (DSPM) tool is a continuous monitoring and data discovery solution that can detect sensitive data and/or enforce data security policies for local and cloud environments. E.g., Cyera, Sentra, IBM Polar Security.

99.caption: Other
99.description: Any other type of detection system or a multi-variate system made up of several other systems.

pattern_match.caption: Pattern Match
pattern_match.description: A text, binary, file name, or datastore that matched against a detection rule.

edge.caption: Edge
edge.description: Represents a connection or relationship between two nodes in a graph.
edge.name: edge

name.caption: Name
name.description: The human-readable name or label for the edge.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: Unique identifier of the edge.

data.caption: Data
data.description: Additional data about the edge such as weight, distance, or custom properties.

is_directed.caption: Directed
is_directed.description: Indicates whether the edge is (true) or undirected (false).

relation.caption: Relation
relation.description: The type of relationship between nodes (e.g. is-attached-to , depends-on, etc).

source.caption: Source
source.description: The unique identifier of the node where the edge originates.

target.caption: Target
target.description: The unique identifier of the node where the edge terminates.

email_auth.caption: Email Authentication
email_auth.description: The Email Authentication object describes the Sender Policy Framework (SPF), DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) and Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting and Conformance (DMARC) attributes of an email.
email_auth.name: email_auth

dkim.caption: DKIM Status
dkim.description: The DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) status of the email.

dkim_domain.caption: DKIM Domain
dkim_domain.description: The DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) signing domain of the email.

dkim_signature.caption: DKIM Signature
dkim_signature.description: The DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) signature used by the sending/receiving system.

dmarc.caption: DMARC Status
dmarc.description: The Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting and Conformance (DMARC) status of the email.

dmarc_override.caption: DMARC Override
dmarc_override.description: The Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting and Conformance (DMARC) override action.

dmarc_policy.caption: DMARC Policy
dmarc_policy.description: The Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting and Conformance (DMARC) policy status.

spf.caption: SPF Status
spf.description: The Sender Policy Framework (SPF) status of the email.

span.caption: Span
span.description: Represents a single unit of work or operation within a distributed trace. A span typically tracks the execution of a request across a service, capturing important details such as the operation, timestamps, and status. Spans help break down the overall trace into smaller, manageable parts, enabling detailed analysis of the performance and behavior of specific operations within the system. They are crucial for understanding latency, dependencies, and bottlenecks in complex distributed systems.
span.name: span

duration.caption: Duration Milliseconds
duration.description: The total time, in milliseconds, that the span represents, calculated as the difference between start_time and end_time. It reflects the operation's performance and latency, independent of event timestamps, and accounts for normalized times used by observability tools to ensure consistency across distributed systems.

end_time.caption: End Time
end_time.description: The end timestamp of the span, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. Like the start time, this timestamp is normalized across the observability system to ensure consistency, even when events are recorded across distributed services with unsynchronized clocks. Normalized time allows for accurate duration calculations and helps observability tools track performance across services, regardless of the individual system time settings.

message.caption: Message
message.description: The message in a span (often referred to as a span event) serves as a way to record significant moments or occurrences during the span's lifecycle. This content typically manifests as log entries, annotations, or semi-structured events as a string, providing additional granularity and context about what happens at specific points during the execution of an operation.

operation.caption: Operation
operation.description: Describes an action performed in a span, such as API requests, database queries, or computations.

parent_uid.caption: Parent Unique ID
parent_uid.description: The ID of the parent span for this span object, establishing its relationship in the trace hierarchy.

service.caption: Service
service.description: Identifies the service or component that generates the span, helping trace its path through the distributed system.

start_time.caption: Start Time
start_time.description: The start timestamp of the span, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. This timestamp is normalized across the observability system, ensuring consistency even when events occur across distributed services with potentially unsynchronized clocks. By using normalized time, observability tools can provide accurate, uniform measurements of operation performance and latency, regardless of where or when the events actually occur.

status_code.caption: Status Code
status_code.description: Indicates the outcome of the operation in the span, such as success, failure, or error. Issues in a span typically refer to problems such as failed operations, timeouts, service unavailability, or errors in processing that can negatively impact the performance or reliability of the system. Tracking the `status_code` helps pinpoint these issues, enabling quicker identification and resolution of system inefficiencies or faults.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier for the span, used in distributed systems and microservices architectures to track and correlate requests across different components of an application. It enables tracing the flow of a request through various services.

end_time_dt.caption: End Time
end_time_dt.description: The end timestamp of the span, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. Like the start time, this timestamp is normalized across the observability system to ensure consistency, even when events are recorded across distributed services with unsynchronized clocks. Normalized time allows for accurate duration calculations and helps observability tools track performance across services, regardless of the individual system time settings.

start_time_dt.caption: Start Time
start_time_dt.description: The start timestamp of the span, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. This timestamp is normalized across the observability system, ensuring consistency even when events occur across distributed services with potentially unsynchronized clocks. By using normalized time, observability tools can provide accurate, uniform measurements of operation performance and latency, regardless of where or when the events actually occur.

software_component.caption: Software Component
software_component.description: The Software Component object describes characteristics of a software component within a software package.
software_component.name: software_component

author.caption: Author
author.description: The author(s) who published the software component.

hash.caption: Hash
hash.description: Cryptographic hash to identify the binary instance of a software component.

license.caption: Software License
license.description: The software license applied to this component.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The software component name.

purl.caption: Package URL
purl.description: The Package URL (PURL) to identify the software component. This is a URL that uniquely identifies the component, including the component's name, version, and type. The URL is used to locate and retrieve the component's metadata and content.

related_component.caption: Related Component
related_component.description: The package URL (PURL) of the component that this software component has a relationship with.

relationship.caption: Relationship
relationship.description: The relationship between two software components, normalized to the caption of the relationship_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

relationship_id.caption: Relationship ID
relationship_id.description: The normalized identifier of the relationship between two software components.

1.caption: Depends On
1.description: The component is a dependency of another component. Can be used to define both direct and transitive dependencies.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The relationship is not mapped. See the relationship attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of software component, normalized to the caption of the type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The type of software component.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Framework
1.description: A software framework.

2.caption: Library
2.description: A software library.

3.caption: Operating System
3.description: An operating system. Useful for SBOMs of container images.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The software component version.

tactic.caption: MITRE Tactic
tactic.description: The MITRE Tactic object describes the ATT&CK® or ATLAS™ Tactic ID and/or name that is associated to an attack.
tactic.name: tactic

name.caption: Name
name.description: The Tactic name that is associated with the attack technique. For example: Reconnaissance or ML Model Access.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The Tactic ID that is associated with the attack technique. For example: TA0043, or AML.TA0000.

src_url.caption: Source URL
src_url.description: The versioned permalink of the Tactic. For example: https://attack.mitre.org/versions/v14/tactics/TA0043/.

rpc_interface.caption: RPC Interface
rpc_interface.description: The RPC Interface represents the remote procedure call interface used in the DCE/RPC session.
rpc_interface.name: rpc_interface

ack_reason.caption: Acknowledgement Reason
ack_reason.description: An integer that provides a reason code or additional information about the acknowledgment result.

ack_result.caption: Acknowledgement Result
ack_result.description: An integer that denotes the acknowledgment result of the DCE/RPC call.

uuid.caption: UUID
uuid.description: The unique identifier of the particular remote procedure or service.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the DCE/RPC protocol being used in the session.

endpoint_connection.caption: Endpoint Connection
endpoint_connection.description: The Endpoint Connection object contains information detailing a connection attempt to an endpoint.
endpoint_connection.name: endpoint_connection

code.caption: Response Code
code.description: A numerical response status code providing details about the connection.

network_endpoint.caption: Network Endpoint
network_endpoint.description: Provides characteristics of the network endpoint.

network_traffic.caption: Network Traffic
network_traffic.description: The Network Traffic object describes characteristics of network traffic over a time period. The metrics represent network data transferred between source and destination during an observation window.
network_traffic.name: network_traffic

bytes.caption: Total Bytes
bytes.description: The total number of bytes transferred in both directions (sum of bytes_in and bytes_out).

bytes_in.caption: Bytes In
bytes_in.description: The number of bytes sent from the destination to the source (inbound direction).

bytes_missed.caption: Bytes Missed
bytes_missed.description: The number of bytes that were missed during observation, typically due to packet loss or sampling limitations.

bytes_out.caption: Bytes Out
bytes_out.description: The number of bytes sent from the source to the destination (outbound direction).

chunks.caption: Chunks
chunks.description: The total number of chunks transferred in both directions (sum of chunks_in and chunks_out).

chunks_in.caption: Chunks In
chunks_in.description: The number of chunks sent from the destination to the source (inbound direction).

chunks_out.caption: Chunks Out
chunks_out.description: The number of chunks sent from the source to the destination (outbound direction).

end_time.caption: End Time
end_time.description: The end time of the observation or reporting period.

packets.caption: Total Packets
packets.description: The total number of packets transferred in both directions (sum of packets_in and packets_out).

packets_in.caption: Packets In
packets_in.description: The number of packets sent from the destination to the source (inbound direction).

packets_out.caption: Packets Out
packets_out.description: The number of packets sent from the source to the destination (outbound direction).

start_time.caption: Start Time
start_time.description: The start time of the observation or reporting period.

timespan.caption: Time Span
timespan.description: The time span object representing the duration of the observation or reporting period.

end_time_dt.caption: End Time
end_time_dt.description: The end time of the observation or reporting period.

start_time_dt.caption: Start Time
start_time_dt.description: The start time of the observation or reporting period.

tls.caption: Transport Layer Security (TLS)
tls.description: The Transport Layer Security (TLS) object describes the negotiated TLS protocol used for secure communications over an establish network connection.
tls.name: tls

alert.caption: Client TLS Alert
alert.description: The integer value of TLS alert if present. The alerts are defined in the TLS specification in RFC-2246.

certificate.caption: Certificate
certificate.description: The certificate object containing information about the digital certificate.

certificate_chain.caption: Certificate Chain
certificate_chain.description: The Chain of Certificate Serial Numbers field provides a chain of Certificate Issuer Serial Numbers leading to the Root Certificate Issuer.

client_ciphers.caption: Client Cipher Suites
client_ciphers.description: The client cipher suites that were exchanged during the TLS handshake negotiation.

handshake_dur.caption: Handshake Duration
handshake_dur.description: The amount of total time for the TLS handshake to complete after the TCP connection is established, including client-side delays, in milliseconds.

ja3_hash.caption: JA3 Hash
ja3_hash.description: The MD5 hash of a JA3 string.

ja3s_hash.caption: JA3S Hash
ja3s_hash.description: The MD5 hash of a JA3S string.

key_length.caption: Key Length
key_length.description: The length of the encryption key.

sans.caption: Subject Alternative Names
sans.description: The list of subject alternative names that are secured by a specific certificate.

server_ciphers.caption: Server Cipher Suites
server_ciphers.description: The server cipher suites that were exchanged during the TLS handshake negotiation.

sni.caption: Server Name Indication
sni.description: The Server Name Indication (SNI) extension sent by the client.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The TLS protocol version.

permission_analysis_result.caption: Permission Analysis Result
permission_analysis_result.description: The Permission Analysis object describes analysis results of permissions, policies directly associated with an identity (user, role, or service account). This evaluates what permissions an identity has been granted through attached policies, which privileges are actively used versus unused, and identifies potential over-privileged access. Use this for identity-centric security assessments such as privilege audits, dormant permission discovery, and least-privilege compliance analysis.
permission_analysis_result.name: permission_analysis_result

analyzed_privileges_count.caption: Analyzed Privileges Count
analyzed_privileges_count.description: The total count of privileges that were analyzed across all services.

condition_keys.caption: Condition Keys
condition_keys.description: The condition keys and their values that were evaluated during policy analysis, including contextual constraints that affect permission grants. These conditions define when and how permissions are applied. Examples: aws:SourceIp:1.2.3.4, aws:RequestedRegion:us-east-1.

granted_privileges.caption: Granted Privileges
granted_privileges.description: The specific privileges, actions, or permissions that are explicitly granted by the analyzed policy. Examples: AWS actions like s3:GetObject, ec2:RunInstances, iam:CreateUser; Azure actions like Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/read; or GCP permissions like storage.objects.get.

policy.caption: Policy
policy.description: Detailed information about the policy document that was analyzed, including policy metadata, version, type (identity-based, resource-based, etc.), and structural details. This provides context for understanding the scope and nature of the permission analysis.

service_privilege_analysis_list.caption: Service Privilege Analysis List
service_privilege_analysis_list.description: The list of privilege analysis results grouped by cloud service or namespace.

total_potential_attacks_count.caption: Total Potential Attacks Count
total_potential_attacks_count.description: The total count of privilege-to-attack technique mappings identified across all analyzed privileges.

unused_privileges_count.caption: Unused Privileges Count
unused_privileges_count.description: The total count of privileges or actions defined in the policy that have not been utilized within the analysis timeframe. This metric helps identify over-privileged access and opportunities for privilege reduction to follow the principle of least privilege. High counts may indicate policy bloat or excessive permissions.

unused_services_count.caption: Unused Services Count
unused_services_count.description: The total count of cloud services or resource types referenced in the policy that have not been accessed or utilized within the analysis timeframe. This helps identify unused service permissions that could be removed to reduce attack surface. Examples: AWS services like S3, SQS, IAM, Lambda; Azure services like Storage, Compute, KeyVault; or GCP services like Cloud Storage, Compute Engine, BigQuery.

session.caption: Session
session.description: The Session object describes details about an authenticated session. e.g. Session Creation Time, Session Issuer.
session.name: session

count.caption: Count
count.description: The number of identical sessions spawned from the same source IP, destination IP, application, and content/threat type seen over a period of time.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time when the session was created.

credential_uid.caption: User Credential ID
credential_uid.description: The unique identifier of the user's credential. For example, AWS Access Key ID.

expiration_reason.caption: Expiration Reason
expiration_reason.description: The reason which triggered the session expiration.

is_mfa.caption: Multi Factor Authentication
is_mfa.description: Indicates whether Multi Factor Authentication was used during authentication.

is_remote.caption: Remote
is_remote.description: The indication of whether the session is remote.

is_vpn.caption: VPN Session
is_vpn.description: The indication of whether the session is a VPN session.

issuer.caption: Issuer Details
issuer.description: The identifier of the session issuer.

terminal.caption: Terminal
terminal.description: The Pseudo Terminal associated with the session. Ex: the tty or pts value.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the session.

uid_alt.caption: Alternate ID
uid_alt.description: The alternate unique identifier of the session. e.g. AWS ARN - arn:aws:sts::123344444444:assumed-role/Admin/example-session.

uuid.caption: UUID
uuid.description: The universally unique identifier of the session.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time when the session was created.

assessment.caption: Assessment
assessment.description: The Assessment object describes a point-in-time assessment, check, or evaluation of a specific configuration or signal against an asset, entity, person, or otherwise. For example, this can encapsulate os_signals from CrowdStrike Falcon Zero Trust Assessments, or account for Datastore configurations from Cyera, or capture details of Microsoft Intune configuration policies.
assessment.name: assessment

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the configuration or signal being assessed. For example: Kernel Mode Code Integrity (KMCI) or publicAccessibilityState.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the configuration or signal being assessed. For example: the signal_id.

category.caption: Category
category.description: The category that the assessment is part of. For example: Prevention or Windows 10.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The description of the assessment criteria, or a description of the specific configuration or signal the assessment is targeting.

meets_criteria.caption: Meets Criteria
meets_criteria.description: Determines whether the assessment against the specific configuration or signal meets the assessments criteria. For example, if the assessment checks if a Datastore is encrypted or not, having encryption would be evaluated as true.

policy.caption: Assessment Policy
policy.description: The details of any policy associated with an assessment.

script.caption: Script
script.description: The Script object describes a script or command that can be executed by a shell, script engine, or interpreter. Examples include Bash, JavsScript, PowerShell, Python, VBScript, etc. Note that the term script here denotes not only a script contained within a file but also a script or command typed interactively by a user, supplied on the command line, or provided by some other file-less mechanism.
script.name: script

file.caption: File
file.description: Present if this script is associated with a file. Not present in the case of a file-less script.

hashes.caption: Hashes
hashes.description: An array of the script's cryptographic hashes. Note that these hashes are calculated on the script in its original encoding, and not on the normalized UTF-8 encoding found in the script_content attribute.

name.caption: Name
name.description: Unique identifier for the script or macro, independent of the containing file, used for tracking, auditing, and security analysis.

parent_uid.caption: Parent Unique ID
parent_uid.description: This attribute relates a sub-script to a parent script having the matching uid attribute. In the case of PowerShell, sub-script execution can be identified by matching the activity correlation ID of the raw ETW events provided by the OS.

script_content.caption: Script Content
script_content.description: The script content, normalized to UTF-8 encoding irrespective of its original encoding. When emitting this attribute, it may be appropriate to truncate large scripts. When consuming this attribute, large scripts should be anticipated.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The script type, normalized to the caption of the type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized script type ID.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The script type is not mapped. See the type attribute which contains an event source specific value.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: Some script engines assign a unique ID to each individual execution of a given script. This attribute captures that unique ID. In the case of PowerShell, the unique ID corresponds to the ScriptBlockId in the raw ETW events provided by the OS.

api.caption: API
api.description: The API, or Application Programming Interface, object represents  information pertaining to an API request and response.
api.name: api

group.caption: Group
group.description: The information pertaining to the API group.

request.caption: API Request Details
request.description: Details pertaining to the API request.

response.caption: API Response Details
response.description: Details pertaining to the API response.

service.caption: Service
service.description: The information pertaining to the API service.

token.caption: Token
token.description: The API or client token used to authenticate or authorize the API request. This attribute contains the base token object that represents: (1) IdP-issued client tokens (type_id: 6) such as Okta API tokens or Microsoft Entra ID Application Registration client secrets, or (2) generic API tokens/keys (type_id: 7) used for SaaS application authentication. Use this attribute when the API request was authenticated using a token that should be tracked as part of the API activity event. Note: Protocol-specific authentication tokens (Kerberos, OIDC, SAML) should be represented using authentication_token in authentication events, not in API activity events.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the API service.

authentication_token.caption: Authentication Token
authentication_token.description: The Authentication Token object extends the base token object and represents standardized authentication tokens, tickets, or assertions that conform to established authentication protocols such as Kerberos, OIDC, and SAML. This object inherits all attributes from token and adds protocol-specific attributes (e.g., kerberos_flags, encryption_details) for authentication events. Use this object in authentication events to represent protocol-specific tokens: Kerberos Ticket Granting Tickets (TGT) and Service Tickets (ST), OIDC Identity Tokens and Refresh Tokens, and SAML Assertions. These tokens are issued by authentication servers and identity providers and carry protocol-specific metadata, lifecycle information, and security attributes defined by their respective specifications. When to use this object: Use authentication_token when representing protocol-specific authentication tokens (type_id values 1-5: Kerberos TGT/ST, OIDC ID/Refresh tokens, SAML assertions) in authentication events. When NOT to use this object: Do NOT use authentication_token for API tokens or client tokens (type_id values 6-7) used in API activity events - use the base token object instead. Do NOT use authentication_token for generic API keys - use the base token object instead.
authentication_token.name: authentication_token

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time that the authentication token was created or issued. This corresponds to the token issuance time, such as the iat (issued at) claim in OIDC tokens, the issue instant in SAML assertions, or the ticket start time in Kerberos tickets.

expiration_time.caption: Expiration Time
expiration_time.description: The expiration time of the authentication token.

is_renewable.caption: Renewable
is_renewable.description: Indicates whether the authentication token is renewable.

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The last time the token was updated.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The human-friendly name of a token or key, if available, such as the name from the Okta API Token API.

tenant_uid.caption: Tenant UID
tenant_uid.description: The unique identifier of the tenant or organization that owns the token or key, or the tenant context in which the token is authorized for use. This is particularly relevant in multi-tenant Identity Provider scenarios where tokens are scoped to specific tenants.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of the authentication token.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized authentication token type identifier. This attribute restricts the base token.type_id enum to only protocol-specific authentication token types (values 0, 1-5, 99). API tokens and client tokens (values 6-7) are not valid for authentication_token - use the base token object for those types.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The Authentication token type is unknown.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Ticket Granting Ticket
1.description: Ticket Granting Ticket (TGT) for Kerberos.

2.caption: Service Ticket
2.description: Service Ticket (ST) for Kerberos.

3.caption: Identity Token
3.description: Identity (ID) Token for OIDC.

4.caption: Refresh Token
4.description: Refresh Token for OIDC.

5.caption: SAML Assertion
5.description: Authentication Assertion for SAML.

6.caption: Client Token
6.description: Client Token issued by an Identity Provider (IdP) for application authentication. Use this value for IdP-issued tokens used for service-to-service authentication. Examples: Microsoft Entra ID Application Registration client secrets, Okta API tokens, Auth0 Machine-to-Machine tokens. Key characteristic: These tokens are issued by Identity Providers, not by individual services.

7.caption: API Token
7.description: A generic API token or API key used for authenticating API requests. Use this value for service-specific API authentication tokens that are NOT issued by Identity Providers. Examples: REST API keys, GraphQL API keys, Stripe API keys, Twilio API keys, AWS API keys. Key characteristic: These tokens are issued by individual services/platforms, not by Identity Providers.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique ID of a token or key, if available, such as the Secret ID of Entra ID Application Registration Client Secrets.

zone.caption: Network Zone
zone.description: The network zone or geographic region that the token or key is authorized to be used from. This may represent network-based access restrictions, geographic limitations, or other zone-based authorization policies. Examples include Okta's network zone restrictions or cloud provider region restrictions.

encryption_details.caption: Encryption Details
encryption_details.description: The encryption details of the authentication token.

kerberos_flags.caption: Kerberos Flags
kerberos_flags.description: A bitmask, either in hexadecimal or decimal form, which encodes various attributes or permissions associated with a Kerberos ticket. These flags delineate specific characteristics of the ticket, such as its renewability or forwardability.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time that the authentication token was created or issued. This corresponds to the token issuance time, such as the iat (issued at) claim in OIDC tokens, the issue instant in SAML assertions, or the ticket start time in Kerberos tickets.

expiration_time_dt.caption: Expiration Time
expiration_time_dt.description: The expiration time of the authentication token.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The last time the token was updated.

ja4_fingerprint.caption: JA4+ Fingerprint
ja4_fingerprint.description: The JA4+ fingerprint object provides detailed fingerprint information about various aspects of network traffic which is both machine and human readable.
ja4_fingerprint.name: ja4_fingerprint

section_a.caption: JA4 Section A
section_a.description: The 'a' section of the JA4 fingerprint.

section_b.caption: JA4 Section B
section_b.description: The 'b' section of the JA4 fingerprint.

section_c.caption: JA4 Section C
section_c.description: The 'c' section of the JA4 fingerprint.

section_d.caption: JA4 Section D
section_d.description: The 'd' section of the JA4 fingerprint.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The JA4+ fingerprint type as defined by FoxIO, normalized to the caption of 'type_id'. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The identifier of the JA4+ fingerprint type.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: JA4
1.description: TLS Client Fingerprint.

2.caption: JA4Server
2.description: TLS Server Response/Session Fingerprint.

3.caption: JA4HTTP
3.description: HTTP Client Fingerprint.

4.caption: JA4Latency
4.description: Latency Measurement/Light Distance Fingerprint.

5.caption: JA4X509
5.description: X509 TLS Certificate Fingerprint.

6.caption: JA4SSH
6.description: SSH Traffic Fingerprint.

7.caption: JA4TCP
7.description: Passive TCP Client Fingerprint.

8.caption: JA4TCPServer
8.description: Passive TCP Server Fingerprint.

9.caption: JA4TCPScan
9.description: Active TCP Server Fingerprint.

cve.caption: CVE
cve.description: The Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) object represents publicly disclosed cybersecurity vulnerabilities defined in CVE Program catalog (CVE). There is one CVE Record for each vulnerability in the catalog.
cve.name: cve

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The Record Creation Date identifies when the CVE ID was issued to a CVE Numbering Authority (CNA) or the CVE Record was published on the CVE List. Note that the Record Creation Date does not necessarily indicate when this vulnerability was discovered, shared with the affected vendor, publicly disclosed, or updated in CVE.

cvss.caption: CVSS Score
cvss.description: The CVSS object details Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) scores from the advisory that are related to the vulnerability.

cwe.caption: CWE
cwe.description: The CWE object represents a weakness in a software system that can be exploited by a threat actor to perform an attack. The CWE object is based on the Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) catalog.

cwe_uid.caption: CWE UID
cwe_uid.description: The Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) unique identifier. For example: CWE-787.

cwe_url.caption: CWE URL
cwe_url.description: Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) definition URL. For example: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/787.html.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: A brief description of the CVE Record.

epss.caption: EPSS
epss.description: The Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) object describes the estimated probability a vulnerability will be exploited. EPSS is a community-driven effort to combine descriptive information about vulnerabilities (CVEs) with evidence of actual exploitation in-the-wild. (EPSS).

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The Record Modified Date identifies when the CVE record was last updated.

product.caption: Product
product.description: The product where the vulnerability was discovered.

references.caption: References
references.description: A list of reference URLs with additional information about the CVE Record.

related_cwes.caption: Related CWEs
related_cwes.description: Describes the Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) details related to the CVE Record.

title.caption: Title
title.description: A title or a brief phrase summarizing the CVE record.

type.caption: Vulnerability Type
type.description:
The vulnerability type as selected from a large dropdown menu during CVE refinement.

Most frequently used vulnerability types are: DoS, Code Execution, Overflow, Memory Corruption, Sql Injection, XSS, Directory Traversal, Http Response Splitting, Bypass something, Gain Information, Gain Privileges, CSRF, File Inclusion. For more information see Vulnerabilities By Type distributions.

uid.caption: CVE ID
uid.description: The Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures unique number assigned to a specific computer vulnerability. A CVE Identifier begins with 4 digits representing the year followed by a sequence of digits that acts as a unique identifier. For example: CVE-2021-12345.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The Record Creation Date identifies when the CVE ID was issued to a CVE Numbering Authority (CNA) or the CVE Record was published on the CVE List. Note that the Record Creation Date does not necessarily indicate when this vulnerability was discovered, shared with the affected vendor, publicly disclosed, or updated in CVE.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The Record Modified Date identifies when the CVE record was last updated.

network_interface.caption: Network Interface
network_interface.description: The Network Interface object describes the type and associated attributes of a physical or virtual network interface.
network_interface.name: network_interface

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the network interface.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier for the network interface.

hostname.caption: Hostname
hostname.description: The hostname associated with the network interface.

ip.caption: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address associated with the network interface.

mac.caption: MAC Address
mac.description: The MAC address of the network interface.

namespace.caption: Namespace
namespace.description: The namespace is useful in merger or acquisition situations. For example, when similar entities exist that you need to keep separate.

open_ports.caption: Open Ports
open_ports.description: The list of open ports on a network interface, including port numbers and associated protocol information.

subnet_prefix.caption: Subnet Prefix Length
subnet_prefix.description: The subnet prefix length determines the number of bits used to represent the network part of the IP address. The remaining bits are reserved for identifying individual hosts within that subnet.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of network interface.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The network interface type identifier.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

vulnerability.caption: Vulnerability Details
vulnerability.description: The vulnerability is an unintended characteristic of a computing component or system configuration that multiplies the risk of an adverse event or a loss occurring either due to accidental exposure, deliberate attack, or conflict with new system components.
vulnerability.name: vulnerability

advisory.caption: Security Advisory
advisory.description: Detail about the security advisory, that is used to publicly disclose cybersecurity vulnerabilities by a vendor.

affected_code.caption: Affected Code
affected_code.description: List of Affected Code objects that describe details about code blocks identified as vulnerable.

affected_packages.caption: Affected Software Packages
affected_packages.description: List of software packages identified as affected by a vulnerability/vulnerabilities.

category.caption: Category
category.description: The category of a vulnerability or weakness, as reported by the source tool, such as Container Security or Open Source Security.

cve.caption: CVE
cve.description: Describes the Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) details related to the vulnerability.

cwe.caption: CWE
cwe.description: Describes the Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) details related to the vulnerability.

dependency_chain.caption: Dependency Chain
dependency_chain.description: Information about the chain of dependencies related to the issue as reported by an Application Security or Vulnerability Management tool. E.g., serverless-offline -> @serverless/utils -> memoizee -> es5-ext.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The description of the vulnerability.

exploit_last_seen_time.caption: Exploit Last Seen Time
exploit_last_seen_time.description: The time when the exploit was most recently observed.

exploit_ref_url.caption: Exploit URL
exploit_ref_url.description: The URL of the exploit code or Proof-of-Concept (PoC).

exploit_requirement.caption: Exploit Requirement
exploit_requirement.description: The requirement description related to any constraints around exploit execution.

exploit_type.caption: Exploit Type
exploit_type.description: The categorization or type of Exploit. E.g., Network or Physical.

first_seen_time.caption: First Seen
first_seen_time.description: The time when the vulnerability was first observed.

fix_available.caption: Fix Availability
fix_available.description: Indicates if a fix is available for the reported vulnerability.

fix_coverage.caption: Fix Coverage
fix_coverage.description: The fix coverage, normalized to the caption of the fix_coverage_id value.

fix_coverage_id.caption: Fix Coverage ID
fix_coverage_id.description: The normalized identifier for fix coverage, applicable to this vulnerability. Typically useful, when there are multiple affected packages but only a subset have available fixes.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The fix coverage is not mapped. See the fix_coverage attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Complete
1.description: All affected packages and components have available fixes or patches to remediate the vulnerability.

2.caption: Partial
2.description: Only some of the affected packages and components have available fixes or patches, while others remain vulnerable.

3.caption: None
3.description: No fixes or patches are currently available for any of the affected packages and components.

is_exploit_available.caption: Exploit Availability
is_exploit_available.description: Indicates if an exploit or a PoC (proof-of-concept) is available for the reported vulnerability.

is_fix_available.caption: Fix Availability
is_fix_available.description: Indicates if a fix is available for the reported vulnerability.

kb_article_list.caption: Knowledgebase Articles
kb_article_list.description: A list of KB articles or patches related to an endpoint. A KB Article contains metadata that describes the patch or an update.

kb_articles.caption: Knowledgebase Articles
kb_articles.description: The KB article/s related to the entity. A KB Article contains metadata that describes the patch or an update.

last_seen_time.caption: Last Seen
last_seen_time.description: The time when the vulnerability was most recently observed.

packages.caption: Software Packages
packages.description: List of vulnerable packages as identified by the security product

references.caption: References
references.description: A list of reference URLs with additional information about the vulnerability.

related_vulnerabilities.caption: Related Vulnerability IDs
related_vulnerabilities.description: List of vulnerability IDs (e.g. CVE ID) that are related to this vulnerability.

remediation.caption: Remediation Guidance
remediation.description: The remediation recommendations on how to mitigate the identified vulnerability.

severity.caption: Severity
severity.description: The vendor assigned severity of the vulnerability.

title.caption: Title
title.description: A title or a brief phrase summarizing the discovered vulnerability.

vendor_name.caption: Vendor Name
vendor_name.description: The name of the vendor that identified the vulnerability.

exploit_last_seen_time_dt.caption: Exploit Last Seen Time
exploit_last_seen_time_dt.description: The time when the exploit was most recently observed.

first_seen_time_dt.caption: First Seen
first_seen_time_dt.description: The time when the vulnerability was first observed.

last_seen_time_dt.caption: Last Seen
last_seen_time_dt.description: The time when the vulnerability was most recently observed.

os.caption: Operating System (OS)
os.description: The Operating System (OS) object describes characteristics of an OS, such as Linux or Windows.
os.name: os

country.caption: Country
country.description:
The operating system country code, as defined by the ISO 3166-1 standard (Alpha-2 code).

Note: The two letter country code should be capitalized. For example: US or CA.

cpe_name.caption: The product CPE identifier
cpe_name.description: The Common Platform Enumeration (CPE) name as described by (NIST) For example: cpe:/a:apple:safari:16.2.

cpu_bits.caption: CPU Bits
cpu_bits.description: The cpu architecture, the number of bits used for addressing in memory. For example: 32 or 64.

edition.caption: OS Edition
edition.description: The operating system edition. For example: Professional.

kernel_release.caption: Kernel Release
kernel_release.description: The kernel release of the operating system. On Unix-based systems, this is determined from the uname -r command output, for example "5.15.0-122-generic".

lang.caption: Language
lang.description: The two letter lower case language codes, as defined by ISO 639-1. For example: en (English), de (German), or fr (French).

name.caption: Name
name.description: The operating system name.

sp_name.caption: OS Service Pack
sp_name.description: The name of the latest Service Pack.

sp_ver.caption: OS Service Pack Version
sp_ver.description: The version number of the latest Service Pack.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of the operating system.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The type identifier of the operating system.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the OS running on the device that originated the event. For example: "Windows 10", "OS X 10.7", or "iOS 9".

databucket.caption: Databucket
databucket.description: The databucket object is a basic container that holds data, typically organized through the use of data partitions.
databucket.name: databucket

name.caption: Name
name.description: The databucket name.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the databucket.

data_classification.caption: Data Classification
data_classification.description: The Data Classification object includes information about data classification levels and data category types.

data_classifications.caption: Data Classification
data_classifications.description: A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time when the databucket was known to have been created.

labels.caption: Labels
labels.description: The list of labels associated to the resource.

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The most recent time when any changes, updates, or modifications were made within the databucket.

tags.caption: Tags
tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the resource.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The databucket type.

uid_alt.caption: Alternate ID
uid_alt.description: The alternative unique identifier of the resource.

agent_list.caption: Agent List
agent_list.description: A list of agent objects associated with a device, endpoint, or resource.

cloud_partition.caption: Cloud Partition
cloud_partition.description: The logical grouping or isolated segment within a cloud provider's infrastructure where the databucket is located.

criticality.caption: Criticality
criticality.description: The criticality of the databucket as defined by the event source.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The description of the databucket.

encryption_details.caption: Encryption Details
encryption_details.description: The encryption details of the databucket. Should be populated if the databucket is encrypted.

file.caption: File
file.description: Details about the file/object within a databucket.

group.caption: Group
group.description: The name of the related resource group.

groups.caption: Groups
groups.description: The group names to which the databucket belongs.

hostname.caption: Hostname
hostname.description: The fully qualified hostname of the databucket.

ip.caption: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address of the resource, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.

is_backed_up.caption: Back Ups Configured
is_backed_up.description: Indicates whether the device or resource has a backup enabled, such as an automated snapshot or a cloud backup. For example, this is indicated by the cloudBackupEnabled value within JAMF Pro mobile devices or the registration of an AWS ARN with the AWS Backup service.

is_encrypted.caption: Encrypted
is_encrypted.description: Indicates if the databucket is encrypted.

is_public.caption: Public
is_public.description: Indicates if the databucket is publicly accessible.

namespace.caption: Namespace
namespace.description: The namespace is useful when similar entities exist that you need to keep separate.

owner.caption: Owner
owner.description: The identity of the service or user account that owns the databucket.

region.caption: Region
region.description: The cloud region of the databucket.

resource_relationship.caption: Resource Relationship
resource_relationship.description: A graph representation showing how this databucket relates to and interacts with other entities in the environment. This can include parent/child relationships, dependencies, or other connections.

size.caption: Size
size.description: The size of the databucket in bytes.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized identifier of the databucket type.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the resource. For example 1.2.3.

zone.caption: Cloud Availability Zone
zone.description: The specific availability zone within a cloud region where the databucket is located.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time when the databucket was known to have been created.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The most recent time when any changes, updates, or modifications were made within the databucket.

token.caption: Token
token.description: The Token object is the base object for representing tokens, API keys, and authentication credentials used across different contexts. This object provides common attributes for all token types, including protocol-specific authentication tokens (Kerberos, OIDC, SAML) and API/client tokens used for service authentication. When to use this object: Use the base token object directly in API activity events to represent API tokens, client tokens, or API keys used to authenticate API requests. Examples include: Okta API tokens, Microsoft Entra ID Application Registration client secrets, Stripe API keys, AWS API keys. When NOT to use this object: Do NOT use the base token object for protocol-specific authentication tokens in authentication events - use authentication_token instead (which extends this object). Do NOT use token for tracking credential lifecycle and usage patterns - use programmatic_credential instead.
token.name: token

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time that the token was created.

expiration_time.caption: Expiration Time
expiration_time.description: The expiration time of the token.

is_renewable.caption: Renewable
is_renewable.description: Indicates whether the token is renewable.

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The last time the token was updated.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The human-friendly name of a token or key, if available, such as the name from the Okta API Token API.

tenant_uid.caption: Tenant UID
tenant_uid.description: The unique identifier of the tenant or organization that owns the token or key, or the tenant context in which the token is authorized for use. This is particularly relevant in multi-tenant Identity Provider scenarios where tokens are scoped to specific tenants.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of the token, normalized to the caption of the type_id value. This indicates whether the token is a Client Token, API Token, or one of the protocol-specific token types.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized token type identifier. Valid values: 0 (Unknown), 1 (Ticket Granting Ticket - Kerberos), 2 (Service Ticket - Kerberos), 3 (Identity Token - OIDC), 4 (Refresh Token - OIDC), 5 (SAML Assertion), 6 (Client Token - IdP-issued), 7 (API Token - generic API keys), 99 (Other).

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Ticket Granting Ticket
1.description: Ticket Granting Ticket (TGT) for Kerberos.

2.caption: Service Ticket
2.description: Service Ticket (ST) for Kerberos.

3.caption: Identity Token
3.description: Identity (ID) Token for OIDC.

4.caption: Refresh Token
4.description: Refresh Token for OIDC.

5.caption: SAML Assertion
5.description: Authentication Assertion for SAML.

6.caption: Client Token
6.description: Client Token issued by an Identity Provider (IdP) for application authentication. Use this value for IdP-issued tokens used for service-to-service authentication. Examples: Microsoft Entra ID Application Registration client secrets, Okta API tokens, Auth0 Machine-to-Machine tokens. Key characteristic: These tokens are issued by Identity Providers, not by individual services.

7.caption: API Token
7.description: A generic API token or API key used for authenticating API requests. Use this value for service-specific API authentication tokens that are NOT issued by Identity Providers. Examples: REST API keys, GraphQL API keys, Stripe API keys, Twilio API keys, AWS API keys. Key characteristic: These tokens are issued by individual services/platforms, not by Identity Providers.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique ID of a token or key, if available, such as the Secret ID of Entra ID Application Registration Client Secrets.

zone.caption: Network Zone
zone.description: The network zone or geographic region that the token or key is authorized to be used from. This may represent network-based access restrictions, geographic limitations, or other zone-based authorization policies. Examples include Okta's network zone restrictions or cloud provider region restrictions.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time that the token was created.

expiration_time_dt.caption: Expiration Time
expiration_time_dt.description: The expiration time of the token.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The last time the token was updated.

http_header.caption: HTTP Header
http_header.description: The HTTP Header object represents the headers sent in an HTTP request or response. HTTP headers are key-value pairs that convey additional information about the HTTP message, including details about the content, caching, authentication, encoding, and other aspects of the communication.
http_header.name: http_header

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the HTTP header.

value.caption: Value
value.description: The value of the HTTP header.

port_info.caption: Port Information
port_info.description: The Port Information object describes a port and its associated protocol details.
port_info.name: port_info

protocol_name.caption: Protocol Name
protocol_name.description: The IP protocol name in lowercase, as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA). For example: tcp or udp.

protocol_num.caption: Protocol Number
protocol_num.description: The IP protocol number, as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA). For example: 6 for TCP and 17 for UDP.

query_info.caption: Query Information
query_info.description: The query info object holds information related to data access within a datastore. To access, manipulate, delete, or retrieve data from a datastore, a query must be written using a specific syntax.
query_info.name: query_info

name.caption: Name
name.description: The query name for a saved or scheduled query.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the query.

bytes.caption: Total Bytes
bytes.description: The size of the data returned from the query.

data.caption: Data
data.description: The data returned from the query execution.

query_string.caption: Query String
query_string.description: A string representing the query code being run. For example: SELECT * FROM my_table

query_time.caption: Query Time
query_time.description: The time when the query was run.

query_time_dt.caption: Query Time
query_time_dt.description: The time when the query was run.

managed_entity.caption: Managed Entity
managed_entity.description: The Managed Entity object describes the type and version of an entity, such as a user, device, or policy. For types in the type_id enum list, an associated attribute should be populated. If the type of entity is not in the type_id list, information can be put into the data attribute, type_id should be 'Other' and the type attribute should label the entity type.
managed_entity.name: managed_entity

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the managed entity. It should match the name of the specific entity object's name if populated, or the name of the managed entity if the type_id is 'Other'.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The identifier of the managed entity. It should match the uid of the specific entity's object UID if populated, or the source specific ID if the type_id is 'Other'.

data.caption: Data
data.description: The managed entity content as a JSON object.

device.caption: Device
device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.

group.caption: Group
group.description: The group object associated with an entity such as user, policy, or rule.

location.caption: Geo Location
location.description: The detailed geographical location usually associated with an IP address.

org.caption: Organization
org.description: The Organization object containing details about the managed organizational entity. This object includes properties such as the organization name, unique identifier, type, and other organizational metadata. This attribute should be populated when type_id is 4 (Organization).

policy.caption: Policy
policy.description: Describes details of a managed policy.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The managed entity type. For example: Policy, User, Organization, Device.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The type of the Managed Entity. It is recommended to also populate the type attribute with the associated label, or the source specific name if Other.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Device
1.description: A managed Device entity. This item corresponds to population of the device attribute.

2.caption: User
2.description: A managed User entity. This item corresponds to population of the user attribute.

3.caption: Group
3.description: A managed Group entity. This item corresponds to population of the group attribute.

4.caption: Organization
4.description: A managed Organization entity. This item corresponds to population of the org attribute.

5.caption: Policy
5.description: A managed Policy entity. This item corresponds to population of the policy attribute.

6.caption: Email
6.description: A managed Email entity. This item corresponds to population of the email attribute.

7.caption: Network Zone
7.description: A managed Network Zone entity. Populate the name attribute with the zone name and/or the uid attribute with the zone ID. Additional zone information can be added to the data attribute.

user.caption: User
user.description: The user that pertains to the event or object.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the managed entity. For example: 1.2.3.

d3f_technique.caption: MITRE D3FEND™ Technique
d3f_technique.description: The MITRE D3FEND™ Technique object describes the leaf defensive technique ID and/or name associated to a countermeasure.
d3f_technique.name: d3f_technique

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the defensive technique. For example: IO Port Restriction.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the defensive technique. For example: D3-IOPR.

src_url.caption: Source URL
src_url.description: The versioned permalink of the defensive technique. For example: https://d3fend.mitre.org/technique/d3f:IOPortRestriction/.

observation.caption: Observation
observation.description: A record of an observed value or event that captures the timing and frequency of its occurrence. Used to track when values/events were first detected, last detected, and their total occurrence count.
observation.name: observation

count.caption: Count
count.description: Integer representing the total number of times this specific value/event was observed across all occurrences. Helps establish prevalence and patterns.

timespan.caption: Time Span
timespan.description: The time window when the value or event was first observed. It is used to analyze activity patterns, detect trends, or correlate events within a specific timeframe.

value.caption: Value
value.description: The specific value, event, indicator or data point that was observed and recorded. This is the core piece of information being tracked.

location.caption: Geo Location
location.description: The Geo Location object describes a geographical location, usually associated with an IP address.
location.name: location

aerial_height.caption: Aerial Height
aerial_height.description: Expressed as either height above takeoff location or height above ground level (AGL) for a UAS current location. This value is provided in meters and must have a minimum resolution of 1 m. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: -1000 m.

coordinates.caption: Coordinates
coordinates.description: A two-element array, containing a longitude/latitude pair. The format conforms with GeoJSON. For example: [-73.983, 40.719].

country.caption: Country
country.description:
The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-2 country code.

Note: The two letter country code should be capitalized. For example: US or CA.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The description of the geographical location.

geodetic_altitude.caption: Geodetic Altitude
