geodetic_altitude.description: The aircraft distance above or below the ellipsoid as measured along a line that passes through the aircraft and is normal to the surface of the WGS-84 ellipsoid. This value is provided in meters and must have a minimum resolution of 1 m. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: -1000 m.

geodetic_vertical_accuracy.caption: Geodetic Vertical Accuracy
geodetic_vertical_accuracy.description: Provides quality/containment on geodetic altitude. This is based on ADS-B Geodetic Vertical Accuracy (GVA). Measured in meters.

geohash.caption: Geohash
geohash.description:
Geohash of the geo-coordinates (latitude and longitude).

Geohashing is a geocoding system used to encode geographic coordinates in decimal degrees, to a single string.

horizontal_accuracy.caption: Horizontal Accuracy
horizontal_accuracy.description: Provides quality/containment on horizontal position. This is based on ADS-B NACp. Measured in meters.

is_on_premises.caption: On-Premises
is_on_premises.description: Indicates whether the location is on-premises.

isp.caption: ISP Name
isp.description: The name of the Internet Service Provider (ISP).

@deprecated.message: Utilize isp attribute available in network_endpoint, whois objects according to your use-case.

lat.caption: Latitude
lat.description: The geographical Latitude coordinate represented in Decimal Degrees (DD). For example: 42.361145.

long.caption: Longitude
long.description: The geographical Longitude coordinate represented in Decimal Degrees (DD). For example: -71.057083.

postal_code.caption: Postal Code
postal_code.description: The postal code of the location.

pressure_altitude.caption: Pressure Altitude
pressure_altitude.description: The uncorrected barometric pressure altitude (based on reference standard 29.92 inHg, 1013.25 mb) provides a reference for algorithms that utilize 'altitude deltas' between aircraft. This value is provided in meters and must have a minimum resolution of 1 m.. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: -1000 m.

provider.caption: Provider
provider.description: The provider of the geographical location data.

region.caption: Region
region.description: The alphanumeric code that identifies the principal subdivision (e.g. province or state) of the country. For example, 'CH-VD' for the Canton of Vaud, Switzerland

resource_details.caption: Resource Details
resource_details.description: The Resource Details object describes details about resources that were affected by the activity/event.
resource_details.name: resource_details

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the resource.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the resource.

data_classification.caption: Data Classification
data_classification.description: The Data Classification object includes information about data classification levels and data category types.

data_classifications.caption: Data Classification
data_classifications.description: A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time when the resource was created.

labels.caption: Labels
labels.description: The list of labels associated to the resource.

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The time when the resource was last modified.

tags.caption: Tags
tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the resource.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The resource type as defined by the event source.

uid_alt.caption: Alternate ID
uid_alt.description: The alternative unique identifier of the resource.

agent_list.caption: Agent List
agent_list.description: A list of agent objects associated with a device, endpoint, or resource.

cloud_partition.caption: Cloud Partition
cloud_partition.description: The logical grouping or isolated segment within a cloud provider's infrastructure where the resource is located. Examples include AWS partitions (aws, aws-cn, aws-us-gov), Azure cloud environments (AzureCloud, AzureUSGovernment, AzureChinaCloud), or similar logical divisions in other cloud providers.

criticality.caption: Criticality
criticality.description: The criticality of the resource as defined by the event source.

group.caption: Group
group.description: The name of the related resource group.

hostname.caption: Hostname
hostname.description: The fully qualified name of the resource.

ip.caption: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address of the resource, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.

is_backed_up.caption: Back Ups Configured
is_backed_up.description: Indicates whether the device or resource has a backup enabled, such as an automated snapshot or a cloud backup. For example, this is indicated by the cloudBackupEnabled value within JAMF Pro mobile devices or the registration of an AWS ARN with the AWS Backup service.

namespace.caption: Namespace
namespace.description: The namespace is useful when similar entities exist that you need to keep separate.

owner.caption: Owner
owner.description: The details of the entity that owns the resource. This object includes properties such as the owner's name, unique identifier, type, domain, and other relevant attributes that help identify the resource owner within the environment.

provider.caption: Cloud Provider
provider.description: The cloud service provider that hosts or manages the resource. This field is typically used when the resource is managed by a different provider than the one generating the event or finding. Examples include AWS, Azure, GCP (Google Cloud Platform), Oracle Cloud, IBM Cloud, Alibaba Cloud, or other public, private, or hybrid cloud providers.

region.caption: Region
region.description: The cloud region where the resource is hosted, as defined by the cloud provider. This represents the physical or logical geographic area containing the infrastructure supporting the resource. Examples include AWS regions (us-east-1, eu-west-1), Azure regions (East US, West Europe), GCP regions (us-central1, europe-west1), or Oracle Cloud regions (us-ashburn-1, uk-london-1).

resource_relationship.caption: Resource Relationship
resource_relationship.description: A graph representation showing how this resource relates to and interacts with other entities in the environment. This can include parent/child relationships, dependencies, or other connections.

role.caption: Role
role.description: The role of the resource in the context of the event or finding, normalized to the caption of the role_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

role_id.caption: Role ID
role_id.description: The normalized identifier of the resource's role in the context of the event or finding.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The role is not mapped. See the role attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Target
1.description: The resource is the primary target or subject of the event/finding.

2.caption: Actor
2.description: The resource is acting as the initiator or performer in the context of the event/finding.

3.caption: Affected
3.description: The resource was impacted or affected by the event/finding.

4.caption: Related
4.description: The resource is related to or associated with the event/finding.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the resource. For example 1.2.3.

zone.caption: Cloud Availability Zone
zone.description: The availability zone within a cloud region where the resource is located. Examples include AWS availability zones (us-east-1a, us-east-1b), Azure availability zones (1, 2, 3 within a region), GCP zones (us-central1-a, us-central1-b), or Oracle Cloud availability domains (AD-1, AD-2, AD-3).

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time when the resource was created.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The time when the resource was last modified.

reporter.caption: Reporter
reporter.description: The entity from which an event or finding was reported.
reporter.name: reporter

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the entity from which the event or finding was reported.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the entity from which the event or finding was reported.

hostname.caption: Hostname
hostname.description: The hostname of the entity from which the event or finding was reported.

ip.caption: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address of the entity from which the event or finding was reported.

org.caption: Organization
org.description: The organization properties of the entity that reported the event or finding.

ticket.caption: Ticket
ticket.description: The Ticket object represents ticket in the customer's IT Service Management (ITSM) systems like ServiceNow, Jira, etc.
ticket.name: ticket

src_url.caption: Source URL
src_url.description: The url of a ticket in the ticket system.

status.caption: Ticket Status
status.description: The status of the ticket normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 99, this value should as defined by the source.

status_details.caption: Status Details
status_details.description: A list of contextual descriptions of the status, status_id values.

status_id.caption: Ticket Status ID
status_id.description: The normalized identifier for the ticket status.

1.caption: New
1.description: The ticket is new and yet to be reviewed.

2.caption: In Progress
2.description: The ticket is actively being worked on.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The status is not mapped. See the status attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

3.caption: Notified
3.description: Relevant parties have been notified about the ticket.

4.caption: On Hold
4.description: Work on the ticket is temporarily suspended.

5.caption: Resolved
5.description: The ticket is resolved and a solution is implemented, pending confirmation or verification from the requestor.

6.caption: Closed
6.description: The ticket has been completed and closed.

7.caption: Canceled
7.description: The ticket has been canceled and will not be processed.

8.caption: Reopened
8.description: The ticket was previously closed, but has been reopened.

type.caption: Ticket Type
type.description: The linked ticket type determines whether the ticket is internal or in an external ticketing system.

type_id.caption: Ticket Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized identifier for the ticket type.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: Unique identifier of the ticket.

malware.caption: Malware
malware.description: The Malware object describes the classification of known malicious software, which is intentionally designed to cause damage to a computer, server, client, or computer network.
malware.name: malware

name.caption: Name
name.description: The malware name, as reported by the detection engine.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: A unique identifier for the specific malware instance, as assigned by the detection engine (e.g., virus signature ID or IPS rule ID).

classification_ids.caption: Classification IDs
classification_ids.description: The list of normalized identifiers of the malware classifications.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The classification is not mapped. See the classifications attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

classifications.caption: Classifications
classifications.description: The list of malware classifications, normalized to the captions of the classification_ids values. In the case of 'Other', they are defined by the event source.

cves.caption: CVE List
cves.description: The list of Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) identifiers associated with the malware. Reference: CVE

files.caption: Files
files.description: The list of file objects representing files that were identified as infected by the malware.

num_infected.caption: Number of Infected Entities
num_infected.description: The number of files that were identified to be infected by the malware.

path.caption: Path
path.description: The filesystem path of the malware that was observed.

provider.caption: Provider
provider.description: The name or identifier of the security solution or service that provided the malware detection information.

severity.caption: Severity
severity.description: The severity of the malware, normalized to the captions of the severity_id values. In the case of 'Other', they are defined by the event source.

severity_id.caption: Severity ID
severity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the malware severity.

1.caption: Informational
1.description: Informational message. No action required.

2.caption: Low
2.description: The user decides if action is needed.

3.caption: Medium
3.description: Action is required but the situation is not serious at this time.

4.caption: High
4.description: Action is required immediately.

5.caption: Critical
5.description: Action is required immediately and the scope is broad.

6.caption: Fatal
6.description: An error occurred but it is too late to take remedial action.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The event/finding severity is not mapped. See the severity attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

occurrence_details.caption: Occurrence Details
occurrence_details.description: Details about where in the target entity, specified information was discovered. Only the attributes, relevant to the target entity type should be populated.
occurrence_details.name: occurrence_details

cell_name.caption: Cell Name
cell_name.description: The cell name/reference in a spreadsheet. e.g A2

column_name.caption: Column Name
column_name.description: The column name in a spreadsheet, where the information was discovered.

column_number.caption: Column Number
column_number.description: The column number in a spreadsheet or a plain text document, where the information was discovered.

end_line.caption: End Line
end_line.description: The line number of the last line of the file, where the information was discovered.

json_path.caption: JSON Path
json_path.description: The JSON path of the attribute in a json record, where the information was discovered

page_number.caption: Page Number
page_number.description: The page number in a document, where the information was discovered.

record_index_in_array.caption: Record Index in Array
record_index_in_array.description: The index of the record in the array of records, where the information was discovered. e.g. the index of a record in an array of JSON records in a file.

row_number.caption: Row Number
row_number.description: The row number in a spreadsheet, where the information was discovered.

start_line.caption: Start Line
start_line.description: The line number of the first line of the file, where the information was discovered.

packet.caption: Packet
packet.description: The Packet object represents a single captured network packet and its associated metadata. It describes where the packet came from, how it is stored or encoded, and how it can be located within a capture file or stream. This object does not interpret protocol content; it only represents the captured packet data and its positioning information.
packet.name: packet

encoding.caption: Encoding
encoding.description: The human-readable name of the encoding used to represent the packet data in the value field. This should match the caption associated with encoding_id. If encoding_id is 99 (Other), this field contains the original data source–specific encoding value.

encoding_id.caption: Encoding ID
encoding_id.description: The normalized identifier of the encoding method used to represent the packet data as a string.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The encoding format of the packet data is not known.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The encoding method is not mapped. Refer to the encoding field for the original source-specific value.

1.caption: Base64
1.description: The packet data is encoded using Base64.

2.caption: Hexadecimal
2.description: The packet data is encoded as a hexadecimal string representation of the raw bytes.

3.caption: URL Encoded
3.description: The packet data is encoded using percent-encoding (URL encoding).

end_offset.caption: End Offset
end_offset.description: The ending byte position of this packet within a capture file or stream.

format.caption: Format
format.description: The human-readable name of the packet capture file format in which the packet is stored. This should match the caption associated with format_id. If format_id is 99 (Other), this field contains the original data source–specific format value.

format_id.caption: Format ID
format_id.description: The normalized identifier of the packet capture format.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The packet format is not mapped. Refer to the format field for the original source-specific value.

1.caption: PCAP
1.description: Standard libpcap/tcpdump packet capture file format.

2.caption: PCAPNG
2.description: Next-generation PCAP format that supports multiple interfaces and enhanced metadata.

3.caption: Snoop
3.description: Solaris/Unix capture format.

4.caption: ERF
4.description: Extensible Record Format used by Endace network monitoring hardware.

5.caption: NetMon
5.description: Microsoft Network Monitor capture file format.

6.caption: 5Views
6.description: Accellent 5Views packet capture format.

sequence_number.caption: Sequence Number
sequence_number.description: The relative order number of this packet within its capture context (such as a PCAP file, network session, or reconstructed stream). This represents chronological capture order, distinct from both protocol-level sequencing (such as TCP sequence numbers).

source.caption: Source
source.description: The human-readable name describing how or where the packet was obtained. This should match the caption associated with source_id. If source_id is 99 (Other), this field contains the original data source–specific value.

source_id.caption: Source ID
source_id.description: A normalized numeric identifier that specifies how the packet was obtained or generated.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The packet source is not mapped. Refer to the source field for the original source-specific value.

1.caption: Wire
1.description: The packet was captured directly from a network interface.

2.caption: Stream
2.description: The packet was reconstructed or derived from a stream of packets.

3.caption: Decoder
3.description: The packet was generated or extracted by a protocol decoder or analysis engine.

4.caption: TAP
4.description: The packet was captured from a physical network Test Access Point (TAP) device used for passive monitoring.

5.caption: SPAN
5.description: The packet was captured from a switch Switched Port Analyzer (SPAN) or mirror port.

6.caption: Endpoint
6.description: The packet was captured by a host-based agent or endpoint detection and response (EDR) sensor.

7.caption: Virtual
7.description: The packet was captured from a virtual network interface, virtual switch, or container network.

start_offset.caption: Start Offset
start_offset.description: The starting byte position of this packet within a capture file or stream.

value.caption: Value
value.description: The actual packet data, represented as a string. The format of this string is determined by the specified encoding_id (e.g., Base64, Hexadecimal, or URL Encoded).

kernel.caption: Kernel Resource
kernel.description: The Kernel Resource object provides information about a specific kernel resource, including its name and type. It describes essential attributes associated with a resource managed by the kernel of an operating system.
kernel.name: kernel

is_system.caption: System
is_system.description: The indication of whether the object is part of the operating system.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the kernel resource.

path.caption: Path
path.description: The full path of the kernel resource.

system_call.caption: System Call
system_call.description: The system call that was invoked.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of the kernel resource.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The type of the kernel resource.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

http_response.caption: HTTP Response
http_response.description: The HTTP Response object contains detailed information about the response sent from a web server to the requester. It encompasses attributes and metadata that describe the response status, headers, body content, and other relevant information.
http_response.name: http_response

body_length.caption: Response Body Length
body_length.description: The actual length of the HTTP response body, in number of bytes, independent of a potentially existing Content-Length header.

code.caption: Response Code
code.description: The Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) status code returned from the web server to the client. For example, 200.

content_type.caption: HTTP Content Type
content_type.description: The request header that identifies the original media type of the resource (prior to any content encoding applied for sending).

http_headers.caption: HTTP Headers
http_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers of an HTTP request or response.

latency.caption: Latency
latency.description: The HTTP response latency measured in milliseconds.

length.caption: Response Length
length.description: The length of the entire HTTP response, in number of bytes.

message.caption: Message
message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

status.caption: Status
status.description: The response status. For example: A successful HTTP status of 'OK' which corresponds to a code of 200.

device.caption: Device
device.description: The Device object represents an addressable computer system or host, which is typically connected to a computer network and participates in the transmission or processing of data within the computer network.
device.name: device

name.caption: Name
name.description:
The alternate device name, ordinarily as assigned by an administrator.

Note: The Name could be any other string that helps to identify the device, such as a phone number; for example 310-555-1234.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the device. For example the Windows TargetSID or AWS EC2 ARN.

container.caption: Container
container.description: The information describing an instance of a container. A container is a prepackaged, portable system image that runs isolated on an existing system using a container runtime like containerd.

namespace_pid.caption: Namespace PID
namespace_pid.description: If running under a process namespace (such as in a container), the process identifier within that process namespace.

agent_list.caption: Agent List
agent_list.description: A list of agent objects associated with a device, endpoint, or resource.

domain.caption: Domain
domain.description: The network domain where the device resides. For example: work.example.com.

instance_uid.caption: Instance ID
instance_uid.description: The unique identifier of a VM instance.

interface_name.caption: Network Interface Name
interface_name.description: The name of the network interface (e.g. eth2).

interface_uid.caption: Network Interface ID
interface_uid.description: The unique identifier of the network interface.

ip.caption: IP Address
ip.description: The device IP address, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.

location.caption: Geo Location
location.description: The geographical location of the device.

mac.caption: MAC Address
mac.description: The Media Access Control (MAC) address of the endpoint.

mac_vendor.caption: MAC Vendor
mac_vendor.description: The vendor or manufacturer of the endpoint's network interface controller (NIC), as identified from the MAC address.

owner.caption: Owner
owner.description: The identity of the service or user account that owns the endpoint or was last logged into it.

pool.caption: Pool
pool.description: The pool of desktops or virtual machines to which the endpoint belongs.

subnet_uid.caption: Subnet UID
subnet_uid.description: The unique identifier of a virtual subnet.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The device type. For example: unknown, server, desktop, laptop, tablet, mobile, virtual, browser, or other.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The device type ID.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Server
1.description: A server.

2.caption: Desktop
2.description: A desktop computer.

3.caption: Laptop
3.description: A laptop computer.

4.caption: Tablet
4.description: A tablet computer.

5.caption: Mobile
5.description: A mobile phone.

6.caption: Virtual
6.description: A virtual machine.

7.caption: IOT
7.description: An IOT (Internet of Things) device.

8.caption: Browser
8.description: A web browser.

9.caption: Firewall
9.description: A networking firewall.

10.caption: Switch
10.description: A networking switch.

11.caption: Hub
11.description: A networking hub.

12.caption: Router
12.description: A networking router.

13.caption: IDS
13.description: An intrusion detection system.

14.caption: IPS
14.description: An intrusion prevention system.

15.caption: Load Balancer
15.description: A Load Balancer device.

vpc_uid.caption: VPC UID
vpc_uid.description: The unique identifier of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

zone.caption: Network Zone
zone.description: The network zone or LAN segment.

autoscale_uid.caption: Autoscale UID
autoscale_uid.description: The unique identifier of the cloud autoscale configuration.

boot_time.caption: Boot Time
boot_time.description: The time the system was booted.

boot_uid.caption: Boot UID
boot_uid.description: A unique identifier of the device that changes after every reboot. For example, the value of /proc/sys/kernel/random/boot_id from Linux's procfs.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time when the device was known to have been created.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The description of the device, ordinarily as reported by the operating system.

eid.caption: EID
eid.description: An Embedded Identity Document, is a unique serial number that identifies an eSIM-enabled device.

first_seen_time.caption: First Seen
first_seen_time.description: The initial discovery time of the device.

groups.caption: Groups
groups.description: The group names to which the device belongs. For example: ["Windows Laptops", "Engineering"].

hypervisor.caption: Hypervisor
hypervisor.description: The name of the hypervisor running on the device. For example, Xen, VMware, Hyper-V, VirtualBox, etc.

iccid.caption: ICCID
iccid.description: The Integrated Circuit Card Identification of a mobile device. Typically it is a unique 18 to 22 digit number that identifies a SIM card.

image.caption: Image
image.description: The image used as a template to run the virtual machine.

imei.caption: IMEI
imei.description: The International Mobile Equipment Identity that is associated with the device.

imei_list.caption: IMEI List
imei_list.description: The International Mobile Equipment Identity values that are associated with the device.

is_backed_up.caption: Back Ups Configured
is_backed_up.description: Indicates whether the device or resource has a backup enabled, such as an automated snapshot or a cloud backup. For example, this is indicated by the cloudBackupEnabled value within JAMF Pro mobile devices or the registration of an AWS ARN with the AWS Backup service.

is_compliant.caption: Compliant Device
is_compliant.description: The event occurred on a compliant device.

is_managed.caption: Managed Device
is_managed.description: The event occurred on a managed device.

is_mobile_account_active.caption: Mobile Account Active
is_mobile_account_active.description: Indicates whether the device has an active mobile account. For example, this is indicated by the itunesStoreAccountActive value within JAMF Pro mobile devices.

is_personal.caption: Personal Device
is_personal.description: The event occurred on a personal device.

is_shared.caption: Shared Device
is_shared.description: The event occurred on a shared device.

is_supervised.caption: Supervised Device
is_supervised.description: The event occurred on a supervised device. Devices that are supervised are typically mobile devices managed by a Mobile Device Management solution and are restricted from specific behaviors such as Apple AirDrop.

is_trusted.caption: Trusted Device
is_trusted.description: The event occurred on a trusted device.

last_seen_time.caption: Last Seen
last_seen_time.description: The most recent discovery time of the device.

meid.caption: MEID
meid.description: The Mobile Equipment Identifier. It's a unique number that identifies a Code Division Multiple Access (CDMA) mobile device.

model.caption: Model
model.description: The model of the device. For example ThinkPad X1 Carbon.

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The time when the device was last known to have been modified.

network_interfaces.caption: Network Interfaces
network_interfaces.description:
The physical or virtual network interfaces that are associated with the device, one for each unique MAC address/IP address/hostname/name combination.

Note: The first element of the array is the network information that pertains to the event.

org.caption: Organization
org.description: Organization and org unit related to the device.

os_machine_uuid.caption: OS Machine UUID
os_machine_uuid.description: The operating system assigned Machine ID. In Windows, this is the value stored at the registry path: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid. In Linux, this is stored in the file: /etc/machine-id.

region.caption: Region
region.description: The region where the virtual machine is located. For example, an AWS Region.

risk_level.caption: Risk Level
risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The risk level is not mapped. See the risk_level attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

risk_score.caption: Risk Score
risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

udid.caption: Unique Device Identifier
udid.description: The Apple assigned Unique Device Identifier (UDID). For iOS, iPadOS, tvOS, watchOS and visionOS devices, this is the UDID. For macOS devices, it is the Provisioning UDID. For example: 00008020-008D4548007B4F26

uid_alt.caption: Alternate ID
uid_alt.description: An alternate unique identifier of the device if any. For example the ActiveDirectory DN.

vendor_name.caption: Vendor Name
vendor_name.description: The vendor for the device. For example Dell or Lenovo.

boot_time_dt.caption: Boot Time
boot_time_dt.description: The time the system was booted.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time when the device was known to have been created.

first_seen_time_dt.caption: First Seen
first_seen_time_dt.description: The initial discovery time of the device.

last_seen_time_dt.caption: Last Seen
last_seen_time_dt.description: The most recent discovery time of the device.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The time when the device was last known to have been modified.

endpoint.caption: Endpoint
endpoint.description: The Endpoint object describes a physical or virtual device that connects to and exchanges information with a computer network. Some examples of endpoints are mobile devices, desktop computers, virtual machines, embedded devices, and servers. Internet-of-Things devices—like cameras, lighting, refrigerators, security systems, smart speakers, and thermostats—are also endpoints.
endpoint.name: endpoint

name.caption: Name
name.description: The short name of the endpoint.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the endpoint.

container.caption: Container
container.description: The information describing an instance of a container. A container is a prepackaged, portable system image that runs isolated on an existing system using a container runtime like containerd.

namespace_pid.caption: Namespace PID
namespace_pid.description: If running under a process namespace (such as in a container), the process identifier within that process namespace.

agent_list.caption: Agent List
agent_list.description: A list of agent objects associated with a device, endpoint, or resource.

domain.caption: Domain
domain.description: The name of the domain that the endpoint belongs to or that corresponds to the endpoint.

hostname.caption: Hostname
hostname.description: The fully qualified name of the endpoint.

instance_uid.caption: Instance ID
instance_uid.description: The unique identifier of a VM instance.

interface_name.caption: Network Interface Name
interface_name.description: The name of the network interface (e.g. eth2).

interface_uid.caption: Network Interface ID
interface_uid.description: The unique identifier of the network interface.

ip.caption: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address of the endpoint, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.

location.caption: Geo Location
location.description: The geographical location of the endpoint.

mac.caption: MAC Address
mac.description: The Media Access Control (MAC) address of the endpoint.

mac_vendor.caption: MAC Vendor
mac_vendor.description: The vendor or manufacturer of the endpoint's network interface controller (NIC), as identified from the MAC address.

owner.caption: Owner
owner.description: The identity of the service or user account that owns the endpoint or was last logged into it.

pool.caption: Pool
pool.description: The pool of desktops or virtual machines to which the endpoint belongs.

subnet_uid.caption: Subnet UID
subnet_uid.description: The unique identifier of a virtual subnet.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The endpoint type. For example: unknown, server, desktop, laptop, tablet, mobile, virtual, browser, or other.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The endpoint type ID.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Server
1.description: A server.

2.caption: Desktop
2.description: A desktop computer.

3.caption: Laptop
3.description: A laptop computer.

4.caption: Tablet
4.description: A tablet computer.

5.caption: Mobile
5.description: A mobile phone.

6.caption: Virtual
6.description: A virtual machine.

7.caption: IOT
7.description: An IOT (Internet of Things) device.

8.caption: Browser
8.description: A web browser.

9.caption: Firewall
9.description: A networking firewall.

10.caption: Switch
10.description: A networking switch.

11.caption: Hub
11.description: A networking hub.

12.caption: Router
12.description: A networking router.

13.caption: IDS
13.description: An intrusion detection system.

14.caption: IPS
14.description: An intrusion prevention system.

15.caption: Load Balancer
15.description: A Load Balancer device.

vpc_uid.caption: VPC UID
vpc_uid.description: The unique identifier of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

zone.caption: Network Zone
zone.description: The network zone or LAN segment.

data_classification.caption: Data Classification
data_classification.description: The Data Classification object includes information about data classification levels and data category types.
data_classification.name: data_classification

category.caption: Category
category.description: The name of the data classification category that data matched into, e.g. Financial, Personal, Governmental, etc.

category_id.caption: Category ID
category_id.description: The normalized identifier of the data classification category.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The type is not mapped. See the data_type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Personal
1.description: Any Personally Identifiable Information (PII), Electronic Personal Health Information (ePHI), or similarly personal information. E.g., full name, home address, date of birth, etc.

2.caption: Governmental
2.description: Any sensitive government identification number related to a person or other classified material. E.g., Passport numbers, driver license numbers, business identification, taxation identifiers, etc.

3.caption: Financial
3.description: Any financially-related sensitive information or Cardholder Data (CHD). E.g., banking account numbers, credit card numbers, International Banking Account Numbers (IBAN), SWIFT codes, etc.

4.caption: Business
4.description: Any business-specific sensitive data such as intellectual property, trademarks, copyrights, human resource data, Board of Directors meeting minutes, and similar.

5.caption: Military and Law Enforcement
5.description: Any mission-specific sensitive data for military, law enforcement, or other government agencies such as specifically classified data, weapon systems information, or other planning data.

6.caption: Security
6.description: Any sensitive security-related data such as passwords, passkeys, IP addresses, API keys, credentials and similar secrets. E.g., AWS Access Secret Key, SaaS API Keys, user passwords, database credentials, etc.

99.caption: Other
99.description: Any other type of data classification or a multi-variate classification made up of several other classification categories.

classifier_details.caption: Classifier Details
classifier_details.description: Describes details about the classifier used for data classification.

confidentiality.caption: Confidentiality
confidentiality.description: The file content confidentiality, normalized to the confidentiality_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidentiality_id.caption: Confidentiality ID
confidentiality_id.description: The normalized identifier of the file content confidentiality indicator.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The confidentiality is not mapped. See the confidentiality attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

discovery_details.caption: Discovery Details
discovery_details.description: Details about the data discovered by classification job.

policy.caption: Policy
policy.description: Details about the data policy that governs data handling and security measures related to classification.

src_url.caption: Source URL
src_url.description: The source URL pointing towards the full classification job details.

status.caption: Status
status.description: The resultant status of the classification job normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_details.caption: Status Details
status_details.description: The contextual description of the status, status_id value.

status_id.caption: Status ID
status_id.description: The normalized status identifier of the classification job.

1.caption: Complete
1.description: The classification job completed for the evaluated resource.

2.caption: Partial
2.description: The classification job partially completed for the evaluated resource.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The classification job type id is not mapped.

3.caption: Fail
3.description: The classification job failed for the evaluated resource.

total.caption: Total
total.description: The total count of discovered entities, by the classification job.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the classification job.

cloud.caption: Cloud
cloud.description: The Cloud object describes the cloud computing environment where an event or finding originated. It provides comprehensive context about the cloud infrastructure, including the cloud service provider, account or subscription details, organizational structure, geographic regions, availability zones, and logical partitions.
cloud.name: cloud

account.caption: Account
account.description:
The Account object containing details about the cloud account, subscription, or billing unit where the event or finding was created. This object includes properties such as the account name, unique identifier, type, labels, and tags.

Examples:
  • AWS: Account object with name, uid (Account ID), type, and other account properties
  • Azure: Subscription object with name, uid (Subscription ID), type, and subscription metadata
  • GCP: Project object with name, uid (Project ID), type, and project attributes
  • Oracle Cloud: Compartment object with name, uid (Tenancy OCID), type, and compartment details

cloud_partition.caption: Cloud Partition
cloud_partition.description:
The logical grouping or isolated segment within a cloud provider's infrastructure where the event or finding was created, often used for compliance, governance, or regional separation.

Examples:
  • AWS: Partition where the event occurred (aws, aws-cn, aws-us-gov)
  • Azure: Cloud environment where the event occurred (AzureCloud, AzureUSGovernment, AzureChinaCloud)

org.caption: Organization
org.description:
The Organization object containing details about the organizational unit or management structure that governs the account, subscription, or project where the event or finding was created. This object includes properties such as the organization name, unique identifier, type, and other organizational metadata.

Examples:
  • AWS: Organization object with name, uid (Organization ID), type, and other organizational properties
  • Azure: Management Group object with name, uid (Management Group ID), type, and management group metadata
  • GCP: Organization object with name, uid (Organization ID), type, and organizational attributes
  • Oracle Cloud: Tenancy object with name, uid (Tenancy OCID), type, and tenancy details

project_uid.caption: Project ID
project_uid.description: The unique identifier of a Cloud project.

provider.caption: Cloud Provider
provider.description: The unique name of the Cloud services provider where the event or finding was created. Examples include AWS, Azure, GCP (Google Cloud Platform), Oracle Cloud, IBM Cloud, Alibaba Cloud, or other public, private, or hybrid cloud providers.

region.caption: Region
region.description:
The cloud region where the event or finding was created, as defined by the cloud provider.

Examples:
  • AWS: Region where the event occurred (us-east-1, eu-west-1)
  • Azure: Region where the event occurred (East US, West Europe)
  • GCP: Region where the event occurred (us-central1, europe-west1)
  • Oracle Cloud: Region where the event occurred (us-ashburn-1, uk-london-1)

zone.caption: Cloud Availability Zone
zone.description:
The availability zone in the cloud region where the event or finding was created, as defined by the cloud provider.

Examples:
  • AWS: Availability zone where the event occurred (us-east-1a, us-east-1b)
  • Azure: Availability zone where the event occurred (1, 2, 3 within a region)
  • GCP: Availability zone where the event occurred (us-central1-a, us-central1-b)
  • Oracle Cloud: Availability zone where the event occurred (AD-1, AD-2, AD-3)

file.caption: File
file.description: The File object represents the metadata associated with a file stored in a computer system. It encompasses information about the file itself, including its attributes, properties, and organizational details.
file.name: file

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the file. For example: svchost.exe

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the file as defined by the storage system, such the file system file ID.

data_classification.caption: Data Classification
data_classification.description: The Data Classification object includes information about data classification levels and data category types.

data_classifications.caption: Data Classification
data_classifications.description: A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

accessed_time.caption: Accessed Time
accessed_time.description: The time when the file was last accessed.

accessor.caption: Accessor
accessor.description: The name of the user who last accessed the object.

attributes.caption: Attributes
attributes.description: The bitmask value that represents the file attributes.

company_name.caption: Company Name
company_name.description: The name of the company that published the file. For example: Microsoft Corporation.

confidentiality.caption: Confidentiality
confidentiality.description: The file content confidentiality, normalized to the confidentiality_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

confidentiality_id.caption: Confidentiality ID
confidentiality_id.description: The normalized identifier of the file content confidentiality indicator.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The confidentiality is not mapped. See the confidentiality attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time when the file was created.

creator.caption: Creator
creator.description: The user that created the file.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The description of the file, as returned by file system. For example: the description as returned by the Unix file command or the Windows file type.

drive_type.caption: Drive Type
drive_type.description: The drive type, normalized to the caption of the drive_type_id value. In the case of Other, it is defined by the source.

drive_type_id.caption: Drive Type ID
drive_type_id.description: Identifies the type of a disk drive, i.e. fixed, removable, etc.

1.caption: Removable
1.description: The drive has removable media; for example, a floppy drive, thumb drive, or flash card reader.

2.caption: Fixed
2.description: The drive has fixed media; for example, a hard disk drive or flash drive.

3.caption: Remote
3.description: The drive is a remote (network) drive.

4.caption: CD-ROM
4.description: The drive is a CD-ROM drive.

5.caption: RAM Disk
5.description: The drive is a RAM disk.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The drive type is not mapped. See the drive_type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

encryption_details.caption: Encryption Details
encryption_details.description: The encryption details of the file. Should be populated if the file is encrypted.

ext.caption: File Extension
ext.description: The extension of the file, excluding the leading dot. For example: exe from svchost.exe, or gz from export.tar.gz.

imported_symbols.caption: Imported Symbols
imported_symbols.description: A list of symbols imported by the executable file.

internal_name.caption: Internal Name
internal_name.description: The name of the file as identified within the file itself. This contrasts with the name by which the file is known on disk. Where available, the internal name is widely used by security practitioners and detection content because the on-disk file name is not reliable. On the Windows OS, most PE files contain a VERSIONINFO resource from which the internal name can be obtained. On macOS, binaries can optionally embed a copy of the application's Info.plist file which in turn contains the name of the executable.

is_deleted.caption: Deleted
is_deleted.description: Indicates if the file was deleted from the filesystem.

is_encrypted.caption: Encrypted
is_encrypted.description: Indicates if the file is encrypted.

is_public.caption: Public
is_public.description: Indicates if the file is publicly accessible. For example in an object's public access in AWS S3

is_readonly.caption: Read-Only
is_readonly.description: Indicates that the file cannot be modified.

is_system.caption: System
is_system.description: The indication of whether the object is part of the operating system.

mime_type.caption: MIME type
mime_type.description: The Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (MIME) type of the file, if applicable.

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The time when the file was last modified.

modifier.caption: Modifier
modifier.description: The user that last modified the file.

owner.caption: Owner
owner.description: The user that owns the file/object.

parent_folder.caption: Parent Folder
parent_folder.description: The parent folder in which the file resides. For example: c:\windows\system32

path.caption: Path
path.description: The full path to the file. For example: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe.

product.caption: Product
product.description: The product that created or installed the file.

signature.caption: Digital Signature
signature.description: The digital signature of the file.

size.caption: Size
size.description: The size of data, in bytes.

storage_class.caption: Storage Class
storage_class.description: The storage class of the file. For example in AWS S3: STANDARD, STANDARD_IA, GLACIER.

tags.caption: Tags
tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the file.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The file type.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The file type ID. Note the distinction between a Regular File and an Executable File. If the distinction is not known, or not indicated by the log, use Regular File. In this case, it should not be assumed that a Regular File is not executable.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

uri.caption: File URI
uri.description: The file URI, such as those reporting by static analysis tools. E.g., file:///C:/dev/sarif/sarif-tutorials/samples/Introduction/simple-example.js

url.caption: URL
url.description: The URL of the file, when applicable.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The file version. For example: 8.0.7601.17514.

volume.caption: Volume
volume.description: The volume on the storage device where the file is located.

xattributes.caption: Extended Attributes
xattributes.description:
An unordered collection of zero or more name/value pairs where each pair represents a file or folder extended attribute.

For example: Windows alternate data stream attributes (ADS stream name, ADS size, etc.), user-defined or application-defined attributes, ACL, owner, primary group, etc. Examples from DCS:

  • ads_name
  • ads_size
  • dacl
  • owner
  • primary_group
  • link_name - name of the link associated to the file.
  • hard_link_count - the number of links that are associated to the file.

accessed_time_dt.caption: Accessed Time
accessed_time_dt.description: The time when the file was last accessed.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time when the file was created.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The time when the file was last modified.

actor.caption: Actor
actor.description: The Actor object contains details about the user, role, application, service, or process that initiated or performed a specific activity. Note that Actor is not the threat actor of a campaign but may be part of a campaign.
actor.name: actor

app_name.caption: Application Name
app_name.description: The client application or service that initiated the activity. This can be in conjunction with the user if present. Note that app_name is distinct from the process if present.

app_uid.caption: Application ID
app_uid.description: The unique identifier of the client application or service that initiated the activity. This can be in conjunction with the user if present. Note that app_name is distinct from the process.pid or process.uid if present.

authorizations.caption: Authorization Information
authorizations.description: Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any associated policies related to the activity/event.

idp.caption: Identity Provider
idp.description: This object describes details about the Identity Provider used.

invoked_by.caption: Invoked by
invoked_by.description: The name of the service that invoked the activity as described in the event.

process.caption: Process
process.description: The process that initiated the activity.

session.caption: Session
session.description: The user session from which the activity was initiated.

user.caption: User
user.description: The user that initiated the activity or the user context from which the activity was initiated.

sbom.caption: Software Bill of Materials
sbom.description: The Software Bill of Materials object describes characteristics of a generated SBOM.
sbom.name: sbom

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time when the SBOM was created.

package.caption: Software Package
package.description: The software package or library that is being discovered or inventoried by an SBOM.

product.caption: Product
product.description: Details about the upstream product that generated the SBOM e.g. cdxgen or Syft.

software_components.caption: Software Components
software_components.description: The list of software components used in the software package.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of SBOM, normalized to the caption of the type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The type of SBOM.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: SPDX
1.description: System Package Data Exchange (SPDX®) is an open standard capable of representing systems with software components in as SBOMs (Software Bill of Materials) and other AI, data and security references supporting a range of risk management use cases. The SPDX specification is a freely available international open standard (ISO/IEC 5692:2021).

2.caption: CycloneDX
2.description: CycloneDX is an International Standard for Bill of Materials (ECMA-424).

3.caption: SWID
3.description: The International Organization for Standardization (ISO) and the International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) publishes, ISO/IEC 19770-2, a standard for software identification (SWID) tags that defines a structured metadata format for describing a software product. A SWID tag document is composed of a structured set of data elements that identify the software product

uid.caption: SBOM ID
uid.description: A unique identifier for the SBOM or the SBOM generation by a source tool, such as the SPDX metadata.component.bom-ref.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The specification (spec) version of the particular SBOM, e.g., 1.6.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time when the SBOM was created.

trait.caption: Trait
trait.description: Describes a characteristic or feature of an entity that was observed. For example, this object can be used to represent specific characteristics derived from events or findings that can be surfaced as distinguishing traits of the entity in question.
trait.name: trait

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the trait.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the trait.

category.caption: Category
category.description: The high-level grouping or classification this trait belongs to.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of the trait. For example, this can be used to indicate if the trait acts as a contributing factor (increases risk/severity) or a mitigating factor (decreases risk/severity), in the context of the related finding.

analysis_target.caption: Analysis Target
analysis_target.description: The analysis target defines the scope of monitored activities, specifying what entity, system or process is analyzed for activity patterns.
analysis_target.name: analysis_target

name.caption: Name
name.description: The specific name or identifier of the analysis target, such as the username of a User Account, the name of a Kubernetes Cluster, the identifier of a Network Namespace, or the name of an Application Component.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The category of the analysis target, such as User Account, Kubernetes Cluster, Network Namespace, or Application Component.

service.caption: Service
service.description: The Service object describes characteristics of a service, e.g. AWS EC2.
service.name: service

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the service.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the service.

labels.caption: Labels
labels.description: The list of labels associated with the service.

tags.caption: Tags
tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the service.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the service.

privilege_attack_info.caption: Privilege Attack Info
privilege_attack_info.description: The Privilege Attack Info object groups privileges by the potential attack they could enable. It maps specific privileges to MITRE ATT&CK techniques, helping identify security risks associated with granted permissions.
privilege_attack_info.name: privilege_attack_info

attack.caption: MITRE ATT&CK® Details
attack.description: The MITRE ATT&CK technique that these privileges could enable.

privilege_info_list.caption: Privilege Info List
privilege_info_list.description: The list of privilege information objects, where each element describes a specific privilege that could enable the associated attack technique.

epss.caption: EPSS
epss.description: The Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) object describes the estimated probability a vulnerability will be exploited. EPSS is a community-driven effort to combine descriptive information about vulnerabilities (CVEs) with evidence of actual exploitation in-the-wild. (EPSS).
epss.name: epss

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The timestamp indicating when the EPSS score was calculated.

percentile.caption: EPSS Percentile
percentile.description: The EPSS score's percentile representing relative importance and ranking of the score in the larger EPSS dataset.

score.caption: EPPS Score
score.description: The EPSS score representing the probability [0-1] of exploitation in the wild in the next 30 days (following score publication).

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the EPSS model used to calculate the score.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The timestamp indicating when the EPSS score was calculated.

metadata.caption: Metadata
metadata.description: The Metadata object describes the metadata associated with the event.
metadata.name: metadata

data_classification.caption: Data Classification
data_classification.description: The Data Classification object includes information about data classification levels and data category types.

data_classifications.caption: Data Classification
data_classifications.description: A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

correlation_uid.caption: Correlation UID
correlation_uid.description: A unique identifier used to correlate this OCSF event with other related OCSF events, distinct from the event's uid value. This enables linking multiple OCSF events that are part of the same activity, transaction, or security incident across different systems or time periods.

debug.caption: Debug Information
debug.description: Debug information about non-fatal issues with this OCSF event. Each issue is a line in this string array.

event_code.caption: Event Code
event_code.description: The identifier of the original event. For example the numerical Windows Event Code or Cisco syslog code.

extension.caption: Schema Extension
extension.description: The schema extension used to create the event.

extensions.caption: Schema Extensions
extensions.description: The schema extensions used to create the event.

is_truncated.caption: Is Truncated
is_truncated.description: Indicates whether the OCSF event data has been truncated due to size limitations. When true, some event data may have been omitted to fit within system constraints.

labels.caption: Labels
labels.description: The list of labels attached to the event. For example: ["sample", "dev"]

log_format.caption: Log Source Format
log_format.description: The format of data in the log where the data originated. For example CSV, XML, Windows Multiline, JSON, syslog or Cisco Log Schema.

log_level.caption: Log Level
log_level.description: The level at which an event was logged. This can be log provider specific. For example the audit level.

log_name.caption: Log Name
log_name.description: The event log name, typically for the consumer of the event. For example, the storage bucket name, SIEM repository index name, etc.

log_provider.caption: Log Provider
log_provider.description: The logging provider or logging service that logged the event. For example AWS CloudWatch or Splunk.

log_source.caption: Log Source
log_source.description: The log system or component where the data originated. For example, a file path, syslog server name or a Windows hostname and logging subsystem such as Security.

log_version.caption: Log Version
log_version.description: The event log schema version of the original event. For example the syslog version or the Cisco Log Schema version

logged_time.caption: Logged Time
logged_time.description:
The time when the logging system collected and logged the event.

This attribute is distinct from the event time in that event time typically contain the time extracted from the original event. Most of the time, these two times will be different.

loggers.caption: Loggers
loggers.description: An array of Logger objects that describe the pipeline of devices and logging products between the event source and its eventual destination. Note, this attribute can be used when there is a complex end-to-end path of event flow and/or to track the chain of custody of the data.

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The time when the event was last modified or enriched.

original_event_uid.caption: Original Event ID
original_event_uid.description: The unique identifier assigned to the event in its original logging system before transformation to OCSF format. This field preserves the source system's native event identifier, enabling traceability back to the raw log entry. For example, a Windows Event Record ID, a syslog message ID, a Splunk _cd value, or a database transaction log sequence number.

original_time.caption: Original Time
original_time.description: The original event time as reported by the event source. For example, the time in the original format from system event log such as Syslog on Unix/Linux and the System event file on Windows. Omit if event is generated instead of collected via logs.

processed_time.caption: Processed Time
processed_time.description: The event processed time, such as an ETL operation.

product.caption: Product
product.description: The product that reported the event.

profiles.caption: Profiles
profiles.description: The list of profiles used to create the event. Profiles should be referenced by their name attribute for core profiles, or extension/name for profiles from extensions.

reporter.caption: Reporter
reporter.description: The entity from which the event or finding was first reported.

sequence.caption: Sequence Number
sequence.description: Sequence number of the event. The sequence number is a value available in some events, to make the exact ordering of events unambiguous, regardless of the event time precision.

source.caption: Source
source.description: The source of the event or finding. This can be any distinguishing name for the logical origin of the data — for example, 'CloudTrail Events', or a use case like 'Attack Simulations' or 'Vulnerability Scans'.

tags.caption: Tags
tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the event.

total_queued_duration.caption: Total Queued Duration
total_queued_duration.description: The amount of time an event spent in a queue awaiting processing. In this case, the value is the difference between processed_time and logged_time. This duration is inclusive of all queues between the originator of the event and the intended long-term storage destination of the event.

transformation_info_list.caption: Transformation Info
transformation_info_list.description: An array of transformation info that describes the mappings or transforms applied to the data.

transmit_time.caption: Transmission Time
transmit_time.description: The time when the event was transmitted from the logging device to it's next destination.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of the event or finding as a subset of the source of the event. This can be any distinguishing characteristic of the data. For example 'Management Events' or 'Device Penetration Test'.

uid.caption: Event UID
uid.description: A unique identifier assigned to the OCSF event. This ID is specific to the OCSF event itself and is distinct from the original event identifier in the source system (see original_event_uid).

untruncated_size.caption: Untruncated Size
untruncated_size.description: The original size of the OCSF event data in kilobytes before any truncation occurred. This field is typically populated when is_truncated is true to indicate the full size of the original event.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the OCSF schema, using Semantic Versioning Specification (SemVer). For example: 1.0.0. Event consumers use the version to determine the available event attributes.

logged_time_dt.caption: Logged Time
logged_time_dt.description:
The time when the logging system collected and logged the event.

This attribute is distinct from the event time in that event time typically contain the time extracted from the original event. Most of the time, these two times will be different.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The time when the event was last modified or enriched.

processed_time_dt.caption: Processed Time
processed_time_dt.description: The event processed time, such as an ETL operation.

transmit_time_dt.caption: Transmission Time
transmit_time_dt.description: The time when the event was transmitted from the logging device to it's next destination.

sso.caption: SSO
sso.description: The Single Sign-On (SSO) object provides a structure for normalizing SSO attributes, configuration, and/or settings from Identity Providers.
sso.name: sso

auth_protocol.caption: Auth Protocol
auth_protocol.description: The authorization protocol as defined by the caption of auth_protocol_id. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

auth_protocol_id.caption: Auth Protocol ID
auth_protocol_id.description: The normalized identifier of the authentication protocol used by the SSO resource.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The authentication protocol is not mapped. See the auth_protocol attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

certificate.caption: SAML Certificate
certificate.description: Digital Signature associated with the SSO resource, e.g., SAML X.509 certificate details.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: When the SSO resource was created.

duration_mins.caption: SSO Session Duration
duration_mins.description: The duration (in minutes) for an SSO session, after which re-authentication is required.

idle_timeout.caption: SSO Idle Timeout
idle_timeout.description: Duration (in minutes) of allowed inactivity before Single Sign-On (SSO) session expiration.

login_endpoint.caption: SSO Login Endpoint
login_endpoint.description: URL for initiating an SSO login request.

logout_endpoint.caption: SSO Logout Endpoint
logout_endpoint.description: URL for initiating an SSO logout request, allowing sessions to be terminated across applications.

metadata_endpoint.caption: SSO Metadata Endpoint
metadata_endpoint.description: URL where metadata about the SSO configuration is available (e.g., for SAML configurations).

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The most recent time when the SSO resource was updated.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the SSO resource.

protocol_name.caption: Supported Protocol
protocol_name.description: The supported protocol for the SSO resource. E.g., SAML or OIDC.

scopes.caption: Scopes
scopes.description: Scopes define the specific permissions or actions that the client is allowed to perform on behalf of the user. Each scope represents a different set of permissions, and the user can selectively grant or deny access to specific scopes during the authorization process.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: A unique identifier for a SSO resource.

vendor_name.caption: Service Provider
vendor_name.description: Name of the vendor or service provider implementing SSO. E.g., Okta, Auth0, Microsoft.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: When the SSO resource was created.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The most recent time when the SSO resource was updated.

kernel_driver.caption: Kernel Extension
kernel_driver.description: The Kernel Extension object describes a kernel driver that has been loaded or unloaded into the operating system (OS) kernel.
kernel_driver.name: kernel_driver

file.caption: File
file.description: The driver/extension file object.

threat_actor.caption: Threat Actor
threat_actor.description: Threat actor is responsible for the observed malicious activity.
threat_actor.name: threat_actor

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the threat actor.

type.caption: Threat Actor Type
type.description: The classification of the threat actor based on their motivations, capabilities, or affiliations. Common types include nation-state actors, cybercriminal groups, hacktivists, or insider threats.

type_id.caption: Threat Actor Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized datastore resource type identifier.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The threat actor type is unknown.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The threat actor type is not mapped.

fingerprint.caption: Fingerprint
fingerprint.description: The Fingerprint object provides detailed information about a fingerprint, which is a compact representation of data used to identify a longer piece of information, such as a public key, file content, or application implementation. It contains the algorithm or scheme and value of the fingerprint, enabling efficient and reliable identification of the associated data.
fingerprint.name: fingerprint

algorithm.caption: Algorithm
algorithm.description: The algorithm or scheme used to create the fingerprint, normalized to the caption of algorithm_id. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

algorithm_id.caption: Algorithm ID
algorithm_id.description: The identifier of the normalized algorithm or scheme, which was used to create the fingerprint.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The algorithm is not mapped. See the algorithm attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: MD5
1.description: MD5 message-digest algorithm producing a 128-bit (16-byte) hash value.

2.caption: SHA-1
2.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 1 producing a 160-bit (20-byte) hash value.

3.caption: SHA-256
3.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 256-bit (32-byte) hash value.

4.caption: SHA-512
4.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 512-bit (64-byte) hash value.

5.caption: CTPH
5.description: The ssdeep generated fuzzy checksum. Also known as Context Triggered Piecewise Hash (CTPH).

6.caption: TLSH
6.description: The TLSH fuzzy hashing algorithm.

7.caption: quickXorHash
7.description: Microsoft simple non-cryptographic hash algorithm that works by XORing the bytes in a circular-shifting fashion.

8.caption: SHA-224
8.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 224-bit (28-byte) hash value.

9.caption: SHA-384
9.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 384-bit (48-byte) hash value.

10.caption: SHA-512/224
10.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 512-bit (64-byte) hash value truncated to a 224-bit (28-byte) hash value.

11.caption: SHA-512/256
11.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 512-bit (64-byte) hash value truncated to a 256-bit (32-byte) hash value.

12.caption: SHA3-224
12.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 3 producing a 224-bit (28-byte) hash value.

13.caption: SHA3-256
13.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 3 producing a 256-bit (32-byte) hash value.

14.caption: SHA3-384
14.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 3 producing a 384-bit (48-byte) hash value.

15.caption: SHA3-512
15.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 3 producing a 512-bit (64-byte) hash value.

16.caption: xxHash H3 64-bit
16.description: xxHash H3 producing a 64-bit hash value.

17.caption: xxHash H3 128-bit
17.description: xxHash H3 producing a 128-bit hash value.

18.caption: Imphash
18.description: Import hash (imphash) based on the import table of a Portable Executable (PE) file producing a 128-bit (16-byte) hash value.

19.caption: NPF
19.description: Network Protocol Fingerprint (NPF) used to identify network protocols and applications.

20.caption: HASSH
20.description: HASSH is a network fingerprinting standard which can be used to identify specific SSH client and server implementations.

value.caption: Value
value.description:
The fingerprint value.

Note: This uses type file_hash_t ("Hash"), which has been generalized for all fingerprints but retains the same name and caption for backwards compatibility.

agent.caption: Agent
agent.description: An Agent (also known as a Sensor) is typically installed on an Operating System (OS) and serves as a specialized software component that can be designed to monitor, detect, collect, archive, or take action. These activities and possible actions are defined by the upstream system controlling the Agent and its intended purpose. For instance, an Agent can include Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR) agents, backup/disaster recovery sensors, Application Performance Monitoring or profiling sensors, and similar software.
agent.name: agent

name.caption: Agent Name
name.description: The name of the agent or sensor. For example: AWS SSM Agent.

policies.caption: Agent Policies
policies.description: Describes the various policies that may be applied or enforced by an agent or sensor. E.g., Conditional Access, prevention, auto-update, tamper protection, destination configuration, etc.

type.caption: Agent Type
type.description: The normalized caption of the type_id value for the agent or sensor. In the case of 'Other' or 'Unknown', it is defined by the event source.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized representation of an agent or sensor. E.g., EDR, vulnerability management, APM, backup & recovery, etc.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Endpoint Detection and Response
1.description: Any EDR sensor or agent. Or any tool that provides similar threat detection, anti-malware, anti-ransomware, or similar capabilities. E.g., Crowdstrike Falcon, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, Wazuh.

2.caption: Data Loss Prevention
2.description: Any DLP sensor or agent. Or any tool that provides similar data classification, data loss detection, and/or data loss prevention capabilities. E.g., Forcepoint DLP, Microsoft Purview, Symantec DLP.

3.caption: Backup & Recovery
3.description: Any agent or sensor that provides backups, archival, or recovery capabilities. E.g., Azure Backup, AWS Backint Agent.

4.caption: Performance Monitoring & Observability
4.description: Any agent or sensor that provides Application Performance Monitoring (APM), active tracing, profiling, or other observability use cases and optionally forwards the logs. E.g., New Relic Agent, Datadog Agent, Azure Monitor Agent.

5.caption: Vulnerability Management
5.description: Any agent or sensor that provides vulnerability management or scanning capabilities. E.g., Qualys VMDR, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, Crowdstrike Spotlight, Amazon Inspector Agent.

6.caption: Log Forwarding
6.description: Any agent or sensor that forwards logs to a 3rd party storage system such as a data lake or SIEM. E.g., Splunk Universal Forwarder, Tenzir, FluentBit, Amazon CloudWatch Agent, Amazon Kinesis Agent.

7.caption: Mobile Device Management
7.description: Any agent or sensor responsible for providing Mobile Device Management (MDM) or Mobile Enterprise Management (MEM) capabilities. E.g., JumpCloud Agent, Esper Agent, Jamf Pro binary.

8.caption: Configuration Management
8.description: Any agent or sensor that provides configuration management of a device, such as scanning for software, license management, or applying configurations. E.g., AWS Systems Manager Agent, Flexera, ServiceNow MID Server.

9.caption: Remote Access
9.description: Any agent or sensor that provides remote access capabilities to a device. E.g., BeyondTrust, Amazon Systems Manager Agent, Verkada Agent.

uid.caption: Agent ID
uid.description: The UID of the agent or sensor, sometimes known as a Sensor ID or aid.

uid_alt.caption: Alternate Agent ID
uid_alt.description: An alternative or contextual identifier for the agent or sensor, such as a configuration, organization, or license UID.

vendor_name.caption: Vendor Name
vendor_name.description: The company or author who created the agent or sensor. For example: Crowdstrike.

version.caption: Agent Version
version.description: The semantic version of the agent or sensor, e.g., 7.101.50.0.

autonomous_system.caption: Autonomous System
autonomous_system.description: An autonomous system (AS) is a collection of connected Internet Protocol (IP) routing prefixes under the control of one or more network operators on behalf of a single administrative entity or domain that presents a common, clearly defined routing policy to the internet.
autonomous_system.name: autonomous_system

name.caption: Name
name.description: Organization name for the Autonomous System.

number.caption: Number
number.description: Unique number that the AS is identified by.

mitigation.caption: MITRE Mitigation
mitigation.description: The MITRE Mitigation object describes the ATT&CK® or ATLAS™ Mitigation ID and/or name that is associated to an attack.
mitigation.name: mitigation

name.caption: Name
name.description: The Mitigation name that is associated with the attack technique. For example: Password Policies, or Code Signing.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The Mitigation ID that is associated with the attack technique. For example: M1027, or AML.M0013.

countermeasures.caption: Countermeasures
countermeasures.description: The D3FEND countermeasures that are associated with the attack technique. For example: ATT&CK Technique T1003 is addressed by Mitigation M1027, and D3FEND Technique D3-OTP.

src_url.caption: Source URL
src_url.description: The versioned permalink of the Mitigation. For example: https://attack.mitre.org/versions/v14/mitigations/M1027.

references.description: ATT&CK® Mitigation to D3FEND™ Technique Mappings

network_connection_info.caption: Network Connection Information
