version.description: The CIS Control version. For example: v8.

gpu_info.caption: GPU Information
gpu_info.description: The GPU information object contains attributes describing graphical processing hardware.
gpu_info.name: gpu_info

bus_type.caption: Bus Type
bus_type.description: The attachment bus or interface standard, normalized to the caption of the bus_type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

bus_type_id.caption: Bus Type ID
bus_type_id.description: The normalized identifier of the attachment bus or interface standard.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The bus type is unknown or not reported.

1.caption: Onboard
1.description: The device is attached directly on the motherboard or SoC (often referred to as 'integrated' or 'onboard').

2.caption: PCIe x16
2.description: Peripheral Component Interconnect Express slot with 16 lanes, typically used for high‑bandwidth add‑in devices.

3.caption: PCIe x8
3.description: Peripheral Component Interconnect Express slot with 8 lanes; common for high‑performance NICs, storage controllers, and accelerators.

4.caption: MXM Type A
4.description: Mobile PCI Express Module (MXM) Type A form factor (compact laptop/embedded GPU module).

5.caption: MXM Type B
5.description: Mobile PCI Express Module (MXM) Type B form factor (larger laptop/embedded GPU module).

6.caption: M.2
6.description: M.2 (NGFF) internal module form factor/connector exposing PCIe (and/or SATA/USB) for SSDs and add‑in modules.

7.caption: CXL
7.description: Compute Express Link (CXL), an open cache‑coherent interconnect for processors, memory expansion, and accelerators built on the PCIe physical layer.

99.caption: Other
99.description: A bus or interface standard not covered by the defined values; the exact value is reported by the event source.

cores.caption: Cores
cores.description: The number of processing cores or compute units for the component.

model.caption: Model
model.description: The model name of the GPU. For example: GeForce RTX A6000, Radeon PRO W7900, or Intel Data Center GPU Max 1550.

vendor_name.caption: Vendor Name
vendor_name.description: The name of the vendor of the GPU. For example: NVIDIA, AMD, or Intel.

vram_mode.caption: VRAM Mode
vram_mode.description: The video memory attachment mode, indicating how the VRAM hardware is integrated with the system (e.g., shared or dedicated), normalized to the caption of the vram_mode_id value. For 'Other', the exact attachment mode is defined by the event source.

vram_mode_id.caption: VRAM Mode ID
vram_mode_id.description: The normalized identifier of the video memory attachment mode.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The VRAM mode is unknown or not reported.

1.caption: Shared
1.description: Video memory is allocated from system memory.

2.caption: Dedicated
2.description: Video memory is physically separate from system memory.

99.caption: Other
99.description: A VRAM mode not covered by the defined values; the exact value is reported by the event source.

vram_size.caption: VRAM Size
vram_size.description: The total amount of installed video RAM.

email.caption: Email
email.description: The Email object describes the email metadata such as sender, recipients, and direction, and can include embedded URLs and files.
email.name: email

data_classification.caption: Data Classification
data_classification.description: The Data Classification object includes information about data classification levels and data category types.

data_classifications.caption: Data Classification
data_classifications.description: A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

cc.caption: Cc
cc.description: The machine-readable email header Cc values, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com.

cc_mailboxes.caption: Cc Mailboxes
cc_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header Cc Mailbox values. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.

delivered_to.caption: Delivered To
delivered_to.description: The machine-readable Delivered-To email header field. For example example.user@usersdomain.com

delivered_to_list.caption: Delivered To List
delivered_to_list.description: The machine-readable Delivered-To email header values. For example example.user@usersdomain.com

files.caption: Files
files.description: The files embedded or attached to the email.

from.caption: From
from.description: The machine-readable email header From value, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com.

from_list.caption: From List
from_list.description: The machine-readable email header From values. This array should contain the value in from. For example example.user@usersdomain.com.

from_mailbox.caption: From Mailbox
from_mailbox.description: The human-readable email header From Mailbox value. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.

from_mailboxes.caption: From Mailboxes
from_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header From Mailbox values. This array should contain the value in from_mailbox. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.

http_headers.caption: HTTP Headers
http_headers.description: Additional HTTP headers of an HTTP request or response.

is_read.caption: Read
is_read.description: The indication of whether the email has been read.

message_uid.caption: Message UID
message_uid.description: The email header Message-ID value, as defined by RFC 5322.

reply_to.caption: Reply To
reply_to.description: The machine-readable email header Reply-To value, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com

reply_to_list.caption: Reply To List
reply_to_list.description: The machine-readable email header Reply-To values, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com

reply_to_mailboxes.caption: Reply To Mailboxes
reply_to_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header Reply To Mailbox values. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.

return_path.caption: Return Path
return_path.description: The address found in the 'Return-Path' header, which indicates where bounce messages (non-delivery reports) should be sent. This address is often set by the sending system and may differ from the 'From' or 'Sender' addresses. For example, mailer-daemon@senderserver.com.

sender.caption: Sender
sender.description: The machine readable email address of the system or server that actually transmitted the email message, extracted from the email headers per RFC 5322. This differs from the from field, which shows the message author. The sender field is most commonly used when multiple addresses appear in the from_list field, or when the transmitting system is different from the message author (such as when sending on behalf of someone else).

sender_mailbox.caption: Sender Mailbox
sender_mailbox.description: The human readable email address of the system or server that actually transmitted the email message, extracted from the email headers per RFC 5322. This differs from the from_mailbox field, which shows the message author. The sender mailbox field is most commonly used when multiple addresses appear in the from_mailboxes field, or when the transmitting system is different from the message author (such as when sending on behalf of someone else).

size.caption: Size
size.description: The size in bytes of the email, including attachments.

smtp_from.caption: SMTP From
smtp_from.description: The value of the SMTP MAIL FROM command.

smtp_to.caption: SMTP To
smtp_to.description: The value of the SMTP envelope RCPT TO command.

subject.caption: Subject
subject.description: The email header Subject value, as defined by RFC 5322.

to.caption: To
to.description: The machine-readable email header To values, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com

to_mailboxes.caption: To Mailboxes
to_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header To Mailbox values. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.

uid.caption: Email Thread UID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the email thread.

urls.caption: URLs
urls.description: The URLs embedded in the email.

x_originating_ip.caption: X-Originating-IP
x_originating_ip.description: The X-Originating-IP header identifying the emails originating IP address(es).

startup_item.caption: Startup Item
startup_item.description: The startup item object describes an application component that has associated startup criteria and configurations.
startup_item.name: startup_item

driver.caption: Kernel Driver
driver.description: The startup item kernel driver resource.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The unique name of the startup item.

run_mode_ids.caption: Run Mode IDs
run_mode_ids.description: The list of normalized identifiers that describe the startup items' properties when it is running.  Use this field to capture extended information about the process, which may depend on the type of startup item.  E.g., A Windows service that interacts with the desktop.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The run mode is not mapped. See the run_modes attribute, which contains data source specific values.

1.caption: Interactive
1.description: The startup item interacts with the desktop.

2.caption: Own Process
2.description: The startup item runs in its own process.

3.caption: Shared Process
3.description: The startup item runs in a shared process.

run_modes.caption: Run Modes
run_modes.description: The list of run_modes, normalized to the captions of the run_mode_id values.  In the case of 'Other', they are defined by the event source.

run_state.caption: Run State
run_state.description: The run state of the startup item.

run_state_id.caption: Run State ID
run_state_id.description: The run state ID of the startup item.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The run state is not mapped. See the run_state attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Stopped
1.description: The service is not running.

2.caption: Start Pending
2.description: The service is starting.

3.caption: Stop Pending
3.description: The service is stopping.

4.caption: Running
4.description: The service is running.

5.caption: Continue Pending
5.description: The service is pending continue.

6.caption: Pause Pending
6.description: The service is pending pause.

7.caption: Paused
7.description: The service is paused.

8.caption: Restart Pending
8.description: The service is pending restart.

start_type.caption: Start Type
start_type.description: The start type of the startup item.

start_type_id.caption: Start Type ID
start_type_id.description: The start type ID of the startup item.

1.caption: Auto
1.description: Service started automatically during system startup.

2.caption: Boot
2.description: Device driver started by the system loader.

3.caption: On Demand
3.description: Started on demand. For example, by the Windows Service Control Manager when a process calls the StartService function.

4.caption: Disabled
4.description: The service is disabled, and cannot be started.

5.caption: All Logins
5.description: Started on all user logins.

6.caption: Specific User Login
6.description: Started on specific user logins.

7.caption: Scheduled
7.description: Stared according to a schedule.

8.caption: System Changed
8.description: Started when a system item, such as a file or registry key, changes.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The start type is not mapped. See the start_type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The startup item type.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The startup item type identifier.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The startup item type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains data source specific values.

1.caption: Kernel Mode Driver
1.description: Kernel mode driver.

2.caption: User Mode Driver
2.description: User mode driver.

3.caption: Service
3.description: A background process typically managed by the operating system, e.g., a service process on Windows or a systemd-managed daemon on Linux.

4.caption: User Mode Application
4.description: An application that runs in the user space.

5.caption: Autoload
5.description: The macOS Autoload Application.

6.caption: System Extension
6.description: System extensions on macOS enables 3rd parties to extend the capabilities of macOS.

7.caption: Kernel Extension
7.description: Kernel extensions on macOS includes Apple provided pre-installs and 3rd party installs which enables support for specific hardware or software features not natively supported by macOS.

8.caption: Scheduled Job, Task
8.description: A job or task that runs on a configured schedule.

win_service.caption: Windows Service
win_service.description: The startup item Windows service resource.

hassh.caption: HASSH
hassh.description: The HASSH object contains SSH network fingerprinting values for specific client/server implementations. It provides a standardized way of identifying and categorizing SSH connections based on their unique characteristics and behavior.
hassh.name: hassh

algorithm.caption: Algorithm
algorithm.description: The concatenation of key exchange, encryption, authentication and compression algorithms (separated by ';'). NOTE: This is not the underlying algorithm for the hash implementation.

fingerprint.caption: Fingerprint
fingerprint.description: The hash of the key exchange, encryption, authentication and compression algorithms.

parameter.caption: Parameter
parameter.description: The Parameter object provides details regarding a parameter of a a function.
parameter.name: parameter

name.caption: Name
name.description: The parameter name.

post_value.caption: Post-Value
post_value.description: The parameter value after function execution.

pre_value.caption: Pre-Value
pre_value.description: The parameter value before function execution.

process.caption: Process
process.description: The Process object describes a running instance of a launched program.
process.name: process

name.caption: Name
name.description: The friendly name of the process, for example: Notepad++.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: A unique identifier for this process assigned by the producer (tool).  Facilitates correlation of a process event with other events for that process.

cmd_line.caption: Command Line
cmd_line.description: The full command line used to launch an application, service, process, or job. For example: ssh user@10.0.0.10. If the command line is unavailable or missing, the empty string '' is to be used.

cpid.caption: Common Process Identifier
cpid.description: A unique process identifier that can be assigned deterministically by multiple system data producers.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time when the process was created/started.

pid.caption: Process ID
pid.description: The process identifier, as reported by the operating system. Process ID (PID) is a number used by the operating system to uniquely identify an active process.

container.caption: Container
container.description: The information describing an instance of a container. A container is a prepackaged, portable system image that runs isolated on an existing system using a container runtime like containerd.

namespace_pid.caption: Namespace PID
namespace_pid.description: If running under a process namespace (such as in a container), the process identifier within that process namespace.

ancestry.caption: Ancestry
ancestry.description: An array of Process Entities describing the extended parentage of this process object. Direct parent information should be expressed through the parent_process attribute. The first array element is the direct parent of this process object. Subsequent list elements go up the process parentage hierarchy. That is, the array is sorted from newest to oldest process. It is recommended to only populate this field for the top-level process object.

environment_variables.caption: Environment Variables
environment_variables.description: Environment variables associated with the process.

file.caption: File
file.description: The process file object.

integrity.caption: Integrity
integrity.description: The process integrity level, normalized to the caption of the integrity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source (Windows only).

integrity_id.caption: Integrity Level
integrity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the process integrity level (Windows only).

99.caption: Other
99.description: The integrity level is not mapped. See the integrity attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

lineage.caption: Lineage
lineage.description: The lineage of the process, represented by a list of paths for each ancestor process. For example: ['/usr/sbin/sshd', '/usr/bin/bash', '/usr/bin/whoami'].

loaded_modules.caption: Loaded Modules
loaded_modules.description: The list of loaded module names.

parent_process.caption: Parent Process
parent_process.description: The parent process of this process object. It is recommended to only populate this field for the top-level process object, to prevent deep nesting. Additional ancestry information can be supplied in the ancestry attribute.

ptid.caption: Process Thread ID
ptid.description: The identifier of the process thread associated with the event, as returned by the operating system.

sandbox.caption: Sandbox
sandbox.description: The name of the containment jail (i.e., sandbox). For example, hardened_ps, high_security_ps, oracle_ps, netsvcs_ps, or default_ps.

session.caption: Session
session.description: The user session under which this process is running.

terminated_time.caption: Terminated Time
terminated_time.description: The time when the process was terminated.

tid.caption: Thread ID
tid.description: The identifier of the thread associated with the event, as returned by the operating system.

@deprecated.message: tid is deprecated in favor of ptid. ptid has type long_t which can accommodate the thread identifiers returned by all platforms (e.g. 64-bit on MacOS).

user.caption: User
user.description: The user under which this process is running.

working_directory.caption: Working Directory
working_directory.description: The working directory of a process.

xattributes.caption: Extended Attributes
xattributes.description: An unordered collection of zero or more name/value pairs that represent a process extended attribute.

auid.caption: Audit User ID
auid.description: The audit user assigned at login by the audit subsystem.

egid.caption: Effective Group ID
egid.description: The effective group under which this process is running.

euid.caption: Effective User ID
euid.description: The effective user under which this process is running.

group.caption: Group
group.description: The group under which this process is running.

hosted_services.caption: Hosted Services
hosted_services.description: The Windows services that this process is hosting.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time when the process was created/started.

terminated_time_dt.caption: Terminated Time
terminated_time_dt.description: The time when the process was terminated.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the environment variable.

value.caption: Value
value.description: The value of the environment variable.

san.caption: Subject Alternative Name
san.description: The Subject Alternative name (SAN) object describes a SAN secured by a digital certificate
san.name: san

name.caption: Name
name.description: Name of SAN (e.g. The actual IP Address or domain.)

type.caption: Type
type.description: Type descriptor of SAN (e.g. IP Address/domain/etc.)

group.caption: Group
group.description: The Group object represents a collection or association of entities, such as users, policies, or devices. It serves as a logical grouping mechanism to organize and manage entities with similar characteristics or permissions within a system or organization, including but not limited to purposes of access control.
group.name: group

name.caption: Name
name.description: The group name.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the group. For example, for Windows events this is the security identifier (SID) of the group. Another example, pool id or desktop id that the device belongs to.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The group description.

domain.caption: Domain
domain.description: The domain where the group is defined. For example: the LDAP or Active Directory domain.

type.caption: Group Type
type.description: The type of the group.

long_string.caption: Long String
long_string.description: This object is a used to capture strings which may be truncated by a security product due to their length.
long_string.name: long_string

is_truncated.caption: Is Truncated
is_truncated.description: Indicates that value has been truncated. May be omitted if truncation has not occurred.

untruncated_size.caption: Untruncated Size
untruncated_size.description: The size in bytes of the string represented by value before truncation. Should be omitted if truncation has not occurred.

value.caption: Value
value.description: The string value, truncated if is_truncated is true.

anomaly_analysis.caption: Anomaly Analysis
anomaly_analysis.description: Describes the analysis of activity patterns and anomalies of target entities to identify potential security threats, performance issues, or other deviations from established baselines. This includes monitoring and analyzing user interactions, API usage, resource utilization, access patterns and other measured indicators.
anomaly_analysis.name: anomaly_analysis

analysis_targets.caption: Analysis Targets
analysis_targets.description: The analysis targets define the scope of monitored activities, specifying what entities, systems or processes are analyzed for activity patterns.

anomalies.caption: Anomalies
anomalies.description: List of detected activities that significantly deviate from the established baselines. This can include unusual access patterns, unexpected user-agents, abnormal API usage, suspicious traffic spikes, unauthorized access attempts, and other activities that may indicate potential security threats or system issues.

baselines.caption: Baselines
baselines.description: List of established patterns representing normal activity that serve as reference points for anomaly detection. This includes typical user interaction patterns like common user-agents, expected API access frequencies and patterns, standard resource utilization levels, and regular traffic flows. These baselines help establish what constitutes 'normal' activity in the system.

response.caption: Response Elements
response.description: The Response Elements object describes characteristics of an API response.
response.name: response

code.caption: Response Code
code.description: The numeric response sent to a request.

containers.caption: Containers
containers.description: When working with containerized applications, the set of containers which write to the standard the output of a particular logging driver. For example, this may be the set of containers involved in handling api requests and responses for a containerized application.

data.caption: Data
data.description: The additional data that is associated with the api response.

flags.caption: Flags
flags.description: The communication flags that are associated with the api response.

message.caption: Message
message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.

product.caption: Product
product.description: The Product object describes characteristics of a software product.
product.name: product

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the product.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the product.

data_classification.caption: Data Classification
data_classification.description: The Data Classification object includes information about data classification levels and data category types.

data_classifications.caption: Data Classification
data_classifications.description: A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.

cpe_name.caption: The product CPE identifier
cpe_name.description: The Common Platform Enumeration (CPE) name as described by (NIST) For example: cpe:/a:apple:safari:16.2.

feature.caption: Feature
feature.description: The feature that reported the event.

lang.caption: Language
lang.description: The two letter lower case language codes, as defined by ISO 639-1. For example: en (English), de (German), or fr (French).

path.caption: Path
path.description: The installation path of the product.

url_string.caption: URL String
url_string.description: The URL pointing towards the product.

vendor_name.caption: Vendor Name
vendor_name.description: The name of the vendor of the product.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The version of the product, as defined by the event source. For example: 2013.1.3-beta.

identity_activity_metrics.caption: Identity Activity Metrics
identity_activity_metrics.description: The Identity Activity Metrics object captures usage patterns, authentication activity, credential usage and other metrics for identities across cloud and on-premises environments. Example identities include AWS IAM Users, Roles, Azure AD Principals, GCP Service Accounts, on-premises Active Directory accounts.
identity_activity_metrics.name: identity_activity_metrics

first_seen_time.caption: First Seen
first_seen_time.description: The timestamp when this identity was first observed or created in the system. This helps establish the identity's age and lifecycle stage for risk assessment.

last_authentication_time.caption: Last Authentication Time
last_authentication_time.description: The timestamp when this identity last successfully authenticated to any system or service. This differs from last_seen_time as it specifically tracks authentication events rather than all activities.

last_seen_time.caption: Last Seen
last_seen_time.description: The timestamp of the most recent activity performed by this identity, including authentication, resource access, or API calls. This is the most comprehensive indicator of identity usage recency.

password_last_used_time.caption: Password Last Used Time
password_last_used_time.description: The timestamp when password-based authentication was last used by this identity. This helps distinguish between password and other authentication methods (MFA, SSO, certificates) and identify password-specific usage patterns.

programmatic_credentials.caption: Programmatic Credentials
programmatic_credentials.description: Details about the programmatic credentials associated with this identity, such as API keys, service account keys, access tokens, and client certificates used for automated access.

first_seen_time_dt.caption: First Seen
first_seen_time_dt.description: The timestamp when this identity was first observed or created in the system. This helps establish the identity's age and lifecycle stage for risk assessment.

last_authentication_time_dt.caption: Last Authentication Time
last_authentication_time_dt.description: The timestamp when this identity last successfully authenticated to any system or service. This differs from last_seen_time as it specifically tracks authentication events rather than all activities.

last_seen_time_dt.caption: Last Seen
last_seen_time_dt.description: The timestamp of the most recent activity performed by this identity, including authentication, resource access, or API calls. This is the most comprehensive indicator of identity usage recency.

password_last_used_time_dt.caption: Password Last Used Time
password_last_used_time_dt.description: The timestamp when password-based authentication was last used by this identity. This helps distinguish between password and other authentication methods (MFA, SSO, certificates) and identify password-specific usage patterns.

kb_article.caption: KB Article
kb_article.description: The KB Article object contains metadata that describes the patch or update.
kb_article.name: kb_article

classification.caption: Classification
classification.description: The vendors classification of the kb article.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The date the kb article was released by the vendor.

install_state.caption: Install State
install_state.description: The install state of the kb article.

install_state_id.caption: Install State ID
install_state_id.description: The normalized install state ID of the kb article.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The normalized install state is unknown.

1.caption: Installed
1.description: The item is installed.

2.caption: Not Installed
2.description: The item is not installed.

3.caption: Installed Pending Reboot
3.description: The item is installed pending reboot operation.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The install state is not mapped. See the install_state attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

is_superseded.caption: The patch is superseded.
is_superseded.description: The kb article has been replaced by another.

os.caption: OS
os.description: The operating system the kb article applies.

product.caption: Product
product.description: The product details the kb article applies.

severity.caption: Severity
severity.description: The severity of the kb article.

size.caption: Size
size.description: The size in bytes for the kb article.

src_url.caption: Source URL
src_url.description: The kb article link from the source vendor.

title.caption: Title
title.description: The title of the kb article.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier for the kb article.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The date the kb article was released by the vendor.

enrichment.caption: Enrichment
enrichment.description: The Enrichment object provides inline enrichment data for specific attributes of interest within an event. It serves as a mechanism to enhance or supplement the information associated with the event by adding additional relevant details or context.
enrichment.name: enrichment

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time when the enrichment data was generated.

data.caption: Data
data.description: The enrichment data associated with the attribute and value. The meaning of this data depends on the type the enrichment record.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: A long description of the enrichment data.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the attribute to which the enriched data pertains.

reputation.caption: Reputation Scores
reputation.description: The reputation of the enrichment data.

short_desc.caption: Short Description
short_desc.description: A short description of the enrichment data.

src_url.caption: Source URL
src_url.description: The URL of the source of the enrichment data.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The enrichment type. For example: location.

value.caption: Value
value.description: The value of the attribute to which the enriched data pertains.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: The time when the enrichment data was generated.

application.caption: Application
application.description: An Application describes the details for an inventoried application as reported by an Application Security tool or other Developer-centric tooling. Applications can be defined as Kubernetes resources, Containerized resources, or application hosting-specific cloud sources such as AWS Elastic BeanStalk, AWS Lightsail, or Azure Logic Apps.
application.name: application

criticality.caption: Business Criticality
criticality.description: The criticality of the application as defined by the event source.

desc.caption: Application Description
desc.description: A description or commentary for an application, usually retrieved from an upstream system.

group.caption: Group
group.description: The name of the related application or associated resource group.

hostname.caption: Hostname
hostname.description: The fully qualified name of the application.

labels.caption: Labels
labels.description: The list of labels associated to the application.

name.caption: Application Name
name.description: The name of the application.

owner.caption: Owner
owner.description: The identity of the service or user account that owns the application.

region.caption: Region
region.description: The cloud region of the resource.

resource_relationship.caption: Application Relationship
resource_relationship.description: A graph representation showing how this application relates to and interacts with other entities in the environment. This can include parent/child relationships, dependencies, or other connections.

risk_level.caption: Risk Level
risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The risk level is not mapped. See the risk_level attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

risk_score.caption: Risk Score
risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.

sbom.caption: Related SBOM
sbom.description: The Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) associated with the application

tags.caption: Tags
tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the application.

type.caption: Application Type
type.description: The type of application as defined by the event source, e.g., GitHub, Azure Logic App, or Amazon Elastic BeanStalk.

uid.caption: Application ID
uid.description: The unique identifier for the application.

uid_alt.caption: Application Alternative ID
uid_alt.description: An alternative or contextual identifier for the application, such as a configuration, organization, or license UID.

version.caption: Application Version
version.description: The semantic version of the application, e.g., 1.7.4.

related_event.caption: Related Event/Finding
related_event.description: The Related Event object describes an event or another finding related to a finding. It may or may not be an OCSF event.
related_event.name: related_event

attacks.caption: MITRE ATT&CK® and ATLAS™ Details
attacks.description: An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques & sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics, techniques & sub-techniques.

count.caption: Count
count.description: The number of times that activity in the same logical group occurred, as reported by the related Finding.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description:
The time when the related event/finding was created.

If the related event/finding is in OCSF and is a Finding, then this value should be equal to finding_info.created_time in the corresponding Finding. If the related event/finding is in OCSF and is not a Finding, then this value should be equal to time in the corresponding event.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: A description of the related event/finding.

first_seen_time.caption: First Seen
first_seen_time.description:
The time when the finding was first observed. e.g. The time when a vulnerability was first observed.
It can differ from the created_time timestamp, which reflects the time this finding was created.

kill_chain.caption: Kill Chain
kill_chain.description: The Cyber Kill Chain® provides a detailed description of each phase and its associated activities within the broader context of a cyber attack.

last_seen_time.caption: Last Seen
last_seen_time.description:
The time when the finding was most recently observed. e.g. The time when a vulnerability was most recently observed.
It can differ from the modified_time timestamp, which reflects the time this finding was last modified.

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The time when the related event/finding was last modified.

observables.caption: Observables
observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.

product.caption: Product
product.description: Details about the product that reported the related event/finding.

product_uid.caption: Product Identifier
product_uid.description: The unique identifier of the product that reported the related event.

@deprecated.message: Use the uid attribute in the product object instead. See specific usage.

severity.caption: Severity
severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.

severity_id.caption: Severity ID
severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.

1.caption: Informational
1.description: Informational message. No action required.

2.caption: Low
2.description: The user decides if action is needed.

3.caption: Medium
3.description: Action is required but the situation is not serious at this time.

4.caption: High
4.description: Action is required immediately.

5.caption: Critical
5.description: Action is required immediately and the scope is broad.

6.caption: Fatal
6.description: An error occurred but it is too late to take remedial action.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The event/finding severity is not mapped. See the severity attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

status.caption: Status
status.description: The related event status. Should correspond to the label of the status_id (or 'Other' status value for status_id = 99) of the related event.

tags.caption: Tags
tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated with the related event/finding.

title.caption: Title
title.description: A title or a brief phrase summarizing the related event/finding.

traits.caption: Traits
traits.description: The list of key traits or characteristics extracted from the related event/finding that influenced or contributed to the overall finding's outcome.

type.caption: Type
type.description:
The type of the related event/finding.

Populate if the related event/finding is NOT in OCSF. If it is in OCSF, then utilize type_name, type_uid instead.

type_name.caption: Type Name
type_name.description:
The type of the related OCSF event, as defined by type_uid.

For example: Process Activity: Launch.

Populate if the related event/finding is in OCSF.

type_uid.caption: Type ID
type_uid.description:
The unique identifier of the related OCSF event type.

For example: 100701.

Populate if the related event/finding is in OCSF.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description:
The unique identifier of the related event/finding.

If the related event/finding is in OCSF, then this value must be equal to metadata.uid in the corresponding event.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description:
The time when the related event/finding was created.

If the related event/finding is in OCSF and is a Finding, then this value should be equal to finding_info.created_time in the corresponding Finding. If the related event/finding is in OCSF and is not a Finding, then this value should be equal to time in the corresponding event.

first_seen_time_dt.caption: First Seen
first_seen_time_dt.description:
The time when the finding was first observed. e.g. The time when a vulnerability was first observed.
It can differ from the created_time timestamp, which reflects the time this finding was created.

last_seen_time_dt.caption: Last Seen
last_seen_time_dt.description:
The time when the finding was most recently observed. e.g. The time when a vulnerability was most recently observed.
It can differ from the modified_time timestamp, which reflects the time this finding was last modified.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The time when the related event/finding was last modified.

scan.caption: Scan
scan.description: The Scan object describes characteristics of a proactive scan.
scan.name: scan

name.caption: Name
name.description: The administrator-supplied or application-generated name of the scan. For example: "Home office weekly user database scan", "Scan folders for viruses", "Full system virus scan"

uid.caption: Scan UID
uid.description: The application-defined unique identifier assigned to an instance of a scan.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of scan.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The type id of the scan.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The scan type id is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Manual
1.description: The scan was manually initiated by the user or administrator.

2.caption: Scheduled
2.description: The scan was started based on scheduler.

3.caption: Updated Content
3.description: The scan was triggered by a content update.

4.caption: Quarantined Items
4.description: The scan was triggered by newly quarantined items.

5.caption: Attached Media
5.description: The scan was triggered by the attachment of removable media.

6.caption: User Logon
6.description: The scan was started due to a user logon.

7.caption: ELAM
7.description: The scan was triggered by an Early Launch Anti-Malware (ELAM) detection.

cis_benchmark.caption: CIS Benchmark
cis_benchmark.description: The CIS Benchmark object describes best practices for securely configuring IT systems, software, networks, and cloud infrastructure as defined by the Center for Internet Security. See also Getting to Know the CIS Benchmarks.
cis_benchmark.name: cis_benchmark

cis_controls.caption: CIS Controls
cis_controls.description: The CIS Critical Security Controls is a prioritized set of actions to protect your organization and data from cyber-attack vectors.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The CIS Benchmark description. For example: The cramfs filesystem type is a compressed read-only Linux filesystem embedded in small footprint systems. A cramfs image can be used without having to first decompress the image.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The CIS Benchmark name. For example: Ensure mounting of cramfs filesystems is disabled.

@deprecated.message: Use the Compliance object with Checks object instead.

keyboard_info.caption: Keyboard Information
keyboard_info.description: The Keyboard Information object contains details and attributes related to a computer or device keyboard. It encompasses information that describes the characteristics, capabilities, and configuration of the keyboard.
keyboard_info.name: keyboard_info

function_keys.caption: Function Keys
function_keys.description: The number of function keys on client keyboard.

ime.caption: IME
ime.description: The Input Method Editor (IME) file name.

keyboard_layout.caption: Keyboard Layout
keyboard_layout.description: The keyboard locale identifier name (e.g., en-US).

domain_contact.caption: Domain Contact
domain_contact.description: The contact information related to a domain registration, e.g., registrant, administrator, abuse, billing, or technical contact.
domain_contact.name: domain_contact

location.caption: Contact Location Information
location.description: Location details for the contract such as the city, state/province, country, etc.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The individual or organization name for the contact.

phone_number.caption: Phone Number
phone_number.description: The number associated with the phone.

type.caption: Domain Contact Type
type.description: The Domain Contact type, normalized to the caption of the type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source

type_id.caption: Domain Contact Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized domain contact type ID.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Registrant
1.description: The contact information provided is for the domain registrant.

2.caption: Administrative
2.description: The contact information provided is for the domain administrator.

3.caption: Technical
3.description: The contact information provided is for the domain technical lead.

4.caption: Billing
4.description: The contact information provided is for the domain billing lead.

5.caption: Abuse
5.description: The contact information provided is for the domain abuse contact.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the contact information, typically provided in WHOIS information.

compliance.caption: Compliance
compliance.description: The Compliance object contains information about Industry and Regulatory Framework standards, controls and requirements or details about custom assessments utilized in a compliance evaluation. Standards define broad security frameworks, controls represent specific security requirements within those frameworks, and checks are the testable verification points used to determine if controls are properly implemented.
compliance.name: compliance

assessments.caption: Assessments
assessments.description: A list of assessments associated with the compliance requirements evaluation.

category.caption: Category
category.description: The category a control framework pertains to, as reported by the source tool, such as Asset Management or Risk Assessment.

checks.caption: Compliance Checks
checks.description: A list of compliance checks associated with specific industry standards or frameworks. Each check represents an individual rule or requirement that has been evaluated against a target device. Checks typically include details such as the check name (e.g., CIS: 'Ensure mounting of cramfs filesystems is disabled' or DISA STIG descriptive titles), unique identifiers (such as CIS identifier '1.1.1.1' or DISA STIG identifier 'V-230234'), descriptions (detailed explanations of security requirements or vulnerability discussions), and version information.

compliance_references.caption: Compliance Standard References
compliance_references.description: A list of reference KB articles that provide information to help organizations understand, interpret, and implement compliance standards. They provide guidance, best practices, and examples.

@deprecated.message: Use the Compliance object with Check array instead.

compliance_standards.caption: Compliance Standards: Details
compliance_standards.description: A list of established guidelines or criteria that define specific requirements an organization must follow.

@deprecated.message: Use the Compliance object with Check array instead.

control.caption: Security Control
control.description: A Control is a prescriptive, actionable set of specifications that strengthens device posture. The control specifies required security measures, while the specific implementation values are defined in control_parameters. E.g., CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark 1.2.0 - Control 2.1 - Ensure CloudTrail is enabled in all regions

control_parameters.caption: Control Parameters
control_parameters.description: The list of control parameters evaluated in a Compliance check. E.g., parameters for CloudTrail configuration might include multiRegionTrailEnabled: true, logFileValidationEnabled: true, and requiredRegions: [us-east-1, us-west-2]

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The description or criteria of a control.

requirements.caption: Compliance Requirements
requirements.description: The specific compliance requirements being evaluated. E.g., PCI DSS Requirement 8.2.3 - Passwords must meet minimum complexity requirements or HIPAA Security Rule 164.312(a)(2)(iv) - Implement encryption and decryption mechanisms

standards.caption: Compliance Standards: List
standards.description: The regulatory or industry standards being evaluated for compliance.

status.caption: Status
status.description: The resultant status of the compliance check normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

status_code.caption: Status Code
status_code.description: The resultant status code of the compliance check.

status_detail.caption: Status Detail
status_detail.description: The contextual description of the status, status_code values.

status_details.caption: Status Details
status_details.description: A list of contextual descriptions of the status, status_code values.

status_id.caption: Status ID
status_id.description: The normalized status identifier of the compliance check.

1.caption: Pass
1.description: The compliance check passed for all the evaluated resources.

2.caption: Warning
2.description: The compliance check did not yield a result due to missing information.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The status is not mapped. See the status attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

3.caption: Fail
3.description: The compliance check failed for at least one of the evaluated resources.

sub_technique.caption: MITRE Sub-technique
sub_technique.description: The MITRE Sub-technique object describes the ATT&CK® or ATLAS™ Sub-technique ID and/or name associated to an attack.
sub_technique.name: sub_technique

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the attack sub-technique. For example: Scanning IP Blocks or User Execution: Unsafe ML Artifacts.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the attack sub-technique. For example: T1595.001 or AML.T0011.000.

src_url.caption: Source URL
src_url.description: The versioned permalink of the attack sub-technique. For example: https://attack.mitre.org/versions/v14/techniques/T1595/001/.

node.caption: Node
node.description: Represents a node or a vertex in a graph structure.
node.name: node

data.caption: Data
data.description: Additional data about the node stored as key-value pairs. Can include custom properties specific to the node.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: A human-readable description of the node's purpose or meaning in the graph.

name.caption: Name
name.description: A human-readable name or label for the node. Should be descriptive and unique within the graph context.

type.caption: Type
type.description: Categorizes the node into a specific class or type. Useful for grouping and filtering nodes.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: A unique string or numeric identifier that distinguishes this node from all others in the graph. Must be unique across all nodes.

scim.caption: SCIM
scim.description: The System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) Configuration object provides a structured set of attributes related to SCIM protocols used for identity provisioning and management across cloud-based platforms. It standardizes user and group provisioning details, enabling identity synchronization and lifecycle management with compatible Identity Providers (IdPs) and applications. SCIM is defined in RFC-7634
scim.name: scim

auth_protocol.caption: Auth Protocol
auth_protocol.description: The authorization protocol as defined by the caption of auth_protocol_id. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

auth_protocol_id.caption: Auth Protocol ID
auth_protocol_id.description: The normalized identifier of the authorization protocol used by the SCIM resource.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The authentication protocol is not mapped. See the auth_protocol attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: When the SCIM resource was added to the service provider.

error_message.caption: Last Error Message
error_message.description: Message or code associated with the last encountered error.

is_group_provisioning_enabled.caption: SCIM Group Provisioning Enabled
is_group_provisioning_enabled.description: Indicates whether the SCIM resource is configured to provision groups, automatically or otherwise.

is_user_provisioning_enabled.caption: SCIM User Provisioning Enabled
is_user_provisioning_enabled.description: Indicates whether the SCIM resource is configured to provision users, automatically or otherwise.

last_run_time.caption: Last Sync Time
last_run_time.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful synchronization.

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The most recent time when the SCIM resource was updated at the service provider.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the SCIM resource.

protocol_name.caption: Supported Protocol
protocol_name.description: The supported protocol for the SCIM resource. E.g., SAML, OIDC, or OAuth2.

rate_limit.caption: Rate Limit
rate_limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed by the SCIM resource within a specified time frame to avoid throttling.

scim_group_schema.caption: SCIM Group Schema
scim_group_schema.description: SCIM provides a schema for representing groups, identified using the following schema URI: urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group as defined in RFC-7634. This attribute will capture key-value pairs for the scheme implemented in a SCIM resource.

references.description: System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) RFC spec.

scim_user_schema.caption: SCIM User Schema
scim_user_schema.description: SCIM provides a resource type for user resources. The core schema for user is identified using the following schema URI: urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User as defined in RFC-7634. his attribute will capture key-value pairs for the scheme implemented in a SCIM resource. This object is inclusive of both the basic and Enterprise User Schema Extension.

references.description: System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) RFC spec.

state.caption: State
state.description: The provisioning state of the SCIM resource, normalized to the caption of the state_id value. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

state_id.caption: State ID
state_id.description: The normalized state ID of the SCIM resource to reflect its activation status.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The provisioning state of the SCIM resource is unknown.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The provisioning state of the SCIM resource is not mapped. See the state attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Pending
1.description: The SCIM resource is Pending activation or creation.

2.caption: Active
2.description: The SCIM resource is in an Active state, or otherwise enabled.

3.caption: Failed
3.description: The SCIM resource is in a Failed state.

4.caption: Deleted
4.description: The SCIM resource is in a Deleted state, or otherwise disabled.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: A unique identifier for a SCIM resource as defined by the service provider.

uid_alt.caption: External ID
uid_alt.description: A String that is an identifier for the resource as defined by the provisioning client. The externalId may simplify identification of a resource between the provisioning client and the service provider by allowing the client to use a filter to locate the resource with an identifier from the provisioning domain, obviating the need to store a local mapping between the provisioning domain's identifier of the resource and the identifier used by the service provider.

url_string.caption: SCIM Endpoint URL
url_string.description: The primary URL for SCIM API requests.

vendor_name.caption: Service Provider
vendor_name.description: Name of the vendor or service provider implementing SCIM. E.g., Okta, Auth0, Microsoft.

version.caption: SCIM Version
version.description: SCIM protocol version supported e.g., SCIM 2.0.

created_time_dt.caption: Created Time
created_time_dt.description: When the SCIM resource was added to the service provider.

last_run_time_dt.caption: Last Sync Time
last_run_time_dt.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful synchronization.

modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time
modified_time_dt.description: The most recent time when the SCIM resource was updated at the service provider.

image.caption: Image
image.description: The Image object provides a description of a specific Virtual Machine (VM) or Container image.
image.name: image

name.caption: Name
name.description: The image name. For example: elixir.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique image ID. For example: 77af4d6b9913.

labels.caption: Labels
labels.description: The list of labels associated to the image.

path.caption: Path
path.description: The full path to the image file.

@deprecated.message: Use the labels or tags attribute instead.

tags.caption: Tags
tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the image.

extension.caption: Schema Extension
extension.description: The OCSF Schema Extension object provides detailed information about the schema extension used to construct the event. The schema extensions are registered in the extensions.md file.
extension.name: extension

name.caption: Name
name.description: The schema extension name. For example: dev.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The schema extension unique identifier. For example: 999.

version.caption: Version
version.description: The schema extension version. For example: 1.0.0-alpha.2.

dns_answer.caption: DNS Answer
dns_answer.description: The DNS Answer object represents a specific response provided by the Domain Name System (DNS) when querying for information about a domain or performing a DNS operation. It encapsulates the relevant details and data returned by the DNS server in response to a query.
dns_answer.name: dns_answer

class.caption: Resource Record Class
class.description: The class of DNS data contained in this resource record. See RFC1035. For example: IN.

packet_uid.caption: Packet UID
packet_uid.description: The DNS packet identifier assigned by the program that generated the query. The identifier is copied to the response.

type.caption: Resource Record Type
type.description: The type of data contained in this resource record. See RFC1035. For example: CNAME.

flag_ids.caption: DNS Header Flags
flag_ids.description: The list of DNS answer header flag IDs.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The flag is not mapped. See the flags attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

flags.caption: DNS Header Flags
flags.description: The list of DNS answer header flags.

rdata.caption: DNS RData
rdata.description: The data describing the DNS resource. The meaning of this data depends on the type and class of the resource record.

ttl.caption: TTL
ttl.description: The time interval that the resource record may be cached. Zero value means that the resource record can only be used for the transaction in progress, and should not be cached.

idp.caption: Identity Provider
idp.description: The Identity Provider object contains detailed information about a provider responsible for creating, maintaining, and managing identity information while offering authentication services to applications. An Identity Provider (IdP) serves as a trusted authority that verifies the identity of users and issues authentication tokens or assertions to enable secure access to applications or services.
idp.name: idp

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the Identity Provider.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the Identity Provider.

auth_factors.caption: Authentication Factors
auth_factors.description: The Authentication Factors object describes the different types of Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) methods and/or devices supported by the Identity Provider.

domain.caption: Domain
domain.description: The primary domain associated with the Identity Provider.

fingerprint.caption: Certificate Fingerprint
fingerprint.description: The fingerprint of the X.509 certificate used by the Identity Provider.

has_mfa.caption: MFA Enforced
has_mfa.description: The Identity Provider enforces Multi Factor Authentication (MFA).

issuer.caption: Issuer Details
issuer.description: The unique identifier (often a URL) used by the Identity Provider as its issuer.

protocol_name.caption: Supported Protocol
protocol_name.description: The supported protocol of the Identity Provider. E.g., SAML, OIDC, or OAuth2.

scim.caption: SCIM
scim.description: The System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) resource object provides a structured set of attributes related to SCIM protocols used for identity provisioning and management across cloud-based platforms. It standardizes user and group provisioning details, enabling identity synchronization and lifecycle management with compatible Identity Providers (IdPs) and applications. SCIM is defined in RFC-7634

references.description: System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) RFC.

sso.caption: SSO
sso.description: The Single Sign-On (SSO) object provides a structure for normalizing SSO attributes, configuration, and/or settings from Identity Providers.

state.caption: State
state.description: The configuration state of the Identity Provider, normalized to the caption of the state_id value. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

state_id.caption: State ID
state_id.description: The normalized state ID of the Identity Provider to reflect its configuration or activation status.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The configuration state of the Identity Provider is unknown.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The configuration state of the Identity Provider is not mapped. See the state attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Active
1.description: The Identity Provider is in an Active state, or otherwise enabled.

2.caption: Suspended
2.description: The Identity Provider is in a Suspended state.

3.caption: Deprecated
3.description: The Identity Provider is in a Deprecated state, or is otherwise disabled.

4.caption: Deleted
4.description: The Identity Provider is in a Deleted state.

tenant_uid.caption: Tenant UID
tenant_uid.description: The tenant ID associated with the Identity Provider.

url_string.caption: Configuration URL
url_string.description: The URL for accessing the configuration or metadata of the Identity Provider.

network_endpoint.caption: Network Endpoint
network_endpoint.description: The Network Endpoint object describes characteristics of a network endpoint. These can be a source or destination of a network connection.
network_endpoint.name: network_endpoint

name.caption: Name
name.description: The short name of the endpoint.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the endpoint.

container.caption: Container
container.description: The information describing an instance of a container. A container is a prepackaged, portable system image that runs isolated on an existing system using a container runtime like containerd.

namespace_pid.caption: Namespace PID
namespace_pid.description: If running under a process namespace (such as in a container), the process identifier within that process namespace.

agent_list.caption: Agent List
agent_list.description: A list of agent objects associated with a device, endpoint, or resource.

domain.caption: Domain
domain.description: The name of the domain that the endpoint belongs to or that corresponds to the endpoint.

hostname.caption: Hostname
hostname.description: The fully qualified name of the endpoint.

instance_uid.caption: Instance ID
instance_uid.description: The unique identifier of a VM instance.

interface_name.caption: Network Interface Name
interface_name.description: The name of the network interface (e.g. eth2).

interface_uid.caption: Network Interface ID
interface_uid.description: The unique identifier of the network interface.

ip.caption: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address of the endpoint, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.

location.caption: Geo Location
location.description: The geographical location of the endpoint.

mac.caption: MAC Address
mac.description: The Media Access Control (MAC) address of the endpoint.

mac_vendor.caption: MAC Vendor
mac_vendor.description: The vendor or manufacturer of the endpoint's network interface controller (NIC), as identified from the MAC address.

owner.caption: Owner
owner.description: The identity of the service or user account that owns the endpoint or was last logged into it.

pool.caption: Pool
pool.description: The pool of desktops or virtual machines to which the endpoint belongs.

subnet_uid.caption: Subnet UID
subnet_uid.description: The unique identifier of a virtual subnet.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The network endpoint type. For example: unknown, server, desktop, laptop, tablet, mobile, virtual, browser, or other.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The network endpoint type ID.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Server
1.description: A server.

2.caption: Desktop
2.description: A desktop computer.

3.caption: Laptop
3.description: A laptop computer.

4.caption: Tablet
4.description: A tablet computer.

5.caption: Mobile
5.description: A mobile phone.

6.caption: Virtual
6.description: A virtual machine.

7.caption: IOT
7.description: An IOT (Internet of Things) device.

8.caption: Browser
8.description: A web browser.

9.caption: Firewall
9.description: A networking firewall.

10.caption: Switch
10.description: A networking switch.

11.caption: Hub
11.description: A networking hub.

12.caption: Router
12.description: A networking router.

13.caption: IDS
13.description: An intrusion detection system.

14.caption: IPS
14.description: An intrusion prevention system.

15.caption: Load Balancer
15.description: A Load Balancer device.

vpc_uid.caption: VPC UID
vpc_uid.description: The unique identifier of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).

zone.caption: Network Zone
zone.description: The network zone or LAN segment.

autonomous_system.caption: Autonomous System
autonomous_system.description: The Autonomous System details associated with an IP address.

fingerprints.caption: Fingerprints
fingerprints.description: Fingerprints that identify the specific application implementation on this endpoint, such as Cisco NPF or HASSH.

intermediate_ips.caption: Intermediate IP Addresses
intermediate_ips.description: The intermediate IP Addresses. For example, the IP addresses in the HTTP X-Forwarded-For header.

isp.caption: ISP Name
isp.description: The name of the Internet Service Provider (ISP).

isp_org.caption: ISP Org
isp_org.description: The organization name of the Internet Service Provider (ISP). This represents the parent organization or company that owns/operates the ISP. For example, Comcast Corporation would be the ISP org for Xfinity internet service. This attribute helps identify the ultimate provider when ISPs operate under different brand names.

network_scope.caption: Network Scope
network_scope.description: Indicates whether the endpoint resides inside the customer’s network, outside on the Internet, or if its location relative to the customer’s network cannot be determined. The value is normalized to the caption of the network_scope_id.

network_scope_id.caption: Network Scope ID
network_scope_id.description: The normalized identifier of the endpoint’s network scope. The normalized network scope identifier indicates whether the endpoint resides inside the customer’s network, outside on the Internet, or if its location relative to the customer’s network cannot be determined.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: Unknown whether this endpoint resides within the customer’s network.

1.caption: Internal
1.description: The endpoint resides inside the customer’s network.

2.caption: External
2.description: The endpoint is on the Internet or otherwise external to the customer’s network.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The network scope is not mapped. See the network_scope attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

port.caption: Port
port.description: The port used for communication within the network connection.

proxy_endpoint.caption: Proxy Endpoint
proxy_endpoint.description: The network proxy information pertaining to a specific endpoint. This can be used to describe information pertaining to network address translation (NAT).

svc_name.caption: Service Name
svc_name.description: The service name in service-to-service connections. For example, AWS VPC logs the pkt-src-aws-service and pkt-dst-aws-service fields identify the connection is coming from or going to an AWS service.

transformation_info.caption: Transformation Info
transformation_info.description: The transformation_info object represents the mapping or transformation used.
transformation_info.name: transformation_info

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the transformation or mapping.

uid.caption: Unique ID
uid.description: The unique identifier of the mapping or transformation.

lang.caption: Language
lang.description: The transformation language used to transform the data.

product.caption: Product
product.description: The product or instance used to make the transformation

time.caption: Event Time
time.description: Time of the transformation.

url_string.caption: URL String
url_string.description: The Uniform Resource Locator String where the mapping or transformation exists.

time_dt.caption: Event Time
time_dt.description: Time of the transformation.

access_analysis_result.caption: Access Analysis Result
access_analysis_result.description: The Access Analysis Result object describes access relationships and pathways between identities, resources, focusing on who can access what and through which mechanisms. This evaluates access levels (read/write/admin), access types (direct, cross-account, public, federated), and the conditions under which access is granted. Use this for resource-centric security assessments such as external access discovery, public exposure analysis, etc.
access_analysis_result.name: access_analysis_result

access_level.caption: Access Level
access_level.description: The generalized access level or permission scope granted to the identity through the analyzed policy configuration. Common examples include Read, Write, List, Delete, Admin, or custom permission levels.

access_type.caption: Access Type
access_type.description: The type or category of access being granted to the identity. This describes the nature of the access relationship, such as cross-account access, public access, federated access, or third-party integration access. Examples include 'Cross-Account', 'Public', 'Federated', 'Service-to-Service', etc.

accessors.caption: Accessors
accessors.description: The identities that are granted access through the analyzed policy configuration. This identifies the specific entity that can exercise the permissions and helps assess the access relationship and potential security implications. Examples include user accounts, service principals, roles, account identifiers, or system identities.

additional_restrictions.caption: Additional Restrictions
additional_restrictions.description: Details about supplementary restrictions and guardrails that may limit the granted access, applied through additional policy types such as Resource Control Policies (RCPs) and Service Control Policies (SCPs) in AWS, or other policy constraints.

condition_keys.caption: Condition Keys
condition_keys.description: The condition keys and their values that constrain when and how the granted access can be exercised. These conditions define the circumstances under which the access relationship is valid and the privileges can be used. Examples: IP address restrictions like 'aws:SourceIp:192.0.2.0/24', time-based constraints like 'aws:RequestedRegion:us-east-1', MFA requirements like 'aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent:true', or custom conditions based on resource tags and request context.

granted_privileges.caption: Granted Privileges
granted_privileges.description: The specific privileges, actions, or permissions that are granted through the analyzed access relationship. This includes the actual operations that the accessor can perform on the target resource. Examples: AWS actions like 'sts:AssumeRole', 's3:GetObject', 'ec2:DescribeInstances'; Azure actions like 'Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/read'; or GCP permissions like 'storage.objects.get'.

privilege_info.caption: Privilege Info
privilege_info.description: The Privilege Info object describes information about a specific privilege, action, or permission. It captures the privilege name, type, usage status, and when it was last used.
privilege_info.name: privilege_info

is_unused.caption: Is Unused
is_unused.description: Indicates whether the privilege is unused within the analysis timeframe.

last_used_time.caption: Last Used Time
last_used_time.description: The most recent time this privilege was used.

name.caption: Name
name.description: The name of the privilege, action, or permission. Examples: GetObject, CreateStoreImageTask (AWS); Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/read (Azure); storage.objects.get (GCP).

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type or category of the privilege, normalized to the caption of the type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized type of the privilege.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The privilege type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Read
1.description: A read-type privilege that permits viewing or retrieving data. Examples: s3:GetObject, Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/read, storage.objects.get.

2.caption: Write
2.description: A write-type privilege that permits creating, modifying, or deleting data. Examples: s3:PutObject, Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/write, storage.objects.create.

3.caption: Execute
3.description: An execute-type privilege that permits running operations or invoking functions. Examples: lambda:InvokeFunction, Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/start/action.

last_used_time_dt.caption: Last Used Time
last_used_time_dt.description: The most recent time this privilege was used.

malware_scan_info.caption: Malware Scan Info
malware_scan_info.description: The malware scan information object describes characteristics, metadata of a malware scanning job.
malware_scan_info.name: malware_scan_info

name.caption: Name
name.description: The administrator-supplied or application-generated name of the scan. For example: "Home office weekly user database scan", "Scan folders for viruses", "Full system virus scan"

uid.caption: Scan UID
uid.description: The application-defined unique identifier assigned to an instance of a scan.

type.caption: Type
type.description: The type of scan.

type_id.caption: Type ID
type_id.description: The type id of the scan.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The scan type id is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

1.caption: Manual
1.description: The scan was manually initiated by the user or administrator.

2.caption: Scheduled
2.description: The scan was started based on scheduler.

3.caption: Updated Content
3.description: The scan was triggered by a content update.

4.caption: Quarantined Items
4.description: The scan was triggered by newly quarantined items.

5.caption: Attached Media
5.description: The scan was triggered by the attachment of removable media.

6.caption: User Logon
6.description: The scan was started due to a user logon.

7.caption: ELAM
7.description: The scan was triggered by an Early Launch Anti-Malware (ELAM) detection.

end_time.caption: End Time
end_time.description: The timestamp indicating when the scan job completed execution.

num_files.caption: Scanned Files
num_files.description: The total number of files analyzed during the scan.

num_infected.caption: Number of Infected Entities
num_infected.description: The total number of files identified as infected with malware during the scan.

num_volumes.caption: Number of Volumes
num_volumes.description: The total number of storage volumes examined during the malware scan.

size.caption: Size
size.description: The total size in bytes of all files that were scanned.

start_time.caption: Start Time
start_time.description: The timestamp indicating when the scan job began execution.

unique_malware_count.caption: Unique Malware Count
unique_malware_count.description: The number of unique malware detected across all infected files.

end_time_dt.caption: End Time
end_time_dt.description: The timestamp indicating when the scan job completed execution.

start_time_dt.caption: Start Time
start_time_dt.description: The timestamp indicating when the scan job began execution.

finding_info.caption: Finding Information
finding_info.description: The Finding Information object describes metadata related to a security finding generated by a security tool or system.
finding_info.name: finding_info

analytic.caption: Analytic
analytic.description: The analytic technique used to analyze and derive insights from the data or information that led to the finding or conclusion.

attack_graph.caption: Attack Graph
attack_graph.description: An Attack Graph describes possible routes an attacker could take through an environment. It describes relationships between resources and their findings, such as malware detections, vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and other security actions.

references.description: MS Defender description of Attack Path

attacks.caption: MITRE ATT&CK® and ATLAS™ Details
attacks.description: The MITRE ATT&CK® technique and associated tactics related to the finding.

created_time.caption: Created Time
created_time.description: The time when the finding was created.

data_sources.caption: Data Sources
data_sources.description: A list of data sources utilized in generation of the finding.

desc.caption: Description
desc.description: The description of the reported finding.

first_seen_time.caption: First Seen
first_seen_time.description:
The time when the finding was first observed. e.g. The time when a vulnerability was first observed.

It can differ from the created_time timestamp, which reflects the time this finding was created.

kill_chain.caption: Kill Chain
kill_chain.description: The Cyber Kill Chain® provides a detailed description of each phase and its associated activities within the broader context of a cyber attack.

last_seen_time.caption: Last Seen
last_seen_time.description:
The time when the finding was most recently observed. e.g. The time when a vulnerability was most recently observed.

It can differ from the modified_time timestamp, which reflects the time this finding was last modified.

modified_time.caption: Modified Time
modified_time.description: The time when the finding was last modified.

product.caption: Product
product.description: Details about the product that reported the finding.

product_uid.caption: Product Identifier
product_uid.description: The unique identifier of the product that reported the finding.

@deprecated.message: Use the uid attribute in the product object instead. See specific usage.

related_analytics.caption: Related Analytics
related_analytics.description: Other analytics related to this finding.

related_events.caption: Related Events/Findings
related_events.description: Describes events and/or other findings related to the finding as identified by the security product. Note that these events may or may not be in OCSF.

related_events_count.caption: Related Events/Findings Count
